summary: Allow Search to Index the Content of MD Files
description: "At the moment, Confluence Search will look at and index the content of some attached file types (Word, Text, PowerPoint, Excel, PDF, and HTML), allowing users to search for content within these files.    While these are the most popular types of attachments, more and more users are utilizing Markdown (MD) files within their Confluence instances and would benefit from being able to search those files as well."
text: Allow Search to Index the Content of MD Files [SEP] """At the moment, Confluence Search will look at and index the content of some attached file types (Word, Text, PowerPoint, Excel, PDF, and HTML), allowing users to search for content within these files.    While these are the most popular types of attachments, more and more users are utilizing Markdown (MD) files within their Confluence instances and would benefit from being able to search those files as well."""
label: design

summary: Confluence allows 'admin' username from external directories and the user can have access to sensitive data
description: "h3. Issue Summary  When you connect Confluence to an external directory(Jira, LDAP), it will not analyze or filter the usernames that are being retrieved.    During the installation, the administrator account must be created. The 'admin' username is often used.    This can bring exploit issues as someone can create the 'admin' account externally. Confluence also binds the permissions with the username, not with the user_key.    h3. Steps to Reproduce    # Install Confluence and create an 'admin' account.  # Connect Confluence with Jira/Jira SD.  # Set the permissions on the ""Global Permissions"" page.  # Enable user signup in Jira/Jira SD.  # Create an account with the 'admin' username.  # Go back to Confluence and synchronize the external directory.    h3. Expected Results  Confluence will block the synchronization of the 'admin' or 'administrator' username.    h3. Actual Results  Confluence synchronizes the user. Due to the permissions set with the username, the user may have full access to the application.    h3. Workaround    * If it is possible to disable user signup in Jira, disable it.  * Change the username of the members of the 'confluence-administrators' group."

Due to the permissions set with the username, the user may have full access to the application.    h3. Workaround    * If it is possible to disable user signup in Jira, disable it.  * Change the username of the members of the 'confluence-administrators' group."""

summary: Widget connector performs port 80 request from server for Youtube thumbnail
description: "h3. Issue Summary    When embedding a Youtube video into a Confluence page the widget connector performs an HTTP get request from the web server to Youtube’s image hosting endpoint over port 80. This is an issue if the server is forbidden from making outgoing requests on this port. It will fail to embed the video on the page if there is any such restriction on the server or within the network.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Install tcpdump on the server where Confluence is installed    # Run: tcpdump -i any -c5 -nn port 80   # Embed a Youtube video in a Confluence page using the Widget Connector macro    h3. Expected Results    There is no traffic from the server to Youtube over HTTP port 80 (e.g.) /vi/4ZyjcdffK4o/mqdefault.jpg    h3. Actual Results    The below output is produced by tcpdump:      h3. Workaround    Potentially you could insert a client side embed using the Confluence HTML macro and the Youtube embed code found on the share page."

label: non-design

summary: Provide support for multiple identity providers (IdPs) in native DC SAML connector
description: "h3. Problem Definition  The native SAML connector in Jira Data Center only allows for the configuration of a single IdP. This is limiting for certain use cases, especially where external users may be authenticating to an instance via a different domain.     h3. Suggested Solution  Provide support for configuration multiple IdPs using the native SAML connector.     h3. Workaround   There are several third-party DC SAML/SSO plugins on the Atlassian Marketplace that allow for the configuration of multiple IdPs."
text: Provide support for multiple identity providers (IdPs) in native DC SAML connector [SEP] """h3. This is limiting for certain use cases, especially where external users may be authenticating to an instance via a different domain.     h3. Suggested Solution  Provide support for configuration multiple IdPs using the native SAML connector.     h3. Workaround   There are several third-party DC SAML/SSO plugins on the Atlassian Marketplace that allow for the configuration of multiple IdPs."""

summary: Newly added users do not receive email when "Send an email to the user you have just created" is selected
description: "h3. Issue Summary    When an administrator adds a new user account to Confluence from ""User Management >> Add Users"", the newly created account is automatically added to *""confluence-users""* group.    If ""confluence-users"" group is not added to Global Permissions and granted ""can use"" permission, newly created users will not receive an email for setting up their passwords.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Setup a mail server in Confluence   # Remove *confluence-users* from *global permissions*   # Go to *User Management* >> *Add Users* >> enter user details and *email address*   # Make sure *""Send an email to the user you have just created...""* option is selected >> click *Add*   # Newly added user does not receive an email to setup their first password         _Note: This behaviour is only observed when *""confluence-users""* is not granted ""Can Use"" Global permission._  h3. Expected Results    Newly added users should receive an email to setup their first password   h3. Actual Results    Newly added users do not receive an email to setup their first password   h3. Workaround    Add *""confluence-users""* group to global permissions and grant it ""Can Use"""

Expected Results    Newly added users should receive an email to setup their first password   h3. Actual Results    Newly added users do not receive an email to setup their first password   h3. Workaround    Add *""""confluence-users""""* group to global permissions and grant it """"Can Use"""""""
label: non-design

summary: Documentation Promotion Update Capability
description: "Hello,         Evening! Currently I use Confluence as a means to document technical aspects and internal facing procedures. A feature that would be very useful and efficient a promote to Prod feature/function.         For Example - My company uses both a ""Staging/test"" workspace for documentation where many colleagues can collaborate on a page and review all documentation requirements/specs/info prior to us moving over to our official PROD documentation workspace. We currently have to copy and paste and it can be quite time consuming depending on what type of technical requirement/information is being displayed/updated. The function that would elevate confluence to the next level and honestly make this application much easier to use is function that allows you to link 2 pages together or workspaces that allows you to take one page (Must share the same name or a token to link them together) and then promote all updates from the lower documentation workspace (STAGING/TEST) to a production environment. This would overwrite the existing page in PROD with the new updates being promoted from the lower workspace environment. "

A feature that would be very useful and efficient a promote to Prod feature/function.         For Example - My company uses both a """"Staging/test"""" workspace for documentation where many colleagues can collaborate on a page and review all documentation requirements/specs/info prior to us moving over to our official PROD documentation workspace. We currently have to copy and paste and it can be quite time consuming depending on what type of technical requirement/information is being displayed/updated. The function that would elevate confluence to the next level and honestly make this application much easier to use is function that allows you to link 2 pages together or workspaces that allows you to take one page (Must share the same name or a token to link them together) and then promote all updates from the lower documentation workspace (STAGING/TEST) to a production environment. This would overwrite the existing page in PROD with the new updates being promoted from the lower workspace environment. """

summary: Scroll to other author's position
description: "When multiple editors are in confluence, it is difficult to find the area that others are editing. Clicking on the users icon should automatically scroll to that person's cursor position. Other online editing tools support this feature."

summary: Show friendly message instead of maxClauseCount error
description: "When a page action (such as the contributors macro) triggers the Lucene ""maxClauseCount"" error, instead of showing that exact error in the UI, show a user-friendly message such as ""Your query is too broad, please limit it"" or something like that, to let the user know _why_ the error is returned and how to possibly prevent it.       And retain the actual ""maxClauseCount"" error in the logs to help support troubleshoot such issues."

summary: Space directory alphabetical pagination
description: "Pagination of Space list is only via numerical pages (in the bottom of the page). From our experience and our users' feedback, it really difficult to find and filter the list of spaces - Since the numbered pagination view doesn't really allow you to know what you have in each page.  It would be more useful and meaningful if the pagination is using Alphabet instead of Numeric."

From our experience and our users' feedback, it really difficult to find and filter the list of spaces - Since the numbered pagination view doesn't really allow you to know what you have in each page.  It would be more useful and meaningful if the pagination is using Alphabet instead of Numeric."""

summary: Italicizing text on a page that is added as an 'includes' macro on another page causes unnecessary line breaks.
description: "h3. Issue Summary    Italicizing text on a page that is added as an 'includes' macro on another page causes unnecessary line breaks.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create 'Page A', add a snippet of text with italics for the middle word. e.g.   'A _product_ name'   # Create Page B, use an 'Includes' macro to add Page A in between other text. e.g.   'Text [Includes Page A] Text'   # Save and View Page B.    h3. Expected Results    Page B should show all the text (including the text from the 'Includes' macro from Page A) inline.  {quote}Text A _product_ name Text  {quote}  h3. Actual Results    Page B shows a line break for the italicized word.    !Screen Shot 2020-10-16 at 10.24.55 AM.png!  h3. Workaround    Two possible workarounds:   - If possible, use 'Excerpt' on Page A and 'Excerpt Include' on Page B. This appears to keep the formatting even with italicized text.   - Remove italicized text altogether on Page A _or_ italicize all the text (which does not add a line break)."

summary: Improve keyboard shortcut for Jira Issues Macro
description: "From the editor toolbar, choose Insert > Other Macros and choose Jira from the Development category.    We can observe the shortcut description at the very bottom of the window:   ""Select MacroHint: type ""Cmd+Shift+J"" in the editor to quickly access this dialog.""    *However:*   1. When using macOS, we should use ""Control""+ ""shift"" + ""j"".    2. When using macOS and Chrome, when we use ""cmd+shift+""j"", chrome opens the download page (CONFSERVER-27085);    3. Our Documentation does not mention this shortcut:   [Confluence help - Keyboard shortcuts|https://confluence.<USER>conf77/keyboard-shortcuts-1021226024.html] has no mention of this shortcut;   [Jira Issues Macro|https://confluence.<USER>doc/jira-issues-macro-139380.html] does not mention any keyboard shortcut;  [Display Jira Issues in Confluence: more power, more control|https://www.<USER>blog/confluence/display-jira-issues-in-confluence-more-power-more-control] - this blog is the only place mentioning the keyboard shortcut cmd/ctrl + Shift + ""j""       *We would like that:*   a) The message should be ""Select MacroHint: type ""*Cmd/Ctrl+Shift+J*"" in the editor to quickly access this dialog."" (so Users would know that they should try with Ctrl also);   b) The keyboard shortcut Cmd/Ctrl + ""j"" was added at the Keyboard help ( ""?"" in the editor toolbar);   c) Our documentation should explain that depends on the key/Operational System/Browser, we can use Command or Ctrl to activate"

summary: when a user with Unlicensed Access clicks on "View" under "Attachments Macro" they get "Not permitted"
description: "h3. Issue Summary    When a user with unlicensed access clicks on ""View"" button under ""Attachment macro"", they get ""not permitted"" message, however if they click on the name of the attachment itself, they are able to preview/download it.  h3. Steps to Reproduce    - Jira <USER>v4.13.0  - Confluence 7.8.0   # Link Confluence Space as Knowledge Base in JSD Project   # Create a user ""tester"" on Confluence side for the same JSD user (don't add the user to any groups in Confluence)   # Go to Jira project and search knowledge base for any article that got ""Attachments"" macro   # Expand the attachment options, and click on ""View"" , it results in ""Not Permitted""   # However clicking on the attachment name itself opens it without any issue    h3. Expected Results    When clicking on ""View"" button, the user with unlicensed access should be able to access the file  h3. Actual Results    When clicking on ""View"" button, the user with unlicensed access is unable to access the file  h3. Workaround    User with unlicensed access can access the attachment by clicking on the name of the attachment instead of ""View"" button"

summary: Gray-out the option 'maximum authentication attempts allowed' when 'CAPTCHA on login' is Disabled
description: "Hello Team,    The option on to enable '*CAPTCHA on Login*' in the Confluence Administration has a sub-option for '*_maximum authentication attempts allowed_*' post which the CAPTCHA will be presented. When the option '*CAPTCHA on Login*' is unchecked the option for maximum attempts allowed is inconsequential and is rather misleading and confusing.    I would request for the option to set '*maximum authentication attempts allowed*' to be grayed-out when the CAPTCHA on Login is disabled.    Kind regards,  Venkat"

summary: Email notification does not follow preferred language on browser settings
description: "h3. Issue Summary  By default, Confluence is using [the configured user's preferred language|https://confluence.<USER>doc/choosing-a-default-language-214860191.html] to be used as the email notification language.     Hence, should the user's preferred language is set to _Automatically detect browser settings_, Confluence should then be using the Browser language set.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Set [the configured user's preferred language|https://confluence.<USER>doc/choosing-a-default-language-214860191.html] for a user to _Automatically detect browser settings_.  # Set the browser's language to a particular language, e.g. Chinese.  # Attempt to send an email notification to the user, e.g. by mentioning the user on a Confluence page.    h3. Expected Results  Email notifications would be sent in Chinese.    h3. Actual Results  Email notifications would be sent in the configured [Confluence's general site default language|https://confluence.<USER>doc/choosing-a-default-language-214860191.html].    h3. Workaround  None at the moment."

summary: Importing a .docx file via 'Import Word Document' does not retain table heading row cell color
description: "h3. Issue Summary    Importing a .docx file via 'Import Word Document' with a table that has table heading row cell color (for instance dark blue) does not retain table heading row color when new page is created.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Log into Confluence.   # In any Space, click the three dots (...) options at the top right.   # Choose 'Import Word Document'   # Import a .docx file that has a table heading row with a color.   # Save    h3. Expected Results  The table heading row cell color is maintained on any new pages created.     !Screen Shot 2020-10-14 at 4.46.22 PM.png|thumbnail!     h3. Actual Results  The heading row for some or all tables will be turned to 'white' backgrounds.     !Screen Shot 2020-10-14 at 4.46.29 PM.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround  Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"

summary: Time to First Response Jira Gadget not displaying Save and Cancel button in Confluence in edit mode
description: "h3. Problem    It's not possible to update the parameters of Jira  Gadget ""Time to First Response"" in the Confluence page in edit mode.    When editing the existing JIRA gadget params, the scrollbar on the right does not allow to show the full frame and hence the ""Save/Close"" button can't be shown.  h3. Environment    Tested in .Confluence 7.3.4 and 6.15.10   h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Established Application link between Jira and Confluence.   # Create a new page in Confluence.    # Insert Time to First response macro in Confluence page > Fill up the parameters > Save > Insert > Publish. (Creating first time it works fine)   # Edit the Gadget i.e > Edit Page > Edit > Config Icon > Edit . (Refer attached Image gadget-display-edit-param )   # Save and Cancel button won't be display.  (attached image gadget-display-ko-chome)    h3. Expected Results    Save and Cancel button should be render as without  these button it is not possible to modify the parameter's value.    If we click on Save button which is present in the outside frame that doesn't take effect to submit the changes as old values would be display.  h3. Actual Results    Save and Cancel are not shown.   h3. Workaround   # _User can deleting the gadget and recreating it on the page with updated values._    # _This works in IE intermittently._    h3. Notes    In order to use ""Time to First Response"" Macro in Confluence Page, install Jira Charting plugin in Jira as ""Time to First Response"" gadget is submodule of it.      "

summary: As an admin I want to migrate a large Confluence space to another instance without size limitations
description: "h3. Problem Definition    A huge space migration can translate also into even greater pain - spaces can get so big that will cause attempts to do the XML export/import to crash.    For a huge space, admins have to devise their own migration tools or suffer from long and painful migration steps (long-running exports, for invalid characters in the files inside the backup zip file, long-running import, <USER>.  h3. Suggested Solution    Today the XML format is used for the main file (entities.xml - which can take an absurd side) and a few other files/directories and the attachments directory.    The suggestion is that this process can be refactored to bypass file sizing limitations by   - using other formats (non-XML), easier to parse and work with (and potentially smaller in size)   - implementing a wire migration tool that'd allow a space to be transported to another instance    Essentially the way to improve it is not the main thing - the main ask is to actually improve the space migration task to make it easier and faster for really large instance admins."

summary: Include 'BUILDNUMBER on CONFVERSION table and confluence.cfg.xml file' in the Post-Upgrade Checks for Confluence
description: "For a customer we noticed the upgrade page as a Success, despite the upgrade not being completed due to the *BUILDNUMBER* mismatch on *CONFVERSION* Table and *confluence.cfg.xml*. Though the workaround is pretty simple and adaptable we this should still be counted an incomplete upgrade and should print this on the UI and logs and point it to the relevant KB to expedite the fixing of the problem.    It seems that whatever checks(pre-upgrade and post-upgrade) that are performed currently to show the upgrade status page, does not consider the BUILDNUMBER matching of the binaries and the database.     Please help include this in the post-upgrade process check so that we could make the upgrade process and messaging even more reliable."

summary: Audit logs break after upgrading Confluence
description: "h3. Issue Summary    Audit logs don't work after upgrading Confluence to recent versions (which have the newer Audit logs). Happens specially with Confluence on Oracle database.     h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Install Confluence v7.3 (or any version older than v7.5)   # Connect it to Oracle DB  #* The issue doesn't happen on PGSql  # Upgrade Confluence to v7.7.4  # Navigate to *General Configuration* > *Audit Logs*    h3. Expected Results    Page should load properly with Audit information    h3. Actual Results    The following error is shown in the UI   !image-2020-10-13-12-08-26-157.png|thumbnail!     {{atlassian-confluence.logs}} show the following exception,  {code}  2020-10-13 10:47:40,950 WARN [http-nio-8090-exec-5] [confluence.impl.hibernate.ConfluenceHibernateTransactionManager] doRollback Performing rollback. Transactions:    ->[PluginReadWriteTx]: PROPAGATION_REQUIRED,ISOLATION_DEFAULT (Session #1262254918)   -- referer: http://localhost:8090/plugins/servlet/audit/ | url: /rest/auditing/1.0/events | traceId: 375d6f342320c025 | userName: admin  2020-10-13 10:47:40,955 ERROR [http-nio-8090-exec-5] [common.error.jersey.ThrowableExceptionMapper] toResponse Uncaught exception thrown by REST service: null   -- referer: http://localhost:8090/plugins/servlet/audit/ | url: /rest/auditing/1.0/events | traceId: 375d6f342320c025 | userName: admin  java.lang.NullPointerException   at java.util.Objects.requireNonNull(Objects.java:203)   at com.atlassian.audit.entity.AuditAuthor$Builder.build(AuditAuthor.java:139)   at com.atlassian.audit.ao.dao.AoAuditEntityMapper.map(AoAuditEntityMapper.java:36)   at java.util.stream.ReferencePipeline$3$1.accept(ReferencePipeline.java:193)   at java.util.Spliterators$ArraySpliterator.tryAdvance(Spliterators.java:958)   at java.util.stream.ReferencePipeline.forEachWithCancel(ReferencePipeline.java:126)   at java.util.stream.AbstractPipeline.copyIntoWithCancel(AbstractPipeline.java:498)   at java.util.stream.AbstractPipeline.copyInto(AbstractPipeline.java:485)   at java.util.stream.AbstractPipeline.wrapAndCopyInto(AbstractPipeline.java:471)   at java.util.stream.ReduceOps$ReduceOp.evaluateSequential(ReduceOps.java:708)   at java.util.stream.AbstractPipeline.evaluate(AbstractPipeline.java:234)   at java.util.stream.ReferencePipeline.collect(ReferencePipeline.java:499)   at com.atlassian.audit.ao.dao.AoAuditEntityDao.createPage(AoAuditEntityDao.java:112)  .  .  {code}    h3. Diagnosis & Workaround    There are NULL values under column *user_id* in table *AO_C77861_AUDIT_ENTITY* which seem to be causing this issue. They can be identified using the following SQL,    {code:sql}  select * from ""AO_C77861_AUDIT_ENTITY"" where user_id is null;  {code}    If NULL values are found, update them to *-1*  {code:sql}  update ""AO_C77861_AUDIT_ENTITY"" set user_id = -1  where user_id is null and user_type = 'user';  {code}  (!) Always test this in a staging environment of Confluence, before making any changes in production.   "

summary: Admin is unable to remove owners space permissions from personal space.
description: "h3. Issue Summary    When trying to remove permissions from the owner in its personal space, the action will not have effects and no messages will be displayed with the reasons for it. It is currently expected, but, a message should be displayed with the reasons for it.  h3. Steps to Reproduce    (i) Make sure to have administrator permissions for the space in question.   # Go to an existing personal space;   # Select *Space settings* at the sidebar;   # Take note of the space's creator and select *Permissions*;   # Select *Edit* and toggle any of the permissions from the owner (to remove them);   # *Save* the performed changes;    h3. Expected Results    A message clarifying why it can't be removed should be displayed.  h3. Actual Results    Nothing happens. The page is refreshed and the owner permissions are in the same state.   !Screen Shot 2020-10-12 at 3.34.09 PM.png|thumbnail!   h3. Workaround    Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available  h3. Notes    I couldn't find any information in our docs explicitly stating this is/isn't possible. However, the Community article elaborates this isn't possible to modifies owners permissions for their personal space. https://community.<USER>t5/Confluence-questions/How-do-I-remove-the-permissions-for-the-owner-creator-or-a/qaq-p/688035"

summary: Allow Confluence to consider alternate spellings in search. Such as: center vs centre
description: "h3. Problem  We have different dialects in English, such as the American and Brittish, which there are some words that are spelled different. However, Confluence doesn't understand that natively and you need to use *tilde (~)* for the Lucene to recognize.    h3. Suggested Solution  Confluence to recognize words spelled different, such as:  * Organize vs Organise  * Center vs Centre  * Favorite vs Favourite  * Behavior vs Behaviour    Just small examples above.    h3. Why This Is Important  Users coming from different nationalities won't need to use the fuzzy search to find the content as the one who wrote the document.    h3. Workaround  Use tilde (~) when searching for words that might have a different spelling."

summary: Incorrect German Translation in notifications
description: "h3. Issue Summary    When a user add a comment to a page, the notification for affected user is translated wrong    This issue appears on 6.15.9 as well as 7.3.3. There are translation issues with Notifications in German  h3. Steps to Reproduce    The problem lies in two of the sentences display by the system when notifications are sent.   ""1 Kommentar zu dies Seite""   ""testuserlb hat einen dies kommentiert""    Which would roughly translate back to:   ""1 comment the this page"" (not accurate)   ""testuserlb has commented a this""(not accurate)  h3. Expected Results    The correct translation would be:   ""1 Kommentar zu dieser Seite"" or ""1 Kommentar auf dieser Seite""   ""testuserlb hat folgendes kommentiert"" or ""tesuserlb hat folgenden Kommentar hinzugefügt""  h3. Actual Results    The translations are seen as below.    ""1 Kommentar zu dies Seite""   ""testuserlb hat einen dies kommentiert""    which translates to    ""1 comment the this page"" (not accurate)   ""testuserlb has commented a this""(not accurate)    !Translation_Issue_7_3_3.PNG!  h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"

summary: Full reindexing leads to memory leak
description: "h3. Issue Summary    Full reindex process stores different objects in ThreadLocal, and do not release them until the whole reindexing process finishes. The larger database is, the more memory is consumed by the end of reindexing process.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Generate a database with at least 10M pages   # Run jconsole and attach it to the existing Confluence process   # Trigger full reindexing   # Monitor memory consumption     h3. Expected Results    Memory usage is not increasing (cache is not taken into account).  h3. Actual Results    Memory usage grows. Memory is released only when the reindex process finishes.    !image-2020-10-08-10-01-00-416.png|width=1004,height=834!    Heap dump analysis shows that 4 reindexing threads consume more that 2G of RAM (mostly by strings containing SQL queries).    !image-2020-10-08-10-28-06-565.png|width=919,height=671!    Another heap dump (for the larger instance) shows that 8Gb of RAM used by indexing threads. Memory was consumed by instances of Lucene42DocValuesProducer.  h3. Workaround    Before triggering full reindexing, allocate more memory for one node, and trigger reindexing on that node.    Run reindexing outside of peak hours."

Workaround    Before triggering full reindexing, allocate more memory for one node, and trigger reindexing on that node.    Run reindexing outside of peak hours."""

Expected Results  The template with an *include page* macro retains its settings (page title) and can also be updated if required.    h3. Add a page title before hitting publish.}}    From the browser developer tools when trying to publish a page created from a template with the include page macro:      h3. summary: Automated PDF Preview file generation for customers that go through the "rebuild file preview from scratch" steps
description: "h3. Problem Definition  Customers that have Office Files (.docx, .xlsx, .pptx, <USER>) with Preview issues, tend to try the instructions from the KB article below:    *  [How to rebuild the file preview cache from scratch in Confluence|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/how-to-rebuild-the-file-preview-cache-from-scratch-in-confluence-765756128.html]    According to the KB above, the instructions mention that after clearing the contents or simply moving the folders  {{<confluence_home>/viewfile}}, {{<confluence_home>/shared-home/dcl-document/}}, {{<confluence_home>/shared-home/dcl-document_hd/}} and {{<confluence_home>/shared-home/dcl-thumbnail/}} and right after, restarting Confluence, the file preview cache will rebuild, and documents should render as expected. That does not seem to be happening. It seems that users need to re-preview the files they need, on an ad-hoc basis, to have the preview re-created.    h3. Suggested Solution  One customer suggested an automation to recreate this content at startup, such as we already do for other types of cache. For them, this would be very useful, since they would like to have the preview available for all files.    h3. Workaround  Currently, after you clear the file preview cache, the users apparently have to preview the files again ad-hoc to recreate the preview."

summary: License counter does not reflect the disabled users immediately
description: "h3. Issue Summary    The License Counter does not reflect the disabled users immediately.  I don't see the same issue on 6.15.2.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Added 12 users into Confluence with external LDAP, so it exceeds user limit with a 10-user license, and pages cannot be edited.   # Removed 3 users from *confluence-user* group in the LDAP and synchronized.   # License Details did not change, and still showed 12 active users:   !license-12users.png|thumbnail!   # Still unable to edit the pages.  So, the issue prevent editing the page!   # Clicked the *Refresh* button on the License details, then the license counter displayed correctly, and the page can be edited.    h3. Expected Results    As soon as disable users, the license counter should be reduced and allow to edit the pages.  h3. Actual Results    The license counter does not update until clicking the *Refresh* button in the License details.  h3. Workaround    To update the license counter,   * Try to click *Refresh* button in the *General Configuration > License Details*   * Or flush the cache on *General Configuration > Cache Statistics > Flush All*."

summary: Accessibility - Keyboard Trap in Search Panel
description: "Once you tab into the search that brings up the search pane, there's no way through the keyboard to get out. Several clients have raised this issue since it is significantly impacting some of their end users. This feature request is to either add a way to deal with the keyboard trap with the new Quick Search Panel, or at the very least disable the new search.  h3. Workaround    You can use custom html to target the search input and then add a tabindex value of -1 to remove it from the list of items you can tab into. For example, you could do this via jQuery with the following code:    This will enable you to move past the search field with using only the keyboard. Then you can just open it manually with the '/' keyboard shortcut if need be."

summary: Improve 'Cache Statistics' Page in the UI
description: "As Confluence Data Center and Server scale in environments, tuning the cache assets to scale along with them can become a daunting task for admins.    On the [Cache Statistics|https://confluence.<USER>doc/cache-statistics-150472.html] knowledge base article, Atlassian references several ways to identify and tune caches for a more performant Confluence application, however there are a few improvements that would help larger, more active deployments as they scale.  h3. Time range for viewing cache statistics    At present, there is a 'flush' cache option for most (if not all) the application caches, and this also flushes the cache stats. However, I believe what would be beneficial here would be a way to check the cache statistics via a time range, so that the admins can compare pre and post flushing of the cache and check if the effectiveness has improved (or whichever metric you're focusing on at that time).  h4. Better cache statistics monitoring    As per CONFSERVER-12837, Atlassian states that they can't recommend cache values because the size of caches depends on a lot of external factors within the host machine and the cluster and primarily should be part of the tuning process to keep the application healthy.    However, I feel like some help here (besides a knowledge base article) can be warranted inside the UI. At the very least, a way to monitor the cache usage in the UI instead of relying on support/admins to review it during a time of crisis/instability/sluggishness. Maybe even a way to suggest values based on effectiveness over a period of time-based on some of the known configuration values. This would help admins make a decision on which caches to focus on during health checks to prevent problems before they arise."

summary: Files uploaded in Attachment macro via drag and drop is not visible in Attachment macro pointing to a different page.
description: "h3. Issue Summary    Files uploaded in Attachment macro via drag and drop is not visible in Attachment macro pointing to a different page. It gets added as an attachment to the current page.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a new Page A   # Add attachment Macro in it and point it to a different page B with attachments.   # Save the Page   # All the attachments from Page B are visible in the attachment macro on Page A   # Drag and Drop an attachment in the macro. The attachment should be visible on the macro but is added in the attachments in the Page A but not visible in the attachment Macro.    h3. Expected Results    Attachments should be added in the Attachment Macro.    If I used browse instead of drag and drop, the attachments are visible in the attachment macro. However with drag and drop this doesn't seem to be the case.     The attachment should be added to the page B and not to page A.  h3. Actual Results    Attachments are added to the current page.  h3. Workaround    Use browse instead of drag and drop, the attachments will be  visible in the attachment macro."

The attachment should be visible on the macro but is added in the attachments in the Page A but not visible in the attachment Macro.    h3. Expected Results    Attachments should be added in the Attachment Macro.    If I used browse instead of drag and drop, the attachments are visible in the attachment macro. However with drag and drop this doesn't seem to be the case.     The attachment should be added to the page B and not to page A.  h3. Actual Results    Attachments are added to the current page.  h3. Workaround    Use browse instead of drag and drop, the attachments will be  visible in the attachment macro."""
label: non-design

summary: Uploading file to Confluence page without space attachment permission can throw incorrect error
description: "h3. Issue Summary    When a user that does not have the Attachments ""Add"" permission on the space, and they drag and drop a file to Confluence, a generic error is sometimes thrown.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Install Confluence 7.4.0  # Setup permissions as shown here: [^spaceperms.png]  # With a user that only belongs to *confluence-users* go to a page on that space  # When viewing the page (not in the editor), drag and drop an attachment to Confluence of small size (less than 1mb)  # Then upload a larger attachment (50mb+)    h3. Expected Results    The following error should be returned right away:      This error is usually thrown when uploading small files.     h3. Actual Results    The below exception is thrown in the browser:   !wrongerror.png|thumbnail!    The dialog window will also show an upload ""in progress"", and show a progress bar, even if the user does not have permissions to upload to any page on the space. Eventually it throws the ""server cannot be reached"" error    h3. Notes  This behavior is intermittent. Sometimes it works and throws the appropriate permission error, and sometimes it cannot be replicated. The following has been tried to workaround this issue:   * Tested in multiple browsers  * Reset *server.xml* to make sure the default is being used  * Removed *protocol=""org.apache.coyote.http11.Http11Nio2Protocol""* from server.xml  * Flushed browser and Confluence cache  * Multiple file formats (zip, png, jpg, mp4, <USER>)    *_Regardless of the error, the file will not be uploaded if you do not have permission_*. So this should not pose any security risks.     h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"

summary: Search for anchors to insert anchor links instead of typing them verbatim in Advanced area
description: "h2. Problem    Inserting anchor links is error prone and slow, [see the current documentation|https://confluence.<USER>display/CONF56/Working+with+Anchors]. Very similar to https://jira.<USER>browse/CONFCLOUD-67372 for Confluence Cloud, this is a request for similar enhancement on Confluence Server / Datacenter.    !image-2020-10-01-13-20-43-641.png|width=550,height=299!  h2. Suggestion/Feature Request:    Streamline and speed up inserting links to anchors with having a search methodology for anchors (similar to how pages are searched). This could prevent errors with spaces in the anchor name, typos, and getting the wrong anchor. Also, if it were in a new area specifically for anchor links, and not hidden on the Advanced tab it would be quicker for new users to apply. "

summary: Confluence pages aren't referenced under "Mentioned in" in Jira after space restore
description: "h3. Issue Summary    When a space backup is restored on Confluence instance, none of the Confluence pages where Jira issues are included get references on the Jira side under I*ssue Links >> Mentioned in*  h3. Steps to Reproduce    For this test, you need two instances of Confluence and one Jira instance   # Create application link between Confluence-A instance and Jira   # Create application link between Confluence-B instance and Jira   # Create *page-A* inside *space-A* on *Confluence-A*   # Insert any Jira Issue from Jira projects inside *Page-A* >> publish   (notice that *1 Jira link* icon appears next to the page title)   # Go to Jira and check the Jira issue, you see that the Confluence page is referenced under *issue links* >> *mentioned in*   # **Now export *space-A* from *Confluence-A* instance as XML   # Restore the space XML file on *Confluence-B*   # Go to *Page-A* on *Confluence-B,* notice +there is no icon+ next to page title showing (1 Jira link) and *page-A* from *Confluence-B* isn't listed under *mentioned in* section of the Jira issue on the Jira side    h3. Expected Results    Upon restoring a space backup, Confluence pages with Jira issues should be listed under *""Issue links >> Mentioned In""* in Jira accordingly  h3. Actual Results    Upon restoring a space backup, Confluence pages with Jira issues are not listed under *""Issue links >> Mentioned In""* in Jira  h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"

summary: Modified users display as anonymous in the recently updated macro
description: "h3. Issue Summary    When a user is modified(disabled, removed from AD, renamed), the Recently Updated macro will show that an Anonymous user made changes to the profile/page of the modified user. description: "h3. Issue Summary    Regarding [https://confluence.<USER>kb/oauth-error-oauth_problem-signature_invalid-720406720.html], suggested KB talks about to change the useBodyEncodingForURI to false but Confluence Docker image doesn't have any parameter to change this value other than the default.  h3. summary: Trying to download all files from a Knowledge Base article from Jira Servicedesk with an unlicensed user results in a Not Permitted message
description: "h3. Issue Summary    As an unlicensed user, if we try to download all attachments via the “Download All” link in the Attachments Macro, Confluence blocks the procedure with a “Not Permitted” message.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a knowledge base for Jira <USER>  # Select the “All active users and customers can access the knowledge base without a Confluence space”   # Create a new page for the knowledge base   # Place an Attachments macro in the page   # Create a user with Jira <USER>access   # If the user base is different for Confluence and Jira, create the user in Confluence as indicated by documentation ([https://confluence.<USER>servicedeskserver/knowledge-base-settings-and-permissions-956713322.html])   # Go the page you created earlier   # Click to “Download All”    h3. Expected Results    All the attachments are downloaded  h3. Actual Results    There is a redirect to an address that looks like this: localhost:8090/confluence/pages/downloadallattachments.action?pageid=<pageID> and there is a “Not Permitted” message shown.    The same behavior is present through the Customer portal.    !Screen Shot 2020-09-29 at 13.54.06.png|width=287,height=172!    !Screen Shot 2020-09-29 at 13.54.17.png|width=285,height=169!    !Screen Shot 2020-09-29 at 13.54.28.png|width=287,height=169!  h3. Workaround    The user can either click the attachment link in the macro and then download the file from the tab that opens up after the click or select to preview the file and then download it from there.    Enabling anonymous access and then not logging in to Confluence also works around this behavior.    !Screen Shot 2020-09-29 at 13.54.42.png|width=291,height=172!"

summary: Page Property Report Macro does not show the task list element without any text
description: "h3. Issue Summary    If you generate a page property report macro from a page property macro with a column including task list element (the square brackets) without any text, the page property report does not show anything for this column.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a page   # Insert a page property macro with the desired label   # Use task list element without any text for one of the page property column's value   # Create another page   # Insert a page property report macro and use the above label for filtering the above page property    h3. Expected Results    Column with the task list element is displayed in the page property report    !6_15_4-page_properties.png|width=256,height=146!!6_15_4-page_properties_report.png|width=256,height=146!  h3. Actual Results    Column with the task list element is not displayed, this column is empty.      !7_7_3-page_properties.png|width=256,height=146!!7_7_3-page_properties_report.png|width=256,height=146!  h3. Workaround    1. If you put some text to the task list element, it will be displayed in the page property report.    2. Enable ""masterdetail.legacy.parse"" dark feature in Confluence. This will result in slower report generation and a hard limit of 1000 rows in the report.            (i) This is working as expected in the older version of Confluence, tested in version 6.15.4."

summary: Spam Control for Password Reset email
description: "h3. Problem Definition  When Confluence is configured with a mail server, Confluence users are able to request for a Password Reset email from the login page by entering a valid username or email address after clicking on the ""Forgot your password?"" link.   !login screenshot.png|thumbnail!       However, there is no spam control to this action as the user can click the ""Send it to me"" button multiple times without any cooldown or verification which in a way can cause a burden to either Confluence or the mail server.   !send link screenshot.png|thumbnail!     h3. Suggested Solution  Implementation of spam control such as CAPTCHA when a continuous attempt to request the password reset email is detected."
text: Spam Control for Password Reset email [SEP] """h3. Problem Definition  When Confluence is configured with a mail server, Confluence users are able to request for a Password Reset email from the login page by entering a valid username or email address after clicking on the """"Forgot your password?"""" link.   !login screenshot.png|thumbnail!       However, there is no spam control to this action as the user can click the """"Send it to me"""" button multiple times without any cooldown or verification which in a way can cause a burden to either Confluence or the mail server.   !send link screenshot.png|thumbnail!     h3. Suggested Solution  Implementation of spam control such as CAPTCHA when a continuous attempt to request the password reset email is detected."""

summary: LDAP users that are auto-added to a can-use group are not re-indexed
description: "h3. Issue Summary    When an LDAP user is synchronized to Confluence without membership in a group that confers can-use access, that user will not be indexed. If LDAP is configured to auto-add users to a ""can-use"" group when they authenticate for the first time, then when the user logs in they will be able to access and use Confluence, but they won't be re-indexed with that group, and so will still not be found in any feature that uses the index, like mentions, the people directory, <USER>     h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create LDAP directory (Connector type) in Confluence, making sure that none of the LDAP groups confer can-use access  # Configure the directory to auto-add users to ""confluence-users""   # Synchronize directory. User should appear on the ""Users"" screen without membership in any can-use group.  # Search for that user in People Directory or try to mention them on a page. This shouldn't be possible since the user does not belong to a group that can access Confluence. This is expected.   # Wait for user to log in for first time and be added to ""confluence-users"" group  # Search for that user in People Directory or try to mention them on a page    h3. Expected Results    The user can be found in the People Directory and in any other feature that utilizes the index    h3. Actual Results    User cannot be found in the index, even though they appear on the ""Users"" screen and can log in with membership in a can-use group    h3. Workaround    Make a minor edit to the user's profile to trigger a re-index of their user object. "

summary: Creating questions in Questions for Confluence solely using certain characters leaves the question inaccessible
description: "h3. Issue Summary    _Questions for Confluence_ doesn't handle certain special characters solely as the title.  _Questions for Confluence_ still accepts the question in the UI, but afterwards users can't easily access the question and therefore cannot delete it.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Install _Questions for Confluence_   # Enter a new question of '????' (without the quotes)   # The question will show up in the 'Recent' questions list but the link to it is not functional and the question cannot be easily removed unless the '?' characters are passed on the URL as %3F.  eg.  (http://localhost:8090/questions/5144594/%3F%3F%3F%3F%3F?src=browse)    h3. Expected Results    Any accepted input should be handled and encoded correctly in links encoded within _Questions for Confluence_.  h3. Actual Results    A created question using only special characters (eg. ???) is not accessible.       ||Question||Encoding||Ok?||  |Is there anyone out there?|http://localhost:8090/questions/5144606/is-there-anyone-out-there?src=browse|Ok|  |_????_|http://localhost:8090/questions/5144594/?src=browse|Not ok|  |##|http://localhost:8090/questions/5144616/%23%23|Ok|  |%%%|http://localhost:8090/questions/5144627/?src=browse|Not ok|            h3. Workaround    Manually append the character encoding if it has been removed. For example, to access a question of '????', modify the URL from:    to    UTF-8 encoding can be referenced from [W3schools|https://www.w3schools.com/tags/ref_urlencode.ASP].     Whilst not a suitable workaround for users, this will allow inaccessible questions to be accessed and deleted."

summary: We would like to have reminders set for each individual events rather than, just each Event Types, like we have in Microsoft Outlook Calendar.
description: "h3. Issue Summary    We would like to have reminders set for each individual events rather than, just each *Event Types*, like we have in Microsoft Outlook Calendar. Maybe we can configure a global reminder for *event type*, and then separate reminder is also possible for each individual events created.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create an Event with Event Type as default ""Events""   # Navigate to the calendar and try to set reminder for the Event created (We don't have an option here and reminder can be set against the Event Types only)    h3. Expected Results    Once, an Event is created, we should be able to set reminder for each event individually.  h3. Actual Results    We can set the Reminder only for *Event Types* as a whole but not to each event created individually.  h3. Workaround    We can create multiple Custom Event Types, as a workaround, which will allow us to set reminder for each of them individually. But, yes that can be an extra work with lots of Custom Event types to manage. I have tried this and below is the screenshot for your quick reference:     !screenshot-1.png|thumbnail! "

summary: Creating custom event and changing calendar creates default type event in Team Calendars
description: "h3. Issue Summary    When creating an event, changing the calendar while custom event selected, and creating the event without touching another thing, a default type event will be created instead of a custom one. And also, if the default types are hidden for this calendar, deleting the event is not possible without allowing the default type again.    Confluence version: 7.7.3   Team Calendars version: 6.0.53  h3. Steps to Reproduce    [^screen_capture_2020_09_25.mp4]   # Create two calendars (Calendar-1, Calendar-2)   # In the calendar edit menu, navigate to ""Event Types"" and hide all default event types for both calendar   # Again in the ""Event Types"" menu, create one custom event type for each calendar (C1-Custom-Event for Calendar-1, C2-Custom-Event for Calendar-2)   # Click ""Add Event""   # Leave the selected calendar on which one comes default. (ex: Calendar-1)   # Select the custom event created for this calendar. (ex: C1-Custom-Event)   # When the event details section is displayed, change the calendar to the other one (ex: Calendar-1 -> Calendar-2)   # Without changing anything else, enter the event name and save it.    h3. Expected Results    Event created with the selected custom event type.  h3. Actual Results    Event created with the default event type.         The below exception is thrown in the Atlassian-confluence.log file:    h3. Workaround    When creating an event, after changing the calendar with a custom event selected, even the custom event type populated correctly to the combo box, you need to choose it again. That way, you can create the event with the desired type."

(ex: Calendar-1)   # Select the custom event created for this calendar. (ex: C1-Custom-Event)   # When the event details section is displayed, change the calendar to the other one (ex: Calendar-1 -> Calendar-2)   # Without changing anything else, enter the event name and save it.    h3. That way, you can create the event with the desired type."""

summary: Clicking on old versions of an attachment with + sign under "Attachments macro" results in page not found
description: "h3. Issue Summary    When Confluence is running behind a reverse proxy (tested with nginx and apache), clicking on an older version of a file listed under attachments macro results in page not found.    This issue does not occur when you go to ""..."" >> attachments >> and click on old versions of the attachments.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Run Confluence behind a reverse proxy   # Create a new page   # Add attachment with + sign in the filename (repeat to create few versions)   # Insert ""Attachments Macro""   # Publish the page   # Next to the attachment filename, expand versions and click on an old version of the file   # Error Page not found    h3. Expected Results    The file should be downloaded when clicking on old version of a file (with + sign in the filename) in Attachments macro  h3. Actual Results    When clicking on old version of a file (with + sign in the filename) in Attachments macro, you get page not found  h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"

summary: Search in current location / space
description: "If I am looking at a page I would like the option to do a search which searches only the current page / children. This would make the search feature 10x more useful on a day to day basis.    If I am looking at a space I would like the option to do a search which only searches the current space (without having to manually add the space name in the search screen after I've already done the search). This would prevent the extra (annoying) step of having to work out the space you were looking at and entering this manually on the search page.    This could easily be achieved in the UI by adding a couple of checkboxes next to the search box, for example: ""Current Space"", ""Current Location"" would be simple and contextual."

summary: Reorder pages alphabetically (permanently)
description: "On confluence spaces, it is possible to reorder pages alphabetically but this happens to only be done ones. Meaning when a new page is created, the alphabetical order is no longer respected and the page gets created at the bottom of the pages tree.    The purpose of ordering alphabetically is to allow order and consistency in viewing the pages and creating new page should obey the alphabetical ordering once selected.    This is the same feature as requested under [CONFCLOUD-65454|https://jira.<USER>browse/CONFCLOUD-65454], but for Confluence Server and Data Center."

summary: Specifying a space key for a page that has a colon (:) right after the first word in the title and it exists on the same space where the Include Page macro is included removes the space key after first edit
description: "h3. Issue Summary    Currently, if a user specifies a page link inside the Include Page macro using the Space Key parameter and the page happens to have the colon (:) right after the first word in the title, the macro will remove the space key and consider part of the title as the space key value when you first edit the page where the macro is placed.    {{If the page title is ‘THIS: is a test title created’}}    *This:*    {{MAL:THIS: is a test title created}}    *Becomes this:*    {{THIS: is a test title created}}    Adding the space key parameter again seems to fix the issue and editing the page can be done normally.    Documentation on the macro    [https://confluence.<USER>doc/include-page-macro-139514.html]  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a new page that contains a colon right after the first word in the title   # Publish the page   # Create a new page on the same space as the previous one   # Add the Include Page macro   # Specify the previously created page using space key value (eg. SPC:THIS: title)   # Publish the page   # Edit the page    h3. Expected Results    The space key value is still there and the page is found  h3. Actual Results    The space key value is removed and the page is not found.    *Before publishing the page*    !Screen Shot 2020-09-23 at 15.27.42.png|width=418,height=113!    *After publishing the page*    *!Screen Shot 2020-09-23 at 15.27.50.png|width=379,height=81!*    *First page edit*    notice the missing space key    *!Screen Shot 2020-09-23 at 15.28.00.png|width=415,height=126!*    *First edit, view mode*    *!Screen Shot 2020-09-23 at 15.28.08.png|width=365,height=90!*  h3. Workaround    Edit the page and add the space key back. After the first edit, the space key value sticks to the original value."

Actual Results    The space key value is removed and the page is not found.    *Before publishing the page*    !Screen Shot 2020-09-23 at 15.27.42.png|width=418,height=113!    *After publishing the page*    *!Screen Shot 2020-09-23 at 15.27.50.png|width=379,height=81!*    *First page edit*    notice the missing space key    *!Screen Shot 2020-09-23 at 15.28.00.png|width=415,height=126!*    *First edit, view mode*    *!Screen Shot 2020-09-23 at 15.28.08.png|width=365,height=90!*  h3. Workaround    Edit the page and add the space key back. After the first edit, the space key value sticks to the original value."""

summary: Confluence allows importing of Secured Word documents for "Import Word Document" in Space which results in error
description: "h2. Summary    Confluence allows users to select a Secured document for the ""Import Word Document"" feature in the Space. This results in a NullPointerException being presented on the screen instead of being handled more gracefully.  h2. Steps to reproduce   # Navigate to any Space in Confluence   # Navigate to ""..."" > Import Word Document   # Select a secured(password protected) word document and click the ""Import"" button    h2. Expected behavior   * Confluence will not allow the Import button to be pressed when the selected document is secured Word document   * OR, the import will proceed initially, but when an error is encountered, display a user-friendly error like ""This file is encrypted and can therefore not be imported""    h2. Actual behavior    An ""Oops - an error has occurred"" screen is shown, and a large stack trace is presented to the user:      h2. Workaround  Use non-protected word document."

summary: Image is not updated in edit mode when using drag and drop
description: "h3. Issue Summary    In Confluence, when dragging & dropping a new version of an image to a page while viewing it and then accessing edit mode, the new version of the image is not displayed. To reflect this change to edit mode, the page needs to be published again with/without any changes.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a page, add an image, and save it.   # In view mode, drag and drop a new version of the image with the same name to the page. The image is updated in *View Mode* as expected.   # Click *Edit* to enter edit mode; the image is displayed in the previous version.    Screen Capture of the issue    [https://share.getcloudapp.com/2Nuyg4YW]  h3. Expected Results    Edit mode will display the new version of the image.  h3. Actual Results    Edit mode will display the previous version of the image.  h3. Workaround   * After uploading a new version of the image in view mode, accessing edit mode (at this point, you see the old version), and publishing it with/without any changes, reflects the change you did in view mode if you go again in edit mode.    h4. Findings    (i) When the _Disable cache_ feature of the browser's developer tools is enabled, it is working as expected."

Findings    (i) When the _Disable cache_ feature of the browser's developer tools is enabled, it is working as expected."""

summary: Pasting code from the Code Block Macro in edit mode removes line break
description: "h3. Issue Summary    If you copy code from the Code Block Macro in edit mode into any plain text editor, the code loses the line breaks. This effectively removes all code indentation.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Copy any code from the code block macro in edit mode by selecting the code and copy via CTRL+C/CMD+C   # Paste the copied copy in a plain text editor or web page.    h3. Expected Results    The copied code is pasted maintaining the indentation of the code.  h3. Actual Results    All the line breaks are removed from the code, subsequently removing all code indentation. Attaching the screen recording for the same.    [^Codeblock copypaste.mov]  h3. Workaround   * Click on the Code Block macro frame and then hit CTRL/CMD+C the newlines are retained when pasting the text elsewhere."

summary: Confluence pages that use the Office Powerpoint macro to reference corrupt PPTX files will generate unterminated threads in the JVM
description: "h3. Issue Summary    Confluence pages that use the Office Powerpoint macro to reference corrupt PPTX files will generate unterminated threads in the JVM every time those pages are loaded..    This appears related to _aspose-slides-19.6_, so Confluence 7.0 - 7.7 is currently affected. Earlier versions of Confluence do not exhibit this problem.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Install Confluence 7.x (Server or Data Center)   # Upload a corrupt Microsoft Powerpoint slide to any page (PPT/PPTX)   # Create a new page and add the 'Office Powerpoint' macro, then reference the corrupt PPTX attachment on the previous page   # Every time the page with the macro is loaded, another '{{conversion-slides-interrupter-thread-0}}' thread  will be generated in the Confluence JVM.    h3. Expected Results    Bad attachments in Confluence shouldn't cause unbounded growth of the number of JVM threads.  h3. Actual Results    The below exception (which may differ or even be non-existent, depending on where the corruption in the PPTX occurs) is thrown in the atlassian-confluence.log file:    'jcmd $CONF_PID Thread.print | grep interrupt' will also show the number of these threads increasing after each page load (of the macro). Here are the threads after 7 page loads:    h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behaviour. A workaround will be added here when available"

summary: Accessing SSO 2.0 configuration as a Confluence Administrator ends up in an infinite login loop
description: "h3. Issue Summary    When you are logged in Confluence through SAML SSO, with Confluence Administrator permission you can't access SSO 2.0 configuration.    By clicking on this menu item, you are redirected to the IdP login page and entering user credentials result in an infinite redirection to the same login form.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Set up Confluence with SAML SSO as the primary authentication   # Log with a user that has Confluence Administrator permission (and isn't a <USER>   # Access the SSO 2.0 menu in Confluence Administration    h3. Expected Results   * A message informing the lack of permission to access this feature is displayed; *or*   * A Confluence Administrator can't see the SSO 2.0 menu; *or*   * Be allowed to see the SSO 2.0 configuration screen    h3. Actual Results   * User is redirected to the IdP login page and can't exit the redirection loop.   ** /login.action?os_destination=%2Fplugins%2Fservlet%2Fauthentication-config&permissionViolation=true   ** /plugins/servlet/authentication-config   ** /login.action?os_destination=%2Fplugins%2Fservlet%2Fauthentication-config&permissionViolation=true   ** /plugins/servlet/authentication-config   ** <USER>    h3. Workaround   * There's no workaround. If the user needs to access the SSO 2.0 settings, he'll need a <USER>permission."

summary: Confluence does not stop processing the previous search request if it receives another one
description: "h3. Issue Summary    When a user is typing something is the quick search box (or in the KB search field in JSD), Confluence is receiving a series of subsequent requests, for example:   * Why do   * Why dogs c   * Why dogs cannot fl   * Why dogs cannot fly?    Small instances are able to provide the answers quickly (before the next request is received).    In case of the large instances, processing of each request could take a few seconds. As a result, Confluence could be overwhelmed with processing a lot of similar requests. What's more, a browser cancelled all previous requests, so the server is just wasting its resources, because nothing is waiting for the results.   h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Have a large instance where each search request takes a few seconds   # Start typing something in quick search box (or in JSD KB search). You should add a delay of one second between entering characters (in this case a separate search request will be sent for each character).   # Make a thread dump and see that a lot of threads are searching the documents.     h3. Expected Results    Cancel and stop the previous search request(s), sent by this user.    Note: the thread should wait until the previous search request finished its work. Because each search request processing consists of two parts:   # Get all allowed/not allowed spaces   # Scan the documents    The first part could be slow (if there is nothing in the cache) and we can't interrupt it. It's better to wait until this part is finished, and the results will be put to the cache. So the second thread will be able to use cached values.    The second part can be interrupted any time.    Also, if a search request notice that there are two (configurable) or more requests are already doing ""search"" for this user, it should respond with ""too many requests"" and do not block one more thread.  h3. Actual Results    A lot of threads are ""searching"" something.  h3. Workaround    Disable quick search. Disable KB plugin."

Expected Results    Cancel and stop the previous search request(s), sent by this user.    Note: the thread should wait until the previous search request finished its work. Because each search request processing consists of two parts:   # Get all allowed/not allowed spaces   # Scan the documents    The first part could be slow (if there is nothing in the cache) and we can't interrupt it. It's better to wait until this part is finished, and the results will be put to the cache. So the second thread will be able to use cached values.    The second part can be interrupted any time.    Also, if a search request notice that there are two (configurable) or more requests are already doing """"search"""" for this user, it should respond with """"too many requests"""" and do not block one more thread.  h3. Actual Results    A lot of threads are """"searching"""" something.  h3. Workaround    Disable quick search. Disable KB plugin."""
label: non-design

summary: Share a template across multiple spaces but not globally
description: "h3. Background    From my research, there is no current way to share a template across multiple spaces without making it global to all users. We are in a large company with many separate spaces and tens of thousands of users, so global is too broad for most templates that a team would create.    For example, one team with their own space has created a functional spec template that other users in a different space may find useful, but we don't want to spam thousands of people with this in their create page popups.     The current solutions I see are:    1) Create a new template in the 2nd space by copy and paste   .. or ..    2) Create a global template for all Confluence users         This is also related to the ability to [copy/import/export|https://jira.<USER>browse/CONFSERVER-7752?error=login_required&error_description=Login+required&state=4f84a1bf-7b50-4e6b-8c50-b0c726e4ed8f] templates using other than the keyboard copy and paste shortcuts.   h3. Request    This request is for an intermediate template option where a template can be configured to share across _some_ spaces within the Confluence installation but not _all_ spaces like a global template would.  h3. Benefits:   * Reduce redundancy and manual effort to maintain templates that may be evolving across spaces   * Restrict global template views to only relevant spaces.         Reference:    [Community discussion how to share templates|https://community.<USER>t5/Confluence-questions/How-do-I-share-templates-between-spaces-in-Confluence/qaq-p/84806]     "

summary: After Space import the Page properties report macro with ancestor doesn't work
description: "h3. Issue Summary    After a space import, the Page properties report with ancestor doesn't work as it still references the page ID of the previous instance.    h3. Environment  * Tested in Confluence 7.4.1 and Confluence 7.4.4  * The Origin Space can be from a Cloud instance or a Server/DC instance and the same issue happens    h3. Steps to Reproduce  h4. In the origin Space follow the Steps below:  # Create a new page, let's name it 'Parent Page'  # Create a child page under the newly created page, let's name it 'Child Page'  # In the 'Child page' add a *Page properties* macro and configure it with any information. For example:   !child_property.png|thumbnail!   # Label this 'Child page' with a new label, let's say 'test'  # In the 'Parent Page' add the *Page properties report* macro and configure it to filter for  #* Label: 'test'  #* In space: 'Current Space'  #* Add a new filter 'With ancestor' and Search for 'Parent page' it will look like this:   !macro01.png|thumbnail!   # Save the changes and Publish the page  # Go to Space tools > Content Tools > Export select XML as the option and create a Full Export of this space.    h4. In the target instance follow the steps below:    # Go to General configuration > Backup & Restore and import the newly created space export  # Once the import completes, go to the imported space > Parent Page    Here is the space exported from a test instance: [^Confluence-space-export-173616-4.xml.zip]     h3. Expected Results    You would see the Parent Page with the Page properties report macro showing the correct content.    h3. Actual Results    The macro in the 'Parent Page' will show 'No content found'   !no_content.png|thumbnail!     When you edit the page and edit the macro to check its configuration you no longer see the title of the page selected in the 'With ancestor' option, it will display the page ID, however, this is the page ID of the previous instance.   !with_page_id.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround    Delete the reference of the Page ID and search the page by its title instead, save the changes and publish the page."

summary: REST API search results limited to first 100,500 records
description: "h3. Issue Summary    When the START value in a REST API search reaches 100,000+ it appears to ignore the START value and just returns the result set from 100,000 through LIMIT, as if the START value was 100,000 (regardless of what it set to).  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a REST API content search for a very large result set (100,000+ records)   # Use [Pagination|https://developer.<USER>server/confluence/pagination-in-the-rest-api/] to search past your defined limit (or past the default limit of 500 records returned)   # Continue this search past the first 100,000 records in the result set     h3. Expected Results    All records in the 100,000+ result set are returned if queried for (limited to to a max 500 at a time).  h3. Actual Results    Only the first 100,500 records are returnable by the REST API search (which is the 100,000 [MAX_START_OFFSET|https://docs.<USER>atlassian-confluence/6.6.0/index.html?constant-values.html] value + the the 500 [MAX_LIMIT|https://docs.<USER>atlassian-confluence/6.6.0/index.html?constant-values.html] value)    Examples:    ""Failure"" here being that the final query didn't return the correct result set, it returned the *exact* same results as the 2nd query.   h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available.   * Alternatively, SQL queries could be used in place of a REST call to accomplish similar goals. "

summary: Pasting text on the Code Macro or the No Format Macro removes leading white spaces
description: "h3. Issue Summary    If you copy text from another source (web, plain text editor, code editor) and then paste it into the *Code Block* or the *No Format* macro, the leading white spaces are removed. This effectively removes all code indentation.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Type some indented code in a plain text editor or code editor   # Copy the text to your clip board (right-click copy or CTRL+C/CMD+C)   # Create a page   # Insert the Code Block macro   # Paste the previously copied code    h3. Expected Results    The copied text is pasted exactly as is.  h3. Actual Results    All the leading spaces are removed, subsequently removing all code indentation.    !Screen Shot 2020-09-18 at 10.39.45.png|width=366,height=169!    !Screen Shot 2020-09-18 at 10.40.04.png|width=373,height=115!  h3. Workaround    None. We need to manually indent the code."

summary: Configuration for Anonymous access control in Global permission section
description: "h3. *Problem*    Currently if you want to make your site visible publicly, you must enable anonymous access at site level.   # In the Global permission page in the *Anonymous Access* section, select the *Can use* checkbox.      And Space administrators can grant editing permissions to anonymous users permissions on a space by space basis to make spaces public.     Having said that there is no configuration available, which can suppress space edit permission for the anonymous users globally(for all the spaces at once).    The configuration which can be set by Confluence administrator in global permission to make site read only for anonymous users (they should not allowed to edit page or comment even space administrator assigned permission)  h3. *Suggested Solution*    The configuration which can be set by Confluence administrator in global permission section to make site read only for anonymous users (after setting up the configuration, anonymous users should not be allowed to edit page or comment in any space even space administrator has assigned permission)  h3. *Why This Is Important*    _When there are more spaces in the site it is difficult to manage._    _So if Confluence administrator wants ready only access for anonymous users they have to manage at space permission level ._   h3. *Workaround*    _Manage permission at the space level permission section._ "

The configuration which can be set by Confluence administrator in global permission to make site read only for anonymous users (they should not allowed to edit page or comment even space administrator assigned permission)  h3. *Suggested Solution*    The configuration which can be set by Confluence administrator in global permission section to make site read only for anonymous users (after setting up the configuration, anonymous users should not be allowed to edit page or comment in any space even space administrator has assigned permission)  h3. *Why This Is Important*    _When there are more spaces in the site it is difficult to manage._    _So if Confluence administrator wants ready only access for anonymous users they have to manage at space permission level ._   h3. *Workaround*    _Manage permission at the space level permission section._ """

summary: Batch Drag-and-drop of files cause the Publish Action to fail
description: "h3. Problem  When creating a new page and adding multiple attachments (the format or size doesn't seem to matter) via Drag-and-Drop at once, the Initial Page title will get removed and the next attempts to publish will fail    h3. Environment  Behavior reproduced with Confluence version *7.7.3* out-of-the-box installation and using:  * Chrome Version 85.0.4183.102 (Official Build) (64-bit)   * Firefox Version 80.0.1 (64-bit)    h3. Steps to Reproduce  * Create a new page  * Add a Page Title  * Drag-and-Drop 3 or multiple files on the page (in my testing 3 txt files containing one character each)  * Attempt to Publish the page    h3. Expected Results  The Page should be published without issue.    h3. Actual Results  The Page Title gets removed and the following attempt(s) to Publish the page will fail.    h3. Workaround  * Drag-and-drop files one at a time and not in batch  * Reloading the page in the browser and adding the title back will get the publish action to work    h3. Notes  A recording illustrating the issue is attached  "

summary: In the preview of Microsoft document and only under internet explorer it is possible to click on Edit in office button, without any effect.
description: "h3. Issue Summary  In the preview of Microsoft document (pptx, word or excel file) and only under Internet Explorer it is possible to click on Edit in the office button, without any effect. As per [edit in office documentation|https://confluence.<USER>conf74/edit-in-office-using-the-office-connector-1003128753.html] this is not possible and the only possible options are:  - Attachments page  - Attachments macro (choose Edit in Office beside each attached office file)   - Office Word and Office Excel macros – choose the Edit button above the content.  - Office PowerPoint macro – choose the edit icon on the viewer.    The button is *not visible* in Edge, Chrome or Firefox.     h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Install Confluence 7.4.1 on Windows.   # Enable edit in office in Confluence Administration > Office Connector.   # Create a page and add an excel document   # In IE click on the excel document in order to generate the preview.   # Once the preview is generated header icons    h3. Expected Results  In the header icons, 3 dots icons will not be displayed.    h3. Actual Results  In the header icons, 3 dots icons will not be displayed, and under it we can see Edit in Office button, that does not work.   !Screen Shot 2020-09-16 at 17.35.15.png|thumbnail!     h3.
