summary: Word Export of historical page versions
description: "h3. Summary    Confluence does not have an option to export page from history as Word  h3. Steps to reproduce    Tested on Confluence 7.3.5    1) Created new page   2) Edit content   3) Opened Info->View page history and clicked on first version of document   4) There is no option to 'Export to Word'  h3. Workaround    There are some potential workarounds.   * Pasting the PageID of the old page version into the URL ""https://example.com/exportword?pageId=<>"" works like a charm. So all that needs to be done is to include this button in the ""three dots menu (...)"" when viewing old page versions.   * The simplest is to restore to the previous version you want to export, perform the export, and then restore back to the current version.   * If you would like a record of all the previous versions you have printed, or you do not want to alter your version history which restores just for printing you could also copy the previous version to a new space when viewing them:  {quote} # Create a new space - eg; Old Versions   # View the page to export - published version   # View page history   # Click on a previous version to view it   # Now copy to the new space   # Export to word from the new space{quote}    You can delete these restores if you do not want it cluttering your history."
text: Word Export of historical page versions [SEP] """h3. Summary    Confluence does not have an option to export page from history as Word  h3. Steps to reproduce    Tested on Confluence 7.3.5    1) Created new page   2) Edit content   3) Opened Info->View page history and clicked on first version of document   4) There is no option to 'Export to Word'  h3. Workaround    There are some potential workarounds.   * Pasting the PageID of the old page version into the URL """"https://example.com/exportword?pageId=<>"""" works like a charm. So all that needs to be done is to include this button in the """"three dots menu (...)"""" when viewing old page versions.   * The simplest is to restore to the previous version you want to export, perform the export, and then restore back to the current version.   * If you would like a record of all the previous versions you have printed, or you do not want to alter your version history which restores just for printing you could also copy the previous version to a new space when viewing them:  {quote} # Create a new space - eg; Old Versions   # View the page to export - published version   # View page history   # Click on a previous version to view it   # Now copy to the new space   # Export to word from the new space{quote}    You can delete these restores if you do not want it cluttering your history."""

summary: 'Could not get unique fields for table' errors constantly printed on the logs after installation
description: "h3. Problem  *Could not get unique fields for table* and *java.sql.SQLException: Invalid argument(s) in call* errors after installation. Some UI functions might also not be working properly, like the workbox notifications or other menus.    h3. Environment    # Oracle Database    h3. Steps to Reproduce    # Follow the documentation to configure Oracle  #* https://confluence.<USER>doc/database-setup-for-oracle-173821.html  # While running it, Create a user with a dot on the name (example: conf.admin)  # Install Confluence and connect to this database    h3. Expected Results    No errors are seen and the instance works as expected.    h3. Actual Results    Some UI functionalities fail and this is seen repeateadly on the *atlassian-confluence.log* file:      h3. Workaround    Since Oracle does not allow renaming the schema, you need to drop it and create a new one without dots. Following the initial example, conf_admin should work fine. On the Confluence side, restart the application setup so it can create the tables on the new schema:    # Stop Confluence  # Remove *<Confluence-home>/confluence.cfg.xml*  # Restart Confluence  # You should be prompted to complete the setup again    h3. Notes    This has previously been reported as a suggestion, but opening it as a bug since Oracle itself allows schema names with dots based on the documentation:    * https://jira.<USER>browse/CONFSERVER-33680  * https://docs.oracle.com/cd/B19306_01/server.102/b14200/sql_elements008.htm"

summary: Confluence Mobile App doesn't redirect to Okta login page
description: "h3. Problem    The Confluence Mobile app is set to point to the login.action when a user attempts to login to Confluence. However, this impacts the use of IDP such as Okta, as users aren't redirected to the custom login page while using the app.   h3. Suggested Solution    Add a login URL field option to the admin page of the Mobile App, similar to the Jira Mobile App.  h3. Why This Is Important    _Would Allow our users to integrate with 3rd-party providers and still take advantage of using the Confluence Mobile._   h3. Workaround    _If a reverse proxy is in play, we can set a forwarding rule at the proxy to redirect the Confluence login.action page to the 3rd-party provider's login._ "

summary: Ability to have the Websudo functionality working with SAML / SSO
description: "h4. Problem Definition    When using JIT provisioning in Confluence Data Center, you can't access Confluence Administration because the websudo form gives a *wrong credentials* error.  h4. Suggested Solution    I am requesting an enhancement to allow websudo to work with SAML setup as well as to allow websudo to work with other marketplace SAML plugins.  h4. Workaround    There are two possible workarounds to access Confluence Administration:   * *Disable the secure sessions*, removing the websudo form. As a side-effect, you can go straight into administration functions without confirmation that you should. This poses a security risk.   * *Set a new password for the user that was created with JIT provisioning*: JIT provisioning creates a user in Confluence Internal Directory and you can define a password for this user. As a side-effect, an administrator will have 2 passwords - the IdP password to log in Confluence and the Internal password to authenticate in secure-sessions.    h4.  "
text: Ability to have the Websudo functionality working with SAML / SSO [SEP] """h4. Problem Definition    When using JIT provisioning in Confluence Data Center, you can't access Confluence Administration because the websudo form gives a *wrong credentials* error.  h4. Suggested Solution    I am requesting an enhancement to allow websudo to work with SAML setup as well as to allow websudo to work with other marketplace SAML plugins.  h4. Workaround    There are two possible workarounds to access Confluence Administration:   * *Disable the secure sessions*, removing the websudo form. As a side-effect, you can go straight into administration functions without confirmation that you should. This poses a security risk.   * *Set a new password for the user that was created with JIT provisioning*: JIT provisioning creates a user in Confluence Internal Directory and you can define a password for this user. As a side-effect, an administrator will have 2 passwords - the IdP password to log in Confluence and the Internal password to authenticate in secure-sessions.    h4.  """

summary: Run XML export in sandbox process
description: "h3. Problem Definition  XML export of space or site consumes a lot of computing resources and can affect the performance of other operation in Confluence.   e.g. Someone runs XML export of a huge space and it causes garbage collection ending up with an overall performance degradation.     h3. summary: As a Confluence user I'd like to have a popup message for export control considerations before every attachment/file upload
description: "h3. Problem Definition      As a Confluence user, I want to be able to have a popup message displayed when a new file is uploaded for export control considerations, and currently, we don't have that option.    h3. Suggested Solution    The Popup box should display ""Have you checked with the content owner to ensure the export markings are current and accurate?""    With ""Yes"" and ""No"" options    If the user selects ""Yes"" the upload is allowed to proceed.  If the user selects ""No"" the upload is denied    h3. Why this is important    This is important so we can ensure accountability for export markings.    h3. Workaround    n/a  "

description: "h3. Issue Summary     If we are using any macro on a confluence page and that macro result shows some more results then on the bottom of the macro will get the scroll bar on the bottom of it. summary: Adding line breaks between two macros in a Confluence page won't be properly rendered as new lines
description: "h3. Issue Summary  When a macro is inserted in a page, the Confluence editor adds the following HTML tag before the macro code:      Pointing the editor cursor to the line with this code and adding line breaks will create new paragraphs with the same class tag.  When the page is published, these paragraphs are not rendered as expected in page view mode, since they are not treated as new lines.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Install a vanilla instance of Confluence.   #* This was validated on Confluence 7.4.3 and 7.7.2.   #* The issue did *not* exist on Confluence *6.6.3* nor on *6.13.4*.   #* The issue does not occur if Collaborative Editing is disabled on the affected versions.  # Create a new Space.  # Create a sample page.  # In the first line of the page add an Info Macro.  # Use the _up arrow_ keyboard button to go the previous line and add another Info Macro.  # Use the _down arrow_ keyboard button to go the next line, which will be between both macros.  # Use the _return (enter)_ keyboard button to add new lines between the two macros.   !screenshot-1.png|thumbnail!   # Publish the page.    h3. Expected Results  When the page is displayed, the new lines created between the two Info Macros are properly rendered as blank space.     h3. Actual Results  There's no new line between the two macros.  If you check the storage format it will be as below:    \\  \\   !screenshot-2.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround  When editing the page, select all lines between the macros and use the CMD+0 (CTRL+0) [keyboard shortcut|https://confluence.<USER>doc/keyboard-shortcuts-139456.html] to change them to the regular paragraph formatting.  "

summary: Confluence 7.8 EAP Feedback
description: "Atlassian says they have an ""eap"" label for the latest EAP release in dockerhub:  [https://hub.docker.com/r/atlassian/confluence-server/ 5|https://hub.docker.com/r/atlassian/confluence-server/]  _""For the latest developer (EAP) release use {{atlassian/confluence-server:eap}} . This will install our latest milestone (not supported for use in production).""_    It's not true. There is no such tag in Confluence's docker hub. We need it.    There is a ""latest"" image and we use that for the latest published version but we need an ""eap"" label so we don't have to dig through the various ""76-eap-1"", ""76-eap-2"", ... labels to find the latest EAP image.    Every time there is an EAP release we have to: 1) notice it, 2) go find out where the image is    To add to that, there is no 7.8 label with ""eap"" in it.  It looks like 7.6 is the last time Atlassian used eap in a docker image label. The docker hub image label naming is inconsistent.         Here is the community thread about this:    [https://community.developer.<USER>t/confluence-docker-images-we-need-a-generic-eap-tag/40502]     "

summary: Cursor moves automatically after section/column macro added to Blog post
description: "h3. Issue Summary    The cursor moves automatically when trying to place the cursor right after a column macro inside a section macro in a Blog post. It doesn't happen in pages.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a new Blog post   # In the editor, add a Section macro   # Inside the section macro, add a Column macro   # Try to place the cursor immediately below the Column macro    h3. Expected Results    The cursor would stay where you clicked so you can type.  h3. Actual Results    The cursor moves up automatically. See the recording below with issue reproduction and workaround.    [^Test_recording.mov]  h3. Workaround    Add some text inside the Section macro before the column macro and don't leave any empty space between the text and the Column macro and you'll be able to type below the Column macro."

summary: Prevent recursive macro execution
description: "h3. Problem Definition  Some Confluence macros could call other macros which would lead to recursive calls. Such operations are resource-extensive and could caused performance issue in Confluence.    h3. Suggested Solution  For Confluence to limit the macro's recursion depth, in order to avoid high resource utilisation and {{StackOverflowError}} in specific.    h3. Workaround   Once identified, disable the culprit apps/plugins temporarily."
text: Prevent recursive macro execution [SEP] """h3. Problem Definition  Some Confluence macros could call other macros which would lead to recursive calls. Such operations are resource-extensive and could caused performance issue in Confluence.    h3. Suggested Solution  For Confluence to limit the macro's recursion depth, in order to avoid high resource utilisation and {{StackOverflowError}} in specific.    h3. Workaround   Once identified, disable the culprit apps/plugins temporarily."""

summary: save color of table cell besides content
description: "User story: In a page property report everything is transported what a cell contains. The report lists the desired property content of a page. Often I am asked how to set background colours in the report table or how to highlight something. We used status macro for that but when we use status macros then the affected row cannot be sorted. The table as an overview helps to prioritize but there is no way to show more than the content - maybe the priority set. The native move would be to set a background color like in MS Excel. This is what we would like to achieve.  Our Request: Make it work that with the page properties also the background-color of the table cell will be shown in the property report.  Effect: the overview can contain more infos than cell values: background-colors can offer more dimensions necessary to compare or display information - which then is a better basis for decisions."

summary: Code Block Macro with 1024 characters wrapped with square bracket [] is not able to be saved
description: "h3. Issue Summary  Code Block Macro with 1024 characters wrapped with square bracket [] is not able to be saved    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Edit a page in Confluence  # Insert a Code Block Macro in the page  # Paste the text below into the Code Block Macro:    (i) There is exactly 1024 characters, including the opening and closing *square bracket*  # Save or Publish the page    h3. Expected Results  Page with the Code Block Macro can be saved without issue    h3. Actual Results  The page cannot be saved and the error message below can be seen from the pop-up:  bq. Unable to communicate with server. Saving is not possible at the moment.    The error stacktrace below is observed in the log:      h3. Information  This issue persists regardless if the Collaborative Editing is turned On or Off    h3. Workaround  Add or remove some characters so that the content in the Code Block Macro doesn't fulfill *both* of the conditions below:  * There is exactly 1024 characters in the macro content  * The content starts with _left square bracket_ ( [ ) and ends with _right square bracket_ ( ] )  "

summary: Companion app will not work if the windows user acount username contains special chracters (#, @, %, and etc))
description: "h3. Issue Summary  If the windows user account username contains special characters, Companion app will not be able to allow the user to open the file and re upload it after the user edited the file.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a local window account with username {{#test}}  # Login with the account that just created  # Try to access a Confluence page with an attachment  # Edit the attachment to launch the companion app  # An error will appear.  (i) For details, please refer to the following video.   [^Recording #3.mp4]     h3. Expected Results  User is able to edit the file and upload to Confluence    h3. Actual Results  User is not able to upload the file after edited the attachment as Companion app is not able to upload it.    The below exception will appear when user edit the attachment.      h3. Workaround  Remove the special characters for the windows account username and ensure the folder name for the user do not contains special characters as well."

summary: As a Confluence administrator I would like to configure cluster members using IP range
description: "h3. Problem Definition  On Confluence Data Center, when using tcp_ip as the cluster join method, cluster members must be configured in {{confluence.cfg.xml}} within the {{confluence.cluster.peers}} attributes.  The only possible configuration is by adding a comma-separated list of IP addresses as described in [this KB article|https://confluence.<USER>doc/change-node-discovery-from-multicast-to-tcp-ip-or-aws-792297728.html].    Confluence Data Center makes use of Hazelcast to administer the cluster.  The Hazelcast framework accepts member list configuration by IP range as described [in their documentation|https://docs.hazelcast.org/docs/3.11/manual/html-single/#discovering-members-by-tcp]:  {quote}  (...)    As shown above, you can provide IP addresses or hostnames for {{member}} elements. You can also give a range of IP addresses, such as {{192.168.1.0-7}}.  Instead of providing members line-by-line as shown above, you also have the option to use the {{members}} element and write comma-separated IP addresses, as shown below.  {{<members>192.168.1.0-7,192.168.1.21</members>}}  If you do not provide ports for the members, Hazelcast automatically tries the ports 5701, 5702 and so on.    (...)  {quote}      h3. Suggested Solution  Make it possible to configure Confluence cluster members using IP range as described in the Hazelcast documentation.     h3. Workaround   Use a comma-separated list of IP addresses as described in Confluence documentation.  "

summary: Unpublished drafts can stop pages from being moved due to already existing page titles
description: "h3. Problem  When moving a page to another Space, if one of its child pages has the same name as a page in the destination Space, the move operation will fail, as Confluence does not allow two pages with the same name to exist in a single Space, which is the expected behavior. Normally, the child page can then be renamed in order to allow the parent page to be moved.    However, this will also happen if that child page (with a matching name) is an unpublished draft, which causes an issue as:  # The unpublished draft does not show under the page tree, or on searches, which makes it difficult to locate  # Only the person who created this draft has access to it (in order to publish or delete it) - most importantly, not even Confluence admins have access to this draft ([CONFSERVER-35921|https://jira.<USER>browse/CONFSERVER-35921])    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create page ""Matching title"" in ""Dest Space""  # Create page ""Page to move"" in ""Source Space""  # Create page ""Matching title"" in ""Source Space"" as child of ""Page to move"", but don't publish it (wait for Confluence to automatically save the unpublished draft)  # Try to move page ""Page to move"" to ""Dest Space""    h3. Expected Results  The page move still shouldn't happen, as expected, since Confluence does not allow pages with duplicated names in the same Space.    However, in cases when the duplicated page is an unpublished draft, Confluence should handle this better, as this issue can't be solved by a regular user or admin (who aren't the owner of the draft).     At least, it could indicate that the duplicated page is indeed a draft, or perhaps provide the option to ignore the conflicting draft when moving the parent page.    h3. Actual Results  Confluence will just throw this error:        h3. Workaround  Deleting the unpublished draft allows the parent page to be moved successfully (however, only the owner of the draft is capable of deleting it).  "

Steps to Reproduce  # Create page """"Matching title"""" in """"Dest Space""""  # Create page """"Page to move"""" in """"Source Space""""  # Create page """"Matching title"""" in """"Source Space"""" as child of """"Page to move"""", but don't publish it (wait for Confluence to automatically save the unpublished draft)  # Try to move page """"Page to move"""" to """"Dest Space""""    h3. summary: Deleting an attachment version causes all labels to be removed from the page
description: "h3. Issue Summary    When you delete a version of an attachment attached to a page, any labels added to the page will be automatically removed.    (i) This only happens when an attachment has multiple versions and you remove one of them, leaving at least one version of the attachment still attached to the page.    If you remove an attachment with only 1 version, the bug doesn't occur.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a new page.   # Add an attachment to the page.   # Add labels to the page and save it.   # Navigate to {{...}} > {{Attachments}} and add a 2nd version of the same attachment.   # Remove any 1 of the 2 attachment versions.   # Go back to the page.    h3. Expected Results    The labels should still be intact on the page.  h3. Actual Results    All labels for that page are missing.  h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behaviour. A workaround will be added here when available.    We suggest sticking with our latest Long Term Support (LTS) Release - Confluence 7.4.4, which is not affected by this bug."

summary: Update the REST API to allow for more endpoints to match previous offerings.
description: "h3. Problem Definition    Since Confluence 5.5, previous Remote APIs, such as JSON-RPC and XML-RPC, have been deprecated and are no longer supported. However, many of the methods available through these API endpoints have not been ported over to the REST API, which is now being utilized.    This can cause issues for any users that were utilizing these older endpoints and are unable to make the switch to the REST API.    While it's technically possible for users to still use the older API endpoints,  it's not specifically stated if these endpoints will be made available in the REST API or if at all, and is also another concern.    h3. Suggested Solution    Create equivalent endpoints in the REST API to match what is available through the older Remote APIs.    h3. Workaround    Depending on the specific functions needed, the Confluence CLI can provide many of the same functionality, but it is a paid third-party add-on."

summary: Implement "/<ID>/descendant/page" Rest API endpoint on Confluence server
description: "h3. Problem Definition    At the moment, there is no method available to get all children pages from a parent page, including all levels of the tree. Only direct children are returned from the existing methods.    h3. Suggested Solution    Implement the same endpoints that are available on Confluence Cloud:    * [Get content descendants|https://developer.<USER>cloud/confluence/rest/#api-content-id-descendant-get]  * [Get content descendants by type|https://developer.<USER>cloud/confluence/rest/#api-content-id-descendant-type-get]    Both requests are available on server instances already. However, the first one only returns attachments and comments. The second one returns the following error if accessed:  * /rest/api/content/<ID>/descendant/page      h3. Why this is important    Making those methods available would greatly reduce the effort to build the full page tree using Rest API.     h3. Workaround     At the moment, the only option is to recursively navigate through the tree, one level at a time.  "

summary: Add support for Apple SSO login methods to native iOS Confluence Server app
description: "We support SSO login as long as the login that is being done inside the in-app web-view is successful. We can see that the for login to work the Apple-specific SSO {{AppleConnect}} extension is needed, which works with iOS Safari browser. The login fails because the in-app web-view is built from Apple's {{WKWebview}} which may not support AppleConnect extension.    The mobile app can solve this by using {{SFSafariViewController}} (even though it's deprecated) within the app, which will allow our Connect Extension to be invoked accordingly.     Apple documentation recommends using the in-app webview class {{ASWebAuthenticationSession}} for web based login authentication, but {{ASWebAuthenticationSession}} does not allow the extension.     This should be resolved with the new plugin functionality in iOS 13, but until then we would need to adopt {{SFSafariViewController}}."
text: Add support for Apple SSO login methods to native iOS Confluence Server app [SEP] """We support SSO login as long as the login that is being done inside the in-app web-view is successful. We can see that the for login to work the Apple-specific SSO {{AppleConnect}} extension is needed, which works with iOS Safari browser. The login fails because the in-app web-view is built from Apple's {{WKWebview}} which may not support AppleConnect extension.    The mobile app can solve this by using {{SFSafariViewController}} (even though it's deprecated) within the app, which will allow our Connect Extension to be invoked accordingly.     Apple documentation recommends using the in-app webview class {{ASWebAuthenticationSession}} for web based login authentication, but {{ASWebAuthenticationSession}} does not allow the extension.     This should be resolved with the new plugin functionality in iOS 13, but until then we would need to adopt {{SFSafariViewController}}."""

summary: Add support for Microsoft Stream to Widget Connector macro
description: "h3. Summary    As a <USER> I would like to be able to use Microsoft Stream within Confluence pages.  h3. Problem Statement    Currently, the Widget Connector macro does not support Microsoft Stream. For a user to connect with Microsoft Stream, HTML or iFrame macro must be used. This type of custom solution can be limiting for Cloud users  h3. Suggested Resolution    Add support for Microsoft Streams into the native macro in Confluence, Widget Connector. This should allow any user within the instance to use the solution.  h3. summary: REST API: Add ETag support
description: "Add ETag support for those REST API calls that do not expand macros.    Use case:   * We have pages that are reset to an updated state on a daily basis.   * In the page history, we do not want to see the idempotent updates.   * However, we want to overwrite edits made by others (persons or scripts); i.e. the script cannot skip the update if the source data has been changed.  We want to send an alert to the page maintainer if this happens.   * Retrieving the data from Confluence to do an ""is this unchanged"" check before updating risks a race condition. Also, we want to avoid triggering a flurry of alerts whenever a Confluence update returns more or different information."
text: REST API: Add ETag support [SEP] """Add ETag support for those REST API calls that do not expand macros.    Use case:   * We have pages that are reset to an updated state on a daily basis.   * In the page history, we do not want to see the idempotent updates.   * However, we want to overwrite edits made by others (persons or scripts); i.e. the script cannot skip the update if the source data has been changed.  We want to send an alert to the page maintainer if this happens.   * Retrieving the data from Confluence to do an """"is this unchanged"""" check before updating risks a race condition. Also, we want to avoid triggering a flurry of alerts whenever a Confluence update returns more or different information."""
label: design

summary: Provide REST API methods to manage users and groups
description: "h3. Summary    As a Confluence <USER>I would like to be able to manage users and groups directly from REST API endpoints. Automation and scripting of user management can be made easier with REST API.  h3. Problem statement    User and groups management is currently not possible via REST API only. Bulk operations, automation or scripting of management actions is not currently possible when using REST API. User management was previously possible with SOAP, which has been deprecated.  h3. Suggested Resolution    Provide methods akin to what as present with SOAP that would allow Admins to manage users and groups via REST API.                   _Previous Ticket Description_   As Confluence is popular tool used by many users, it should provide the API to add users to groups, similar to what Jira Cloud has."

summary: Add butler API for downloading file artifacts from datastore
description: "As we move to cloud datastores, people can no longer grab a file from a butler repository directly by browsing the file system.    We therefore need an API that allows a file artifact to be downloaded directly to a user workspace without going through a python object (ie not requiring a butler.get followed by an exposure.writeFits). The actual persisted file being retrieved.    For Butler API something like {{butler.getArtifacts(refs, exportDir, transfer=""auto"")}} should be sufficient (which would iterate through each ref, obtaining the URIs and then downloading the artifacts at those URIs).  I'm not so sure about a command line interface.    The simplest option would be for butler query-datasets to have a mode that just writes the URIs and then have a butler get-artifacts that takes URIs as inputs (rather than adding a {{\-\-download}} option to {{query-datasets}}."
text: Add butler API for downloading file artifacts from datastore [SEP] """As we move to cloud datastores, people can no longer grab a file from a butler repository directly by browsing the file system.    We therefore need an API that allows a file artifact to be downloaded directly to a user workspace without going through a python object (ie not requiring a butler.get followed by an exposure.writeFits). The actual persisted file being retrieved.    For Butler API something like {{butler.getArtifacts(refs, exportDir, transfer=""""auto"""")}} should be sufficient (which would iterate through each ref, obtaining the URIs and then downloading the artifacts at those URIs).  I'm not so sure about a command line interface.    The simplest option would be for butler query-datasets to have a mode that just writes the URIs and then have a butler get-artifacts that takes URIs as inputs (rather than adding a {{\-\-download}} option to {{query-datasets}}."""
label: design

summary: Actually make Database.sync a context manager
description: "DM-24355 was originally about making Database.sync a context manager to make it compatible with having other operations in the same transaction, but when I did the work on that ticket I thought I could make it transaction-friendly without that kind of API change.    That was only somewhat true - the new Database.sync implementation was safe in terms of rigorously guarding against inconsistencies even with concurrent writers, but instead it was subject to deadlocks.    DM-27217 includes an analysis of the problem and made a quick fix that makes the only practical deadlock problem (that we know of) impossible while making other deadlock problems much less likely.  The remaining work (described on DM-27217) was deferred to this ticket, and that involves making Database.sync a context manager, as originally planned."
text: Actually make Database.sync a context manager [SEP] """DM-24355 was originally about making Database.sync a context manager to make it compatible with having other operations in the same transaction, but when I did the work on that ticket I thought I could make it transaction-friendly without that kind of API change. DM-27217 includes an analysis of the problem and made a quick fix that makes the only practical deadlock problem (that we know of) impossible while making other deadlock problems much less likely.  The remaining work (described on DM-27217) was deferred to this ticket, and that involves making Database.sync a context manager, as originally planned."""

summary: Avoid deadlocks in Database.sync
description: "The new implementation of Database.sync introduced on DM-24355 is subject to deadlocks, as first reported [here|https://lsstc.slack.com/archives/C2JPT1KB7/p1603061206230500].    This is actually a problem with any code that uses the {{lock}} argument to {{Database.transaction}}, because that acquires a SHARED lock (in PostgreSQL) that can be owned by multiple readers, and when both readers then try to upgrade to a more exclusive lock for writing, we get a deadlock.    A partial, simple, possibly-good-enough-for-now fix is to do at a table level what we already do at a full-database level in SQLite - make explicit transaction locking EXCLUSIVE, and thus force any other processes waiting for a lock to block.  It looks like (according to the [PostgreSQL|https://www.postgresql.org/docs/current/explicit-locking.html] docs) this does not prevent concurrent reads from the same table.  This is only a partial fix because I think the exclusive lock probably needs to be acquired at the outermost transaction context in order to force blocking instead of deadlocks.  That's in daf_butler's control in the Datastore case that's causing problems now, but it's in obs_base or skymap code in other concrete {{Database.sync}} usage contexts, and since {{Registry.transaction}} is a public API we can't control usage from spreading to other external code a swell.  I think the only way to express that in terms of interfaces is to go back to the original idea of this ticket, which is to make {{Database.sync}} itself into a context manager that cannot be nested within other transactions, but which starts its own transaction context in which other operations can be nested.    Anyhow, given that the intended usage of {{Database.sync}} is primarily on small, infrequently modified tables (like those that track {{instruments}} and {{Datastore}} s), I don't think that locking is likely to be a problem, except perhaps for our usage of {{sync}} on {{exposure}} in raw ingest (but even there, let's just keep an eye on that and deal with it if it becomes a problem).  Longer-term, I there's an alternate approach to all of this that uses SERIALIZABLE transaction isolation and retries instead of explicit locking, and that may scale better to more concurrency as well as be more database-agnostic.  But that's a lot harder to map to a usable Python interface, because those retries need to include all database calls in the same transaction context, not just those invoked by {{Database.sync}}, and that means we need to remember those calls in the Python layer rather than forward them on to SQLAlchemy immediately.  DM-26302 will probably require interface changes that would be similar to the kinds of interface changes this would require (i.e. having {{Database.transaction}} return a connection-holding object that would be used to make database calls, instead of those happening on the {{Database}} itself).    I am leaning towards just using this ticket for the simple/partial fix, and deferring future API-change work to other tickets, because aside from the Datastore creation race problem (which the simple fix should address), any other problems should be both extremely rare and work-around-able via manual retries.  I'll open other tickets for future work when I close this one, if necessary."

This is actually a problem with any code that uses the {{lock}} argument to {{Database.transaction}}, because that acquires a SHARED lock (in PostgreSQL) that can be owned by multiple readers, and when both readers then try to upgrade to a more exclusive lock for writing, we get a deadlock.    A partial, simple, possibly-good-enough-for-now fix is to do at a table level what we already do at a full-database level in SQLite - make explicit transaction locking EXCLUSIVE, and thus force any other processes waiting for a lock to block.  It looks like (according to the [PostgreSQL|https://www.postgresql.org/docs/current/explicit-locking.html] docs) this does not prevent concurrent reads from the same table.  This is only a partial fix because I think the exclusive lock probably needs to be acquired at the outermost transaction context in order to force blocking instead of deadlocks.  That's in daf_butler's control in the Datastore case that's causing problems now, but it's in obs_base or skymap code in other concrete {{Database.sync}} usage contexts, and since {{Registry.transaction}} is a public API we can't control usage from spreading to other external code a swell.  I think the only way to express that in terms of interfaces is to go back to the original idea of this ticket, which is to make {{Database.sync}} itself into a context manager that cannot be nested within other transactions, but which starts its own transaction context in which other operations can be nested.    Anyhow, given that the intended usage of {{Database.sync}} is primarily on small, infrequently modified tables (like those that track {{instruments}} and {{Datastore}} s), I don't think that locking is likely to be a problem, except perhaps for our usage of {{sync}} on {{exposure}} in raw ingest (but even there, let's just keep an eye on that and deal with it if it becomes a problem).  Longer-term, I there's an alternate approach to all of this that uses SERIALIZABLE transaction isolation and retries instead of explicit locking, and that may scale better to more concurrency as well as be more database-agnostic.  But that's a lot harder to map to a usable Python interface, because those retries need to include all database calls in the same transaction context, not just those invoked by {{Database.sync}}, and that means we need to remember those calls in the Python layer rather than forward them on to SQLAlchemy immediately.  DM-26302 will probably require interface changes that would be similar to the kinds of interface changes this would require (i.e. having {{Database.transaction}} return a connection-holding object that would be used to make database calls, instead of those happening on the {{Database}} itself).    I am leaning towards just using this ticket for the simple/partial fix, and deferring future API-change work to other tickets, because aside from the Datastore creation race problem (which the simple fix should address), any other problems should be both extremely rare and work-around-able via manual retries.  I'll open other tickets for future work when I close this one, if necessary."""

summary: Correctly use the `tooManyPeaks` config option in meas_deblender and meas_extensions_scarlet
description: "The behavior for `deblend_tooManyPeaks` is wrong for both deblenders! In the docs for both config files:    https://github.com/lsst/meas_deblender/blob/master/python/lsst/meas/deblender/sourceDeblendTask.py#L92-L94    {code:python}  maxNumberOfPeaks = pexConfig.Field(dtype=int, default=0,                                         doc=(""Only deblend the brightest maxNumberOfPeaks peaks in the parent""                                              "" (<= 0: unlimited)""))      So there is no actual check to skip this if `config.maxNumberOfPeaks <= 0`! It also appears that I implemented the same logic in mes:    https://github.com/lsst/meas_extensions_scarlet/blob/master/python/lsst/meas/extensions/scarlet/scarletDeblendTask.py#L595-L598    {code:python}  if len(peaks) > self.config.maxNumberOfPeaks:                  src.set(self.tooManyPeaksKey, True)                  msg = 'Parent {0}: Too many peaks, using the first {1} peaks'                  self.log.trace(msg.format(int(src.getId()), self.config.maxNumberOfPeaks))  {code}    So for mes, with the default `config.maxNumberOfPeaks == 0` it flags every parent as having too many peaks (I checked the RC run for 9813 and confirmed that this is the case). But it doesn't actually skip the blend, it just carries on as if nothing happened. It should do the same thing for meas_deblender, except that somewhere in the hsc config files there must be a value set for `maxNumberOfPeaks`, otherwise every parent with any children would be flagged with having too many peaks, just like for mes.    So this ticket is to check that when the user specifies the default (`maxNumberOfPeaks=0`) that the `tooManyPeaksKey` is not set."
text: Correctly use the `tooManyPeaks` config option in meas_deblender and meas_extensions_scarlet [SEP] """The behavior for `deblend_tooManyPeaks` is wrong for both deblenders! In the docs for both config files:    https://github.com/lsst/meas_deblender/blob/master/python/lsst/meas/deblender/sourceDeblendTask.py#L92-L94    {code:python}  maxNumberOfPeaks = pexConfig.Field(dtype=int, default=0,                                         doc=(""""Only deblend the brightest maxNumberOfPeaks peaks in the parent""""                                              """" (<= 0: unlimited)""""))      So there is no actual check to skip this if `config.maxNumberOfPeaks <= 0`! It also appears that I implemented the same logic in mes:    https://github.com/lsst/meas_extensions_scarlet/blob/master/python/lsst/meas/extensions/scarlet/scarletDeblendTask.py#L595-L598    {code:python}  if len(peaks) > self.config.maxNumberOfPeaks:                  src.set(self.tooManyPeaksKey, True)                  msg = 'Parent {0}: Too many peaks, using the first {1} peaks'                  self.log.trace(msg.format(int(src.getId()), self.config.maxNumberOfPeaks))  {code}    So for mes, with the default `config.maxNumberOfPeaks == 0` it flags every parent as having too many peaks (I checked the RC run for 9813 and confirmed that this is the case). But it doesn't actually skip the blend, it just carries on as if nothing happened. It should do the same thing for meas_deblender, except that somewhere in the hsc config files there must be a value set for `maxNumberOfPeaks`, otherwise every parent with any children would be flagged with having too many peaks, just like for mes.    So this ticket is to check that when the user specifies the default (`maxNumberOfPeaks=0`) that the `tooManyPeaksKey` is not set."""
label: non-design

summary: Make meas_deblender and meas_extensions_scarlet catalogs consistent
description: "It is currently difficult to match results from meas-deblender and meas_extensions_scarlet. Both deblenders start with the same ""mergeDet"" catalog of parents, where each parent has a peak table that lists the sources that need to be deblended. Unfortunately the peak ID's are not unique, so propagating them will not help, however the peak coordinates are unique for peaks in the same tract (even across bands). In meas_extensions_scarlet we already propagate the peak x and y positions, so if we do the same for meas_deblender and persist those columns into ""deepCoadd_forced_src"" then we should be able to make a 1-1 match between sources in meas_extensions_scarlet and meas_deblender."
text: Make meas_deblender and meas_extensions_scarlet catalogs consistent [SEP] """It is currently difficult to match results from meas-deblender and meas_extensions_scarlet. Both deblenders start with the same """"mergeDet"""" catalog of parents, where each parent has a peak table that lists the sources that need to be deblended. Unfortunately the peak ID's are not unique, so propagating them will not help, however the peak coordinates are unique for peaks in the same tract (even across bands). In meas_extensions_scarlet we already propagate the peak x and y positions, so if we do the same for meas_deblender and persist those columns into """"deepCoadd_forced_src"""" then we should be able to make a 1-1 match between sources in meas_extensions_scarlet and meas_deblender."""

summary: NaNs in measurePhotonTransferCurve.py causing failures
description: "When running the PTC analysis on BOT run 12606, many amps failed to return valid PTC curves.  I traced this to saturated images in the flat pairs, which caused NaNs in the mean/variance values.  When the code runs the _getInitialGoodPoints routine, the medianRatio parameter becomes NaN, and then all points fail.  I was able to fix this by changing the medianRatio from np.median to np.nanmedian, and then the PTC curves ran OK, but then the plotPtc.py routine failed to plot the PTCs.  As a workaround, I just eliminated the saturated flat pairs from the input deck, but long term the code needs to be robust to saturated inputs.  FWIW, I swear that this problem was not there a few weeks ago."

label: non-design

summary: Standardize aliases on Gen 2 Exposure get
description: "RFC-730 proposes a new filter system that will support only standardized names for each band or filter, but no aliases. While new aliases will no longer be introduced after DM-27167, old files may still use them in Gen 2 (Gen 3 will internally ensure that all names are standardized). To ensure that algorithmic code can safely assume standardized names in {{FilterLabel}} regardless of whether an input Exposure was loaded from Gen 2 or Gen 3, standardize the filter names in an Exposure when loading them from Gen 2.    Note that there is no standardization problem when using {{Filter}}; the problem will only emerge after {{Exposure}} starts using {{FilterLabel}} as the primary API."
text: Standardize aliases on Gen 2 Exposure get [SEP] """RFC-730 proposes a new filter system that will support only standardized names for each band or filter, but no aliases. To ensure that algorithmic code can safely assume standardized names in {{FilterLabel}} regardless of whether an input Exposure was loaded from Gen 2 or Gen 3, standardize the filter names in an Exposure when loading them from Gen 2.    Note that there is no standardization problem when using {{Filter}}; the problem will only emerge after {{Exposure}} starts using {{FilterLabel}} as the primary API."""

summary: Phase out use of FilterProperty
description: "RFC-730 proposes a filter system that does not contain any metadata other than the filter names. This particularly includes wavelength information.    Search for code that refers to the {{FilterProperty}} class or {{Filter::getFilterProperty}}, and rewrite it to depend on external sources of the same information (e.g., transmission curves for wavelength information). I am recommending that this be done *before* {{FilterLabel}} is added to {{Exposure}} (and therefore before {{FilterProperty}} can be deprecated) so that any lessons learned can be incorporated into the {{FilterLabel}} design."

summary: Stop using filter alias names in DM stack
description: "RFC-730 proposes a new filter system that will support only standardized names for each filter, but no aliases. To prepare for the new system, ensure that we are no longer introducing aliases into the codebase (in particular, as the primary name of old-style filters).    Proposed algorithm:  # Go through the filter definition file of every {{obs}} package, and make a list of names that appear in either the {{afw_name}} or {{alias}} field of a {{FilterDefinition}}, along with their corresponding {{physical_filter}}.  # Search the entire {{lsst}} and {{lsst-dm}} for hardcoded references to these names, except for those in {{FilterDefinition}} itself and calls to {{Filter::define}}, {{Filter::defineAlias}}, and {{image.utils.defineFilter}}. Calls to the {{Filter}} constructor *are* covered by the search.  # Replace each reference to an afw_name or alias with a reference to the corresponding physical_filter.    It is possible that step 2 will find no matches."

To prepare for the new system, ensure that we are no longer introducing aliases into the codebase (in particular, as the primary name of old-style filters).    Proposed algorithm:  # Go through the filter definition file of every {{obs}} package, and make a list of names that appear in either the {{afw_name}} or {{alias}} field of a {{FilterDefinition}}, along with their corresponding {{physical_filter}}.  # Search the entire {{lsst}} and {{lsst-dm}} for hardcoded references to these names, except for those in {{FilterDefinition}} itself and calls to {{Filter::define}}, {{Filter::defineAlias}}, and {{image.utils.defineFilter}}. Calls to the {{Filter}} constructor *are* covered by the search.  # Replace each reference to an afw_name or alias with a reference to the corresponding physical_filter.    It is possible that step 2 will find no matches."""

summary: Add task to compute and persist VisitSummary tables
description: "Currently, the only way to access the individual visit metadata, WCS information, photoCalib, PSF size, etc, is to read in all of the components from over 100 individual {{calexp}} files (for HSC and LSSTCam).  This is a performance bottleneck for {{fgcmcal}}, {{jointcal}}, and {{pipe_analysis/visitAnalysis}} to name a few tasks.  This ticket is to add a task to {{pipe_tasks/postprocess.py}} called {{ConsolidateVisitSummaryTask}} (supporting both Gen2 and Gen3) that will consolidate all of the summary info from the constituent detectors into one {{afw}} {{ExposureCatalog}}.  This task can be more easily parallelized by the workflow manager, while it reduces the i/o burden (and input logic) on the consumers of this product significantly.  Simple tests show a reduction in time from 30-40 seconds per visit on HSC data to less than a second to retrieve the {{VisitSummary}}.  "

summary: Usability improvement suggestions for butler collection commands
description: "I gave {{butler prune-collection}} and {{butler query-collections}} a try for the first time today when prepping for an Ops Bootcamp talk, and found some rough edges we should smooth and some missing functionality we should figure out how to support:   * My first thought was to do ""butler prune-collection REPO <collection>"", not ""butler prune-collection REPO --collection <collection>"", and given that the collection option is not optional, I think it probably should be a positional argument   * The error messages printed when trying to remove a RUN-type collection without --purge or passing --purge without --unstore reference the Python Registry API, and hence offer confusing suggestions about how to fix the problem from the command-line.   * It'll be common for people to want to delete either collections matching a pattern or the child collections of a CHAINED collection.  We may want to just make this into ""prune-collections"" (i.e. plural) and accept the same kind of GLOB and many of the other arguments of query-collections, and start with a query for what to delete.  A catch here is that if the query includes both RUN and non-RUN collections, we need to be careful about passing purge=True down appropriately (and update the command-line docs accordingly).   * It'd be nice to have a way to print collection types in {{query-collections}} (these can be obtained by calling {{Registry.getCollectionType}} on the collection name).   * It'd be nice to have a way to print CHAINED collection definitions (these can be obtained by calling {{Registry.getCollectionChain}} on the collection name).  It'd also be nice to have a way to set them ({{Registry.setCollectionChain}}), too, though, so maybe these should be a new pair of subcommands rather than making the former part of {{query-collections}}.  I'm not sure.   * I don't think we're gaining much from using YAML to format the output of {{query-collections}}, as it's always just a flat list, so the ""collections:"" heading and the ""-"" before each line are effectively just noise.  The last two bullets undercut that argument a bit, but I think if printing/setting CHAINED definitions is its own subcommand, optionally printing collection types still doesn't represent enough structure that YAML makes sense."
text: Usability improvement suggestions for butler collection commands [SEP] """I gave {{butler prune-collection}} and {{butler query-collections}} a try for the first time today when prepping for an Ops Bootcamp talk, and found some rough edges we should smooth and some missing functionality we should figure out how to support:   * My first thought was to do """"butler prune-collection REPO <collection>"""", not """"butler prune-collection REPO --collection <collection>"""", and given that the collection option is not optional, I think it probably should be a positional argument   * The error messages printed when trying to remove a RUN-type collection without --purge or passing --purge without --unstore reference the Python Registry API, and hence offer confusing suggestions about how to fix the problem from the command-line.   * It'll be common for people to want to delete either collections matching a pattern or the child collections of a CHAINED collection.  We may want to just make this into """"prune-collections"""" (i.e. plural) and accept the same kind of GLOB and many of the other arguments of query-collections, and start with a query for what to delete.  A catch here is that if the query includes both RUN and non-RUN collections, we need to be careful about passing purge=True down appropriately (and update the command-line docs accordingly).   * It'd be nice to have a way to print collection types in {{query-collections}} (these can be obtained by calling {{Registry.getCollectionType}} on the collection name).   * It'd be nice to have a way to print CHAINED collection definitions (these can be obtained by calling {{Registry.getCollectionChain}} on the collection name).  It'd also be nice to have a way to set them ({{Registry.setCollectionChain}}), too, though, so maybe these should be a new pair of subcommands rather than making the former part of {{query-collections}}.  I'm not sure.   * I don't think we're gaining much from using YAML to format the output of {{query-collections}}, as it's always just a flat list, so the """"collections:"""" heading and the """"-"""" before each line are effectively just noise.  The last two bullets undercut that argument a bit, but I think if printing/setting CHAINED definitions is its own subcommand, optionally printing collection types still doesn't represent enough structure that YAML makes sense."""
label: design

summary: Defaulting the instrument in registry
description: "Unlike gen2, gen3 can work with multiple instruments and so therefore generally requires that an instrument be specified in a dataId or where clause when selecting datasets.    This requirement to always specify an ""unnecessary"" instrument has generated some negative feedback.    The problem with globally declaring that a registry only has one instrument is that if you ever change your mind (say adding LATISS data to an LSSTCam registry) then all the code that assume the instrument would default now breaks.    One proposal is to treat instrument as a special property (see also DM-27152) and allow for instruments to be associated with specific collections. If you are using collection ""HSC/raw/all"" then it is likely that a single instrument is relevant. If a collection happens to include data from multiple instruments it would not be associated with a defaulted instrument but that would be rare and in most cases would have been deliberately configured to be like that."
text: Defaulting the instrument in registry [SEP] """Unlike gen2, gen3 can work with multiple instruments and so therefore generally requires that an instrument be specified in a dataId or where clause when selecting datasets.    This requirement to always specify an """"unnecessary"""" instrument has generated some negative feedback.    The problem with globally declaring that a registry only has one instrument is that if you ever change your mind (say adding LATISS data to an LSSTCam registry) then all the code that assume the instrument would default now breaks.    One proposal is to treat instrument as a special property (see also DM-27152) and allow for instruments to be associated with specific collections. If you are using collection """"HSC/raw/all"""" then it is likely that a single instrument is relevant. If a collection happens to include data from multiple instruments it would not be associated with a defaulted instrument but that would be rare and in most cases would have been deliberately configured to be like that."""
label: design

summary: Allow instrument-specific variants in dataIds
description: "The commissioning team refer to individual exposures by the ""dayobs"", sequence number for the night, and controller (OCS vs camera) but these concepts are not known to butler registry since they are effectively instrument-specific. Butler registry knows either the exposure integer ID or the exposure ""observation identifier"" string.    Rather than modify registry to have duplicate information (or go down the path of gen2 instrument-specific exposure registries) one option is to have a dataId preprocessor that can convert the instrument-specific concepts to something known by registry.    For the specific case of LSSTCam there is a standard mapping from the dayobs,seqnum,controller tuple to the exposure integer ID so this does not require access to the exposure record.  "

Rather than modify registry to have duplicate information (or go down the path of gen2 instrument-specific exposure registries) one option is to have a dataId preprocessor that can convert the instrument-specific concepts to something known by registry.    For the specific case of LSSTCam there is a standard mapping from the dayobs,seqnum,controller tuple to the exposure integer ID so this does not require access to the exposure record.  """
label: design

summary: Allow record information such as detector name in dataIds
description: "In gen2 people can specify the detector by name in a dataId.    This only seems to work for query expressions at the moment (where you can say things like exposure.name or detector.full_name).    It would be nice if a user could use the detector.full_name=R01_S10 or exposure.name=AT_O_20201002_00043 in a dataId without always requiring the integer forms. Registry does know them and in these two examples they are guaranteed to uniquely define the exposure or detector.    Aside: detector doesn't have detector.name at all so there is an inconsistency there with other dimensions."

summary: Add per-camera umbrella collections and levels of indirection for versioned root datasets
description: "We've talked in the past about adding umbrella CHAINED collections for each instrument to gather raws, calibs, refcats, masks, etc.  It's time to do that now.    We should also make sure any RUN-type collections created by ConvertRepoTask are truly going to be valid forever or hidden behind CHAINED collection links.  For example, it's not appropriate to put the particular version of the HSC bright object masks we find in repos today as ""HSC/masks""; we probably want them to go in ""HSC/masks/<version>"" and have an ""HSC/masks"" CHAINED collection referencing that.    Here's a more complete proposal: https://confluence.lsstcorp.org/display/DM/Standard+Collections+and+Collection+Naming+Conventions"

summary: Fix pickling of Task with parent
description: "In DM-27097 we are having difficulties with multiprocessing because in order to pickle RawIngestTask in that context we need to pickle the parent task. In general, ensuring that every parent task is pickleable is hard to do and it turns out that a task only needs its parent task to do some minor adjustments to internal configuration and does not use the parentTask at all other than when pickling.    Change pickling in Task so that it pickles the things it needs from the parent task but does not pickle the parent task."
text: Fix pickling of Task with parent [SEP] """In DM-27097 we are having difficulties with multiprocessing because in order to pickle RawIngestTask in that context we need to pickle the parent task. summary: Add dummy pipeline to ap_verify testing
description: "Mock-based testing of {{pipeline_driver.py}} has run into several problems, including over-dependence on {{ctrl_mpexec}}. It would be cleaner to create  a mock pipeline whose tasks do no processing besides creating output datasets. Implement such a pipeline and use it in tests that currently try to mock the pipeline, its executor, or {{pipeline_driver}}.    Note that {{ap_verify}} currently does some run-time configuration of the AP pipeline (e.g., database location), so the mock pipeline needs to have at least some of the same configuration hooks as the real pipeline. Also, since tasks must be importable to be included in a pipeline, they will need to go somewhere in the main python directory of {{ap_verify}}."

summary: Migrate to Fastly's built-in configuration features
description: "Since our initial launch of LSST the Docs in 2016, Fastly has incorporated built-in configuration options for several of the things that we originally needed to add custom configuration for. There are also configuration aspects that are no longer relevant.    This ticket is to make these configuration changes. Note that DM-24955 is where we're fixing directly index support with query strings.   # Switch `Redirect to HTTPS` request setting to the built-in `Force TLS and enable HSTS`. Also, drop the ""If not TLS"" condition to ""Reset original host for HTTPS redirect"" [https://docs.fastly.com/en/guides/enabling-hsts-through-fastly]   # Drop the RTD redirect Content header settings.   # Stop using `900` range for internal states. Instead, use `600`. Based on Howell's blog post from [McDowell|https://www.integralist.co.uk/posts/fastly-varnish/].   # Change headers like http.location to `http.Location`.   # Fastly's built-in error page support (e.g. 404).   # Fastly's built-in gzip support"

Also, drop the """"If not TLS"""" condition to """"Reset original host for HTTPS redirect"""" [https://docs.fastly.com/en/guides/enabling-hsts-through-fastly]   # Drop the RTD redirect Content header settings.   # Stop using `900` range for internal states. summary: REST API for building the secondary index in Qserv
description: "In the current implementation of the new Ingest system the _secondary index_ of a database is normally populated in the end of the _super-transactions_. The algorithm harvests contributions from the corresponding MySQL _partitions_ of the director table's chunks and loads them into the index table at Qserv ""czar""'s database . Depending on the amount of data ingested into that table during a transaction the index building operation may noticeably slow down the commit phase of the transaction. After gaining the first experience with using the new Ingest system it has become apparent that some workflows may benefit from having the _secondary index_ construction as an explicit stage deferred till a moment when all director table's contributions were loaded into Qserv.    Hence, a goal of the current development is to refine and extend the REST API of the Master Replication Controller to allow more options in building the index. Specifically, the following changes are proposed:  * add an optional parameter *build_secondary_index=<val>* to existing database creation service to indicate an intent to defer the index creation till the very last stage. The default value of the parameter will be 0 which would preserve the current behavior.  * extend the database information structure DatabaseInfo with the corresponding flag.  * modify the transaction commit service not to make contributions into the index if the deferred index construction for the database was requested.  * add a new POST service for constructing indexes as a separate operation to be performed before (or with extra controls) after publishing a database. The service should also allow an optional parameter to allow rebuilding the index from scratch.  * update the documentation on the Ingest System accordingly.  * evaluate if the version of the Ingest API needs to be incremented as well."

summary: Extend the Replication/Ingest framework and REST API with algorithms and tools for indexing catalogs
description: "The current implementation of the Replication/Ingest doesn't provide any mechanism for managing (inspecting, creating or dropping) indexes on the data tables within Qserv. Only a few essential (for Qserv to function) indexes get automatically created. A lack of indexes may affect a performance of many user queries. Hence a goal of this development is to add the following capabilities to the system:  * extending the Replication Framework with a set of _request_ and _job_ classes for managing indexes at workers.  * adding a command-line version of the Replication _controller_ tool for managing the indexes  * extending the REST services of the *Master Replication Controller* with the index management services    Other notes:  * the REST services need to be protected with the _auth_key_ mechanism as explained in: https://jira.lsstcorp.org/browse/DM-22745  * it may be necessary to consider putting a cap on the number of indexes which were created by the system due to disk space constraints at a particular Qserv deployment. For example, the system may need to evaluate the amount of the available disk space on the coresponding file system (at Qserv workers) before attempting to create a new index.  "
text: Extend the Replication/Ingest framework and REST API with algorithms and tools for indexing catalogs [SEP] """The current implementation of the Replication/Ingest doesn't provide any mechanism for managing (inspecting, creating or dropping) indexes on the data tables within Qserv. Only a few essential (for Qserv to function) indexes get automatically created. A lack of indexes may affect a performance of many user queries. Hence a goal of this development is to add the following capabilities to the system:  * extending the Replication Framework with a set of _request_ and _job_ classes for managing indexes at workers.  * adding a command-line version of the Replication _controller_ tool for managing the indexes  * extending the REST services of the *Master Replication Controller* with the index management services    Other notes:  * the REST services need to be protected with the _auth_key_ mechanism as explained in: https://jira.lsstcorp.org/browse/DM-22745  * it may be necessary to consider putting a cap on the number of indexes which were created by the system due to disk space constraints at a particular Qserv deployment. For example, the system may need to evaluate the amount of the available disk space on the coresponding file system (at Qserv workers) before attempting to create a new index.  """
label: design

summary: Implement a method to have Jenkins pipeline jobs trigger other jobs or pipelines
description: "We want to have an automated process that starts with an interface change (XML update) and runs all the jobs necessary to create libraries and run the application builds.  These application builds will run through the unit tests and ensure the interface change hasn't broken anything.  Currently, we have these jobs working individually, but we want them to run in series and automatically.  So the first job, when successfully complete, will trigger the next and so on.  Having one giant pipeline job seems unwieldy, so we need a way for a Jenkins pipeline job to trigger another pipeline or freestyle job, passing necessary parameters."

summary: Prototype a confluent schema registry instead of building our own registry
description: "Today, we manage schema versions by committing them in the sample-avro-alert repository, and we expect them to be on the local filesystem of the producer or consumer. We load all schemas into memory using a custom SchemaRegistry class in sample-avro-alert's Python utility code. alert_stream's code relies on this.    This setup probably won't survive in production. It depends too much on synchronizing consumers and producers by managing their local state - we'd have to send out an email telling people to please refresh files on their machines in order to take advantage of a schema update.    In addition, our SchemaRegistry implementation is somewhat buggy: it doesn't canonicalize schemas, so minor whitespace and documentation changes would be breaking (this is DM-24293).    One alternative seems to be the [Confluent Schema Registry|https://www.confluent.io/confluent-schema-registry/]. This is in use today at LSST by SQuARE; the kafkit project provides access to the schema registry API directly, and the confluent_kafka consumer/producer library supports transparent ser/de if a schema registry is available. It would handle canonicalization for us, as well as fingerprinting of schemas.    Output of this work would be a working set of Docker containers that runs a Schema Registry alongside a Kafka broker, and Python code that produces and consumes the alert stream using that data. A good demo would be to show an update to the schema. Output should also include a DMTN summarizing the work and the result."
text: Prototype a confluent schema registry instead of building our own registry [SEP] """Today, we manage schema versions by committing them in the sample-avro-alert repository, and we expect them to be on the local filesystem of the producer or consumer. We load all schemas into memory using a custom SchemaRegistry class in sample-avro-alert's Python utility code. alert_stream's code relies on this.    This setup probably won't survive in production. It depends too much on synchronizing consumers and producers by managing their local state - we'd have to send out an email telling people to please refresh files on their machines in order to take advantage of a schema update.    In addition, our SchemaRegistry implementation is somewhat buggy: it doesn't canonicalize schemas, so minor whitespace and documentation changes would be breaking (this is DM-24293).    One alternative seems to be the [Confluent Schema Registry|https://www.confluent.io/confluent-schema-registry/]. This is in use today at LSST by SQuARE; the kafkit project provides access to the schema registry API directly, and the confluent_kafka consumer/producer library supports transparent ser/de if a schema registry is available. It would handle canonicalization for us, as well as fingerprinting of schemas.    Output of this work would be a working set of Docker containers that runs a Schema Registry alongside a Kafka broker, and Python code that produces and consumes the alert stream using that data. A good demo would be to show an update to the schema. Output should also include a DMTN summarizing the work and the result."""

summary: Generate documentation on the alert distribution message schema
description: "This is a nice-to-have: it would be great to generate and distribute documentation on the avro schema we are providing for alert distribution.    [avrodoc|https://github.com/ept/avrodoc] can do this, but the tool hasn't been maintained in 7 years; it took some banging to get it to generate anything on LSST's schemas. Still, its output is pretty useful (it's attached). We could use avrodoc, or write our own thing. Writing our own thing is probably a lot more work, but might be less likely to suddenly break. Since documentation changes slowly, I think suddenly breaking wouldn't be noticed, really."

summary: JavaScript.RequestRegistration - File Order Issue
description: "We're running into issues with the core implementation of JavaScript.RequestRegistration.  We have installed a newer version of Knockout.js thus creating a newer entry in the JavaScriptLibraries folder. Due to the default nature of how these are registered - it's the file order priority that screws this up.      Example:  Knockout.js 3.3.0 is installed.  Also added are Kncokout.Mapping 2.4.1, and we install Knockout.validation.js 2.0.3  During development - we upgrade to knockout.js 3.4.1 - install the extension as per the rules.  Because Mapping and validation are not also updated (they don't need to be) we run into issues.    It's in the JavaScript.cs file ([located here|https://github.com/dnnsoftware/Dnn.Platform/blob/development/DNN%20Platform/Library/Framework/JavaScriptLibraries/JavaScript.cs]) at lines 449 - the private static in GetFileOrder function.    If the javascript library isn't one of the default core (jquery, hoverintent, etc) - it gets the DefaultPriority (100) PLUS the JavaScriptLibrary table's Package ID.    Thus - but upgrading our knockout version - it has a Primary key ID of 165 (plus 100 = 265) which is higher than the validation package id.    This breaks our custom modules even though we are following DNN best practices.  The work around is to change the knockout.js extension to load in the HEAD But this is not the preferred solution!!!    It would be ideal if we could pass a priority into the JavaScript.RequestRegistration call - that takes the overall highest priority(?) or something similar.  Or best if because the dependencies are known by knockout.mapping and knockout.validation - that it would load knockout.js first.    Here's a screenshot of the JavaScriptLibraries table: https://www.screencast.com/t/zbi1ISQE"
text: JavaScript.RequestRegistration - File Order Issue [SEP] """We're running into issues with the core implementation of JavaScript.RequestRegistration.  We have installed a newer version of Knockout.js thus creating a newer entry in the JavaScriptLibraries folder. Due to the default nature of how these are registered - it's the file order priority that screws this up.      Example:  Knockout.js 3.3.0 is installed.  Also added are Kncokout.Mapping 2.4.1, and we install Knockout.validation.js 2.0.3  During development - we upgrade to knockout.js 3.4.1 - install the extension as per the rules.  Because Mapping and validation are not also updated (they don't need to be) we run into issues.    It's in the JavaScript.cs file ([located here|https://github.com/dnnsoftware/Dnn.Platform/blob/development/DNN%20Platform/Library/Framework/JavaScriptLibraries/JavaScript.cs]) at lines 449 - the private static in GetFileOrder function.    If the javascript library isn't one of the default core (jquery, hoverintent, etc) - it gets the DefaultPriority (100) PLUS the JavaScriptLibrary table's Package ID.    Thus - but upgrading our knockout version - it has a Primary key ID of 165 (plus 100 = 265) which is higher than the validation package id.    This breaks our custom modules even though we are following DNN best practices.  The work around is to change the knockout.js extension to load in the HEAD But this is not the preferred solution!!!    It would be ideal if we could pass a priority into the JavaScript.RequestRegistration call - that takes the overall highest priority(?) or something similar.  Or best if because the dependencies are known by knockout.mapping and knockout.validation - that it would load knockout.js first.    Here's a screenshot of the JavaScriptLibraries table: https://www.screencast.com/t/zbi1ISQE"""

summary: Persona Bar iFrame disables page when edit is toggled
description: "After the Persona Bar Edit button is toggled, clicking on links or buttons in the main content area and the lower part of the PB has no effect. This is because the PB iFrame is resized on clicking the Edit button. An attribute ""width:100%"" is applied to the iFrame which extends it over the page content area.    *Steps to reproduce*  # Create a new DNN9 installation  # Log in as host  # In development tools observe the width attribute on the Persona Bar iFrame  # On the home page, click the Persona Bar Edit button  # Move the mouse over the PB Close button and click  # Observe application response  # Move the mouse over any other link on the page other than in the PB left panel and click  # Observe application response  # In development tools observe the width attribute on the Persona Bar iFrame    *Expected results*  3. width:80px  6. Button changes appearance as the mouse passes over; on click, the panel closes  8. As 6  9. width:80px    *Actual results*  3. width:80px  6. Button does not respond  8. As 6  9. width:100%  "

summary: Cannot create a new page from Pages > Add a New Page if Languages/Localization is enabled
description: "This Issue is a follow up to DNN-8487 (and my copy of it DNN-10722)    *Problem*: In a multi language environment adding pages does not always work.  *Reproduction*:  1) Create a DNN environment with TWO Languages (eg. EN / FR)  2) Create a parent page in two languages (eg. EN / FR)  3) Create a Child Page with two languages (EN / FR)  4) Now ADD a third Language to the portal (eg. DE) with ""Add missing languages"" - Button  5) go to the CHILD page and try to add missing language --> Page reloads, language is not added, error as in  DNN-8487    *Workaround / Fix:*  After above Step 4:  4a) Go to PARENT Page, edit localisation and add missing translation  5) go to the CHILD page, edit localisation and add missing translation  6) CHILD Page is successfully created in 3rd language"
text: Cannot create a new page from Pages > Add a New Page if Languages/Localization is enabled  [SEP] """This Issue is a follow up to DNN-8487 (and my copy of it DNN-10722)    *Problem*: In a multi language environment adding pages does not always work.  *Reproduction*:  1) Create a DNN environment with TWO Languages (eg. EN / FR)  2) Create a parent page in two languages (eg. EN / FR)  3) Create a Child Page with two languages (EN / FR)  4) Now ADD a third Language to the portal (eg. DE) with """"Add missing languages"""" - Button  5) go to the CHILD page and try to add missing language --> Page reloads, language is not added, error as in  DNN-8487    *Workaround / Fix:*  After above Step 4:  4a) Go to PARENT Page, edit localisation and add missing translation  5) go to the CHILD page, edit localisation and add missing translation  6) CHILD Page is successfully created in 3rd language"""
