summary: Call ACLProvider.CheckACLNoChannel directly when there is no channel
description: "This is a follow up to the PR review discussion:  [https://github.com/hyperledger/fabric/pull/1791#discussion_r475417325]    In the referred PR, we added a CheckACLNoChannel func to ACLProvider interface. With this interface update, it may cause confusion due to inconsistent calling pattern:   * if you work with SignedProposal and have no channel , existing code calls the CheckACL with channelID as an empty string   * if you work with SignedData and have no channel, new code calls CheckACLNoChannel     We should update the existing code to explicitly call CheckACLNoChannel when there is no channel.     "

summary: Flake in registrar_test.go reveals bug in block_puller.go
description: "Both the registrar_test.go and the block_puller.go factory do not assign value to     ```go  cluster.BlockPuller{     MaxPullBlockRetries     ...  }  ```    which may cause an endless loop [here|https://github.com/hyperledger/fabric/blob/3b62ca4080703c63e04c8d20b2c8ffe8fc79add3/orderer/common/cluster/deliver.go#L143]    Solution is to initialize a non zero value.  In addition, when a follower is started in registrar_test.go, it must be stopped, otherwise the go-routine will run until  the process exits."

summary: Data privacy within a peer which has different users
description: "In hyperledger fabric, consider peer0.org1 contains 5 users. How can we maintain data privacy among these users?    Example: peer0.org1 has user1,  user2, user3, user4 and user5. All the users don't want to share their the data with others. If one of the user, took ledger backup of the peer, then that user will get all the data present in the ledger. In this case, how to maintain data privacy?     "

summary: Allow looser matching on orderer TLS consenter certs
description: "The orderer's cluster service identifies other consenters in the cluster by X.509 TLS certificate.  It performs a byte for byte comparison of the certificate presented to the TLS connection compared to the TLS certificate it is aware of from the channel configuration.    Unfortunately, this makes rotating certificates quite challenging, especially when dealing with benign expiration, rather than key compromise. Because an orderer may be a member of many channels, in order to rotate an orderer's TLS certificate, it must usually be stopped, have the TLS certificate changed, change all channels to recognize the new TLS certificate, and then be restarted. For an orderer which is a member of dozens or hundreds of channels, the channel config updates could take quite a while, and be quite complex to perform (requiring gathering signatures from multiple parties etc.).    In the case where the private key of the TLS certificate is still secure, but only the issued certificate is expiring, it should be possible to issue a new TLS certificate based on the same public/private keypair, but with a new valid expiration date. In this case, we can avoid the channel configuration updates and associated complexity by relaxing the connection validation to inspect only the public key of the certificate, and not the entire set of certificate bytes. This should still be safe as only the owner of the identity can create (and use) a new certificate with the corresponding public key bytes, and it will allow the orderer to simply be restarted with the new certificate, rather than requiring channel config updates across all channels.    Acceptance criteria: We should be able to start a Raft based network (system channel only is fine), stop one node, renew its certificate with the same private key, and restart that node. The node should resume consenting as part of the consenter set without channel reconfiguration."

Because an orderer may be a member of many channels, in order to rotate an orderer's TLS certificate, it must usually be stopped, have the TLS certificate changed, change all channels to recognize the new TLS certificate, and then be restarted. For an orderer which is a member of dozens or hundreds of channels, the channel config updates could take quite a while, and be quite complex to perform (requiring gathering signatures from multiple parties etc.).    In the case where the private key of the TLS certificate is still secure, but only the issued certificate is expiring, it should be possible to issue a new TLS certificate based on the same public/private keypair, but with a new valid expiration date. In this case, we can avoid the channel configuration updates and associated complexity by relaxing the connection validation to inspect only the public key of the certificate, and not the entire set of certificate bytes. This should still be safe as only the owner of the identity can create (and use) a new certificate with the corresponding public key bytes, and it will allow the orderer to simply be restarted with the new certificate, rather than requiring channel config updates across all channels.    Acceptance criteria: We should be able to start a Raft based network (system channel only is fine), stop one node, renew its certificate with the same private key, and restart that node. The node should resume consenting as part of the consenter set without channel reconfiguration."""

summary: DevMode - Modify chaincode endorsement to support DevMode
description: "In order to support DevMode with new lifecycle, chaincode endorsement info need to be slightly modified.     As when developer running the DevMode, chaincode will be launched by developer, and it will not be installed and launched by peer. When `CachedChaincodeInfo`, `InstallInfo` will be missing when DevMode is enabled, and this behavior need to be handled and returned correctly.          Acceptance:   * Have correct behavior for `CachedChaincodeInfo` and `ChaincodeEndorsementInfo` when DevMode is enabled.   * Have unit test coverage to test behavior of `ChaincodeEndorsementInfoSource` when DevMode is enabled."

summary: Raft active nodes flakey when switching follower to member
description: "At times, the active nodes reported by Raft are temporarily unstable. I've noticed this while working on an integration test where I add an OSN as a follower, wait for it to catch up, and then add it to the consenters set.     When the active nodes are incorrectly reported, I see the following (note the Check logs - one halfway down and one at the end):     You can see the leader initially reports the active nodes as [1 2 3] and then follows it up by reporting the active nodes of [1 2].          This causes problems when attempting to determine when a newly added consenter is truly active. Subsequent attempts to, for example, remove a consenter result in an incorrect failure:  "

summary: Certificate expiration - Allow TLSHandshakeTimeShift to be specified for common orderer server port.
description: "Today, you may specify 'TLSHandshakeTimeShift' in the cluster configuration of the orderer to work around expired TLS certificates to allow the orderer to restart with quorum even when TLS certificates have expired.    However, this config variable only applies when there is a separate cluster port listener involved.  When the cluster port is shared with the standard orderer port, this option is ignored.    This story is to expose a similar config option for the general TLS options of the orderer, to be applied to the standard orderer TLS listener.  In the event that a separate cluster port is utilized, then the setting in the cluster settings should take precedence."
text: Certificate expiration - Allow TLSHandshakeTimeShift to be specified for common orderer server port. This story is to expose a similar config option for the general TLS options of the orderer, to be applied to the standard orderer TLS listener.  In the event that a separate cluster port is utilized, then the setting in the cluster settings should take precedence."""

summary: When instantiating chaincode with indices, peer should log if they already exist.
description: "Currently if a user instantiates a chaincode that specifies couch indices, the peer logs the following message:    ```    2020-08-13T13:34:15.582571949Z [34m2020-08-13 13:34:15.582 UTC [couchdb] CreateIndex -> INFO f69d1[0m Created CouchDB index [indexOwner] in state database [dummy_marbles] using design document [_design/indexOwnerDoc]   ```         However, if upgrading or instantiating new chaincode that uses the same index/indices, nothing is logged.  This has caused confusion as to whether the index already existed or there was some issue and it didn't get created at all.         The request is for a similar log message saying something like ""index [IndexOwner] in state database [dummy_marbles] already exists.""  I suppose in the case where it already exists but has been modified, a similar ""modifying"" message would be nice.         Not high priority, but I think it would help.  I didn't turn on debug logs, so maybe this does get logged at other logging levels?  If so, it's infrequent enough that I think moving it to info would be OK.     "

summary: Refactor start/halt flow between registrar and consenter
description: "From a [review|https://github.com/hyperledger/fabric/pull/1628] by [~<USER> :    ""I'd like to note for the record, that this control flow still seems very awkward to me. The registrar is calling into the consenter, which as it halts is calling back into the registrar. Any time we have bidirectional binding like this is a red flag in my opinion.    I'd much rather see this implemented in one of two ways:  1. Leave the internal run state management to the consenter plugins as is done today, and expose a channel or similar mechanism to allow the the registrar to detect when the consenter has exited.  2. Remove the 'start' / 'halt' bits of the consenter API, and instead require that the caller inject a go routine, and simply have control flow return once the consenter exits, indicating that a shutdown occurred.    I think (2) is probably easier to read and more natural, while (1) is less of an API change and less code. In either case, I think both are better options than an opaque ""I'm stopping"" callback."""

summary: Allow modifying orderer Tick Time setting without requiring a manual restart of orderers
description: "Today in Fabric, the default tick time setting for channels, which is the basis for controlling how often the orderers heartbeat on a channel and how long before they evict nodes, is defaulted to 500ms.         We've found that with multiple distributed orderers and many channels, this setting is way too low.  This is because RAFT treats each channel independently, so for example if you had 5 orderers and 200 channels, that would be 1000 heartbeats being sent every 500ms.  On slower or widely distributed networks, this can quickly overwhelm the network, which causes heartbeats to be missed, nodes to get evicted, causing more traffic, causing more evictions, etc.         To modify this, the user can modify tick time.  However, this isn't as straight forward as it should be.  To modify tick time, each orderer needs to tear down etcd.  Right now in fabric, the only way to do this is to restart the orderer.  So again in a cluster of 5 orderers for example, the user would have to modify tick time on a channel, wait for the transaction to be recorded on all 5 orderers, then manually restart each orderer so that it picks up the change.  This is problematic because you don't want the orderers to be using different tick time settings as this could make the problem I listed above worse.     So the request here is to better automate the setting of tick time on a channel.  One way to do this would be for each orderer to be able to tear down and restart etcd with the new tick time setting as soon as it gets the transaction vs relying on the customer to manually handle restarts."

label: design

summary: Test crash fault tolerance: on-boarding
description: "create a network with 3 OSNs,  Start 2 and create a channel, push a few normal transactions, do a config change as well to make the last config block.number>0.  Start the 3rd OSN (to create the filerepo). Shut it down.    Emulating a failure in the 3rd OSN Join sequence:    Scenario 1: failure after the join block is is written to the filerepo but before block pulling starts. Put the the last config block taken from OSN1 in the filerepo/joinblock folder of OSN3. Restart OSN3 and check onboarding proceeds as expected.    Scenario 2: failure after the join block is is written to the filerepo, ledger folder created, but before block pulling starts. Put the the last config block taken from OSN1 in the filerepo/joinblock folder of OSN3. Create the channel folder in the ledger. Restart OSN3 and check onboarding proceeds as expected.    Scenario 3: failure after the block puling starts, but before reaching the join-block. Put the the last config block taken from OSN1 in the filerepo/joinblock folder of OSN3. Create the ledger and place the genesis block in it. Restart OSN3 and check onboarding proceeds as expected.     "

summary: Refactor multichannel.ChainSupport and its constituents
description: "Following the discussion in this [pull request|https://github.com/hyperledger/fabric/pull/1693], this task is dedicated to the refactoring of the multichannel.ChainSupport and its constituents, especially the embedded components that provide functionality around channel-config and ledger access.    See these comments for more details:  https://github.com/hyperledger/fabric/pull/1693#issuecomment-671987198  https://github.com/hyperledger/fabric/pull/1693#issuecomment-672050466  https://github.com/hyperledger/fabric/pull/1693#issuecomment-672640721  https://github.com/hyperledger/fabric/pull/1693#issuecomment-672649655    The goal is to allow the follower, the chain, and possibly cluster to have access to objects that provide the functionality of channel-config & ledger access trough a well defined set of independent objects, rather than the monolith ""ChainSupport"".     As a first step, we'll expose and isolate some of the constituents of ""ChainSupport"", but keep the functionality of ""ChainSupport"" itself as is. This is mainly to avoid too many changes at the same time, particularly at the  etcdraft.Chain that uses it.     In a second step we'll look at improving the way the  follower.Chain makes use of the individual components the emerged out of ""ChainSupport"", especially the channel-config.    In a third step we'll look at improving the way the  etcdraft.Chain makes use of the individual components of ""ChainSupport"".  "

summary: Remove ledger resources in an asynch way
description: "In this task FAB-17964 the ledger resources are removed in a synchronous way. This may create problems when the channel's ledger is big. Here we improve upon that by removing the ledger in an asynch fashion. (see review comments [here|https://github.com/hyperledger/fabric/pull/1637#discussion_r462343435]  and [here|https://github.com/hyperledger/fabric/pull/1637#discussion_r465503129] ).         The `r.ledgerFactory.Remove(channelID)` operation may take a long time. This operation is performed under the global registrar lock. If this takes even more then a fraction of a second, it will impact the performance of the server on all other channels. This method    // GetChain retrieves the chain support for a chain if it exists.    func (r *Registrar) GetChain(chainID string) *ChainSupport     \{    r.lock.RLock()    defer r.lock.RUnlock()    return r.chains[chainID]    \}    is called by every client request, through BroadcastChannelSupport  so we can't really hold that lock for too long (for write - exclusive).    What is propose is asynch deletion:   * introduce a {{*toBeRemoved* set (e.g. map[string]bool }}) that holds channels that are being removed.   * After stopping the channel move the *{{channelID}}* to that set,   * and only then delete the ledger, but make the delete async with a different go-routine.   * release the lock.   * when the delete go-routine finishes, it removes the *{{channelID}}* from the *{{toBeRemoved}}*   * any attempt to `Join` or `Remove` should also check the *{{toBeRemoved}}*"

Submit a few transactions for the  ledger height to be >>1. Submit a config TX (change something other than consensus related stuff) so that the last config will be number > 0.    Start the 3rd node w/o channels. Get the last config block  from one of the 2 active cluster nodes and join the channel on the 3rd node.    Check that the 3rd node pulls all the blocks.    Check the cluster-relation (=follower) and status for the transition (onboarding->active).    Submit some transactions to verify the 3rd pulls blocks.    Submit a config TX that adds the 3rd node to the consenters set.    Submit a few TXs to the 4th to verify it mutated into an etcdraft.Chain. "

summary: Augment BlockPuller with a method to update the endpoints
description: "As the follower pulls blocks beyond the join-block, it needs to continuously apply the channel config from each config block to the BlockPuller. One aspect of the channel config are the endpoints.    It is possible to simply recreate the block puller on every config block. However this is inefficient because it drops the internal buffer that is full of fetched and validated blocks. In order to avoid this we add a method that updates the endpoints, disconnects from  the current connection, but does not drop the internal buffer.     The next invocations of PullBlock will first re-connect, and then pop blocks from the buffer until it is empty.     "
text: Augment BlockPuller with a method to update the endpoints [SEP] """As the follower pulls blocks beyond the join-block, it needs to continuously apply the channel config from each config block to the BlockPuller. One aspect of the channel config are the endpoints.    It is possible to simply recreate the block puller on every config block. However this is inefficient because it drops the internal buffer that is full of fetched and validated blocks. In order to avoid this we add a method that updates the endpoints, disconnects from  the current connection, but does not drop the internal buffer.     The next invocations of PullBlock will first re-connect, and then pop blocks from the buffer until it is empty.     """
label: non-design

summary: BlockPuller goes into an endless loop without a way to stop
description: "If the `cluster.BlockPuller` receives a set of endpoint to which it cannot connect (ever), and then `PullBlock()` is called, the calling thread will go into an endless loop. The `tryFetchBlock` will try to connect to some endpoint and will fail every time, sleep, and try again without limit.    [see here |https://github.com/hyperledger/fabric/blob/cb1a062f9a37310c967b70528c718d25c6b95881/orderer/common/cluster/deliver.go#L117] for the loop.     This prevents the follower chain from implementing an exponential backoff and listening to the stop signal. This means that this chain cannot be `Halt()` in order to remove the follower.Chain when necessary, or stop it in tests.    It is better to return nil after a present number of retries rather than get stuck in that loop for ever.         [https://dev.azure.com/Hyperledger/0227bd2c-c4f6-478f-be00-ee519f115180/_apis/build/builds/19160/logs/66]          "
text: BlockPuller goes into an endless loop without a way to stop [SEP] """If the `cluster.BlockPuller` receives a set of endpoint to which it cannot connect (ever), and then `PullBlock()` is called, the calling thread will go into an endless loop. The `tryFetchBlock` will try to connect to some endpoint and will fail every time, sleep, and try again without limit.    [see here |https://github.com/hyperledger/fabric/blob/cb1a062f9a37310c967b70528c718d25c6b95881/orderer/common/cluster/deliver.go#L117] for the loop.     This prevents the follower chain from implementing an exponential backoff and listening to the stop signal. This means that this chain cannot be `Halt()` in order to remove the follower.Chain when necessary, or stop it in tests.    It is better to return nil after a present number of retries rather than get stuck in that loop for ever.         [https://dev.azure.com/Hyperledger/0227bd2c-c4f6-478f-be00-ee519f115180/_apis/build/builds/19160/logs/66]          """
label: non-design

summary: Chain life cycle on fatal errors
description: "Currently, when a chain (follower/etcdraft) encounters an error  it cannot recover from, it panics, bringing the entire server down. This may cause other channels, which may be completely fine, to stop working, or to have reduced fault tolerance.     With the channel participation API, channels are effectively independent, yet multi-tenant on the same orderer. Therefore we want to move to a model in which when a chain (follower/etcdraft) encounters an error it cannot recover from, it will report it in the log, and exit leaving the chain with status = types.StatusError.     The new ""List"" API may be used by the admin to discover such events, and allow him to possibly handle the issue without interrupting other channels.         One may also consider to include the number of channels with status = types.StatusError in the metrics of the Registrar. This will allow the metrics reporting system to detect such failures and alert an operator.     "
text: Chain life cycle on fatal errors  [SEP] """Currently, when a chain (follower/etcdraft) encounters an error  it cannot recover from, it panics, bringing the entire server down. This may cause other channels, which may be completely fine, to stop working, or to have reduced fault tolerance.     With the channel participation API, channels are effectively independent, yet multi-tenant on the same orderer. Therefore we want to move to a model in which when a chain (follower/etcdraft) encounters an error it cannot recover from, it will report it in the log, and exit leaving the chain with status = types.StatusError.     The new """"List"""" API may be used by the admin to discover such events, and allow him to possibly handle the issue without interrupting other channels.         One may also consider to include the number of channels with status = types.StatusError in the metrics of the Registrar. This will allow the metrics reporting system to detect such failures and alert an operator.     """
label: design

summary: Improve robustness of cluster.BlockPuller
description: "The BlockPuller from the cluster package fetches a batch of blocks from another orderer, and then uses the following function to verify the batch.    [https://github.com/hyperledger/fabric/blob/84396aaa4164a0d992b3b3e61e55b6ad7a9878c8/orderer/common/cluster/util.go#L202]    However, that function does not verify all the signatures of the blocks, only config blocks and the last block.    In addition, it verifies the hash chain only within the batch, disregarding the block just previous to the batch.     [https://github.com/hyperledger/fabric/blob/84396aaa4164a0d992b3b3e61e55b6ad7a9878c8/orderer/common/cluster/util.go#L210]    As a result, code that uses the BlockPuller is forced to verify the hash chain itself, and worse, there is a danger of accepting blocks with corrupt signatures.         In this task the block verification BlockPuller should be augmented and made to resemble what the peer does: check every signature and verify the hash chain completely, skipping no blocks.         When this task is done, it will be possible to remove the redundant hash chain checks from code that uses the BlockPuller."
text: Improve robustness of cluster.BlockPuller [SEP] """The BlockPuller from the cluster package fetches a batch of blocks from another orderer, and then uses the following function to verify the batch.    [https://github.com/hyperledger/fabric/blob/84396aaa4164a0d992b3b3e61e55b6ad7a9878c8/orderer/common/cluster/util.go#L202]    However, that function does not verify all the signatures of the blocks, only config blocks and the last block.    In addition, it verifies the hash chain only within the batch, disregarding the block just previous to the batch.     [https://github.com/hyperledger/fabric/blob/84396aaa4164a0d992b3b3e61e55b6ad7a9878c8/orderer/common/cluster/util.go#L210]    As a result, code that uses the BlockPuller is forced to verify the hash chain itself, and worse, there is a danger of accepting blocks with corrupt signatures.         In this task the block verification BlockPuller should be augmented and made to resemble what the peer does: check every signature and verify the hash chain completely, skipping no blocks.         When this task is done, it will be possible to remove the redundant hash chain checks from code that uses the BlockPuller."""
label: non-design

summary: Chaincode should be able to emit an event when it's invoked by other chaincode
description: "The current behaviour regarding chaincode events is as follows:    ""Only the chaincode invoked by the client can emit events""    So that means it's not possible to implement a proxy chaincode that invokes another chaincode (child chaincode) because all the events related to this last one will be lost.    This doesn't make sense and becomes a limitation. Events should be thrown in a basic delegate call implementation.     "

This doesn't make sense and becomes a limitation. Events should be thrown in a basic delegate call implementation.     """
label: non-design

summary: Validate TLS intermediate certs against the TLS root certs
description: "Created based on [this discussion in github|https://github.com/hyperledger/fabric-config/pull/35#discussion_r460927399].    The validateCACerts code [here|https://github.com/hyperledger/fabric-config/blob/a97807f0619130c8aa7fe1ce33b19109168ca55a/configtx/msp.go#L893-L905] should be refactored/reused to validate that the signature for each of the TLS intermediate CA certs has a valid signature from one of the TLS root CA certs.    Note: if you really feel like getting extra credit, you can see if we can incorporate the TODO to use cert.Verify() to provide a more complete verification but simply using tlsIntermediateCert.CheckSignatureFrom(tlsRootCert) is sufficient for now. "
text: Validate TLS intermediate certs against the TLS root certs [SEP] """Created based on [this discussion in github|https://github.com/hyperledger/fabric-config/pull/35#discussion_r460927399].    The validateCACerts code [here|https://github.com/hyperledger/fabric-config/blob/a97807f0619130c8aa7fe1ce33b19109168ca55a/configtx/msp.go#L893-L905] should be refactored/reused to validate that the signature for each of the TLS intermediate CA certs has a valid signature from one of the TLS root CA certs.    Note: if you really feel like getting extra credit, you can see if we can incorporate the TODO to use cert.Verify() to provide a more complete verification but simply using tlsIntermediateCert.CheckSignatureFrom(tlsRootCert) is sufficient for now. """

summary: Convert PTE to the High Level Node SDK
description: "PTE is written using the fabric-common package, which is the low-level SDK. Now that the high-level SDK exists and that is the area of focus for future development, PTE should be modified to use the high-level SDK.    Instead of attempting to modify the existing PTE, the rewrite should be a copy and sit in a folder next to PTE (maybe PTEv2) for now. Development will be incremental, code should be pushed frequently, and reviewed and merged frequently, rather than pushing an entire rewrite of the codebase in single commit series.    We should open tasks around each component of PTE we need to implement.    Acceptance criteria: I can run all E2E system tests using the high-level SDK."

summary: Channel participation API - Join, Leave & List channels w/o a system channel
description: "Currently in order to create a channel one needs to issue a config transaction to the system channel. This has *privacy, scalability* and *operational* disadvantages, since the system channel (and the orderers running it) is aware of all channels and of all channel members (at creation time).    In this feature we propose to expose a ""channel participation"" API that would allow a local orderer administrator to *join* and *leave* a channel, as well as to *list* all the channels that the *local orderer* is part of. This allows a Fabric network to be operated *without* the use of *a system channel*, which improves the privacy and scalability of the ordering service and the network as a whole. It also alleviates some of the operational problems associated with the current style of channel creation.    This feature is described in an RFC titled ""Channel participation api without system channel"" ([currently PR #24|https://github.com/hyperledger/fabric-rfcs/pull/24]).    This feature was presented in a [Fabric contributor's meeting|https://wiki.hyperledger.org/display/fabric/Contributor+Meetings+2020] on April 1st 2020, the presentation is here: [^Channel Participation API - community call - 1Apr2020.pdf].     "

summary: Refactor Version and Revision Cache in State CouchDB
description: "Among ValidateAndPrepare(), PrepareExpiringKeys(), and RemoveStaleAndCommitPvtDataOfOldBlocks(), we can allow only one function to execute at a time. The reason is that each function calls LoadCommittedVersions() which would clear the existing entries in the transient buffer and load new entries (such a transient buffer is not   applicable for the golevelDB). As a result, these three functions can interleave and nullify the optimization provided by the bulk read API.     Once the ledger cache (FAB-103) is introduced and existing LoadCommittedVersions() would be refactored to return a map. Then, we can allow these three functions to execute parallely."
text: Refactor Version and Revision Cache in State CouchDB [SEP] """Among ValidateAndPrepare(), PrepareExpiringKeys(), and RemoveStaleAndCommitPvtDataOfOldBlocks(), we can allow only one function to execute at a time. The reason is that each function calls LoadCommittedVersions() which would clear the existing entries in the transient buffer and load new entries (such a transient buffer is not   applicable for the golevelDB). As a result, these three functions can interleave and nullify the optimization provided by the bulk read API.     Once the ledger cache (FAB-103) is introduced and existing LoadCommittedVersions() would be refactored to return a map. Then, we can allow these three functions to execute parallely."""
label: design

summary: Use cc lifecycle APIs in test tools
description: "Implement new code in our tools to use the new v2.0 cc lifecycle APIs (package/approve/commit chaincodes).   * Add PTE functions and Operator functions to use new APIs in node-sdk APIs   * Add/update functions in NL to use the CLI APIs   * Add commands to testInput file and add functions in operator   * Update all tests to use the new methods   * And of course enable v2_0 application_capabilities in the networkSpec files used for any tests using new cc lifecycle         Below are some notes from FAB-16262 ""Use new lifecycle APIs of node-sdk in Operator/PTE tools"" (which was closed as dup).    v2.0.0 will have the old (i.e. install, instantiate) and new (i.e. approve, commit...) APIs. Tools currently use the old APIs, but should be updated to work with the newer flow (and most certainly before 2.1 when the old APIs may be deprecated).    Some of the newer lifecycle items in node-sdk will be delayed, though, and may not be available in v2.0. Discussions about node-sdk support in fabric v2.0 are ongoing; here are some points stated recently:   * fabric-client (master) supports new fabric v2.0 lifecycle (just a few updates are required based on recent fabric changes).   * The intent is to publish fabric-client as a v1.5 release in parallel with fabric v2.0 (target end of September).   * fabric-client will be end-of-lifed at v1.5.x.   * node.js SDK v2.0 will continue to have fabric-network high level API, but will use a new reworked fabric-base low level API. We'll encourage everybody to use the high level API.   * node.js SDK v2.0 release will not be ready by end of September, but that's not a problem - it does not need to coincide with fabric v2.0 release, especially since the v1.5 SDK will have support for fabric v2.0 including new lifecycle.   * node.js SDK v2.0 should eventually have support for fabric v2.0 lifecycle (this will largely be a copy of the fabric-client v1.5 lifecycle code).   * node.js SDK v2.0 will not have backwards compatibility wrt node.js v1.x SDKs for applications using low level API...we will need to write guidance for users to shift their apps from using low level v1.x node.js SDK to high level v2.x node.js SDK         Confer with the node sdk dev team to get details. In 2.0, they will make some major changes, which we need to adapt to:   * create a fabric-admin for APIs for the new cc lifecycle functions   * eliminate fabric-client (no longer available, even in 2.0-beta)   * must use cli commands for create-channel and join-channel functions   * suggest using fabric-network or fabric-common for other functions"

APIs. Tools currently use the old APIs, but should be updated to work with the newer flow (and most certainly before 2.1 when the old APIs may be deprecated).    Some of the newer lifecycle items in node-sdk will be delayed, though, and may not be available in v2.0. Discussions about node-sdk support in fabric v2.0 are ongoing; here are some points stated recently:   * fabric-client (master) supports new fabric v2.0 lifecycle (just a few updates are required based on recent fabric changes).   * The intent is to publish fabric-client as a v1.5 release in parallel with fabric v2.0 (target end of September).   * fabric-client will be end-of-lifed at v1.5.x.   * node.js SDK v2.0 will continue to have fabric-network high level API, but will use a new reworked fabric-base low level API. We'll encourage everybody to use the high level API.   * node.js SDK v2.0 release will not be ready by end of September, but that's not a problem - it does not need to coincide with fabric v2.0 release, especially since the v1.5 SDK will have support for fabric v2.0 including new lifecycle.   * node.js SDK v2.0 should eventually have support for fabric v2.0 lifecycle (this will largely be a copy of the fabric-client v1.5 lifecycle code).   * node.js SDK v2.0 will not have backwards compatibility wrt node.js v1.x SDKs for applications using low level API...we will need to write guidance for users to shift their apps from using low level v1.x node.js SDK to high level v2.x node.js SDK         Confer with the node sdk dev team to get details. In 2.0, they will make some major changes, which we need to adapt to:   * create a fabric-admin for APIs for the new cc lifecycle functions   * eliminate fabric-client (no longer available, even in 2.0-beta)   * must use cli commands for create-channel and join-channel functions   * suggest using fabric-network or fabric-common for other functions"""

Tools currently use the old APIs, but should be updated to work with the newer flow (and most certainly before 2.1 when the old APIs may be deprecated).    Some of the newer lifecycle items in node-sdk will be delayed, though, and may not be available in v2.0. Discussions about node-sdk support in fabric v2.0 are ongoing; here are some points stated recently:     * fabric-client (master) supports new fabric v2.0 lifecycle (just a few updates are required based on recent fabric changes).  * The intent is to publish fabric-client as a v1.5 release in parallel with fabric v2.0 (target end of September).  * fabric-client will be end-of-lifed at v1.5.x.  * node.js SDK v2.0 will continue to have fabric-network high level API, but will use a new reworked fabric-base low level API. We'll encourage everybody to use the high level API.  * node.js SDK v2.0 release will not be ready by end of September, but that's not a problem - it does not need to coincide with fabric v2.0 release, especially since the v1.5 SDK will have support for fabric v2.0 including new lifecycle.  * node.js SDK v2.0 should eventually have support for fabric v2.0 lifecycle (this will largely be a copy of the fabric-client v1.5 lifecycle code).  * node.js SDK v2.0 will not have backwards compatibility wrt node.js v1.x SDKs for applications using low level API...we will need to write guidance for users to shift their apps from using low level v1.x node.js SDK to high level v2.x node.js SDK    """
label: design

summary: Design & implement channel participation REST API - Join, Remove, and List channels
description: "With FAB-15708 and FAB-15709 implemented, an orderer can start without any genesis method, or any channels defined, but does nothing.      We need an API to be able to:    * Join this OSN to a new or an existing channel,  * Remove this OSN from an existing channel,  * List the channels this OSN is a member of.    This story is dedicated to the detailed design and implementation of the REST API of this feature, with the underlying behavior described in epic FAB-17712 and associated RFC. The REST API implementation will accept the queries defined and will transfer them to an abstract interface of the underlying implementation.    The first product of this task is a design document.    The REST API design is [HERE|https://docs.google.com/document/d/1kYoicS_Z59gNvmuuB7VFZ6WsOL31C34dWMeOFQiIygA/edit?usp=sharing]."

summary: Remove the redundant TLS certificate information stored per block
description: "Presently, the TLS certificates are specified in the channel config, then replicated into every block metadata persisted to disk.  Because the TLS certificates are non-trivial in size (and there may be many raft nodes) this presents a significant amount of wasted space.    Because there is a set of Raft nodes specified in the channel config, all we need to know is how to map those certs in the channel config to the underlying Raft IDs."

summary: Removing an Issue Type from an Issue Type Scheme when there exists archived issues of that type shows incorrect error
description: "h3. Issue Summary    When removing an Issue Type from an Issue Type Scheme, Jira will need to perform a migration task for all issues of that Issue Type in the Projects that use that Issue Type Scheme.    As of Jira 8.0.0, it is possible to archive specific issues in a Data Center environment, per [Archiving an issue|https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/archiving-an-issue-968669980.html], which prevents them from being searchable and edited.    When an Issue Type is removed from an Issue Type Scheme, and there are *archived* issues of that Issue Type in a project that uses that Issue Type Scheme, the entire migration will fail, as *archived* issues cannot be edited, and thus cannot be migrated to a different issue type.    However, the administrator will receive an unhelpful error that does not explain that the root cause of the migration failure is due to an archived issue, and instead receives the following message:  {quote}You do not have the correct permissions to move the issues from project Software for issue type Bug{quote}   !image-2020-10-23-14-22-05-234.png|thumbnail!     h3. Steps to Reproduce  # In any project, archive an issue. Note that issue's Issue Type.  # Attempt to remove that Issue Type from the Issue Type Scheme for the project    h3. Expected Results    A clear error explaining that archived issues exist for the Issue Type being moved is shown.    h3. Actual Results    The Administrator receives this error, suggesting that the problem relate to project permissions:    {quote}You do not have the correct permissions to move the issues from project Software for issue type Bug{quote}    h3. Workaround    Since there are no project permissions that can control someones ability to move only issues of *specific* issue types, if the administrator can verify that they have the ability to migrate *other* issue types and just not certain affected ones, then it is likely that archived issues are the culprit. They may then unarchive the issues in order to proceed."

summary: Global permission "Create Shared Objects" enables a user to edit shared Dashboard/Filter
description: "h3. Issue Summary    JRASERVER-17783 introduced a feature that enable users to edit others' dashboard. But specifying user and group as editors do not enable the user to edit the shared dashboard/filter but adding them to a group having global permission ""Create Shared Objects"" does the same  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Log in as an admin user   Create 2 normal users named 'edit.user' and 'share.user'. Both are in group 'jira-users' which gives general access to Jira.   # Add 'share.user' to another group ""jira-servicedesk-users"", that is assigned to the global permission ""Create Shared Objects"".   # Login as 'Share.user' and create a new dashboard. Add 'jira-users' as viewers and edit.user as editor.   # Login as 'edit.user'. The shared dashboard looks like this for user 'edit.user': dash_view_edit.user.png. 'edit.user' should be able to edit this dashboard but cannot.   # user 'edit.user' is added to the group ""jira-servicedesk-users"" and reloads the dashboard. Then 'edit.user' can edit. (see: dash_view_edit.user_plus_global_perm.png)      h3. Expected Results    When 'share.user' sets view + edit group to 'jira-users' for the shared dashboard, 'edit.user' should be able to edit the dashboard.  h3. Actual Results    Only when edit.user is added to the group having the global perm ""Create Shared Objects"" editing is possible."

summary: Adding a custom field option value with more than 255 characters results on a 500 error page
description: "h3. Issue Summary    When updating an option value with more than 255 characters at a ""Select List (single choice)"" custom field, a 500 error page is exhibited.  The issue happens because the value exceeds the size from column ""customvalue"" of the table ""customfieldoption"".    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create a ""Select List (single choice)"" custom field with any option value.  # At the Jira Custom Fields management page (Administration > Issues > Custom fields), click at the Actions > Configure button for the newly created custom field.  # At the bottom of the page, click at the ""Edit Options"" link.  # Try to add a new Custom Field Option or edit one that already exists using a text field with more than 255 characters.  # A 500 error page will occur when trying to save the value      h3. Expected Results    There should be a filter at the UI for these fields, limiting the field up to 255 characters.    h3. Actual Results    A 500 error message containing the text below:          h3. Workaround    Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"

summary: "Add watchers" option is visible for users who do not have "Manage Watchers" permission
description: "h3. Issue Summary  ""Add watchers"" option is visible for users who do not have ""Manage Watchers"" permission.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a Jira project. Add a user to the project (non admin).   # Remove ""Manage Watchers"" permission for the added user.   # Login as 'user'. Create a new Issue. After issue is created ""Add Watchers"" option will not be visible for the user, which is as expected.  # Access the same issue through *Projects > Project > Issues > Issue number*, the ""Add watchers"" option will be visible for the agent even if the user does not have 'Manage Watchers' permission.   (i) However, the user will not be able to add any watchers, and an error message will be displayed as the agent/user does not have ""Manage Watchers"" permission.    But, having the ""Add watchers"" option for user even if user is not having any permission leads to confusion.    h3. Expected Results  ""Add watchers"" option should not be displayed for 'user' who do not have 'Manager Watchers' permission. This should be the case when issue is accessed through any method.    h3. Actual Results  Access the issue through *Projects > Project > Issues > Issue number*, the ""Add watchers"" option will be visible for the agent even if user does not have 'Manage Watchers' permission.       h3. Workaround   Currently, there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"

summary: Automatic password rotation for AWS Secrets Manager
description: "AWS Secrets Manager allows to use a rotate password for the Databases, that changes every X days, without human interaction. But there is no such a function or change the Database password on Confluence automatically.  h3. Suggested Solution  Create an integration between Jira and the AWS Secrets Manager to update the database password automatically.    AWS provides a reference JDBC implementation that intercepts calls to the driver and replaces credentials on the fly. The examples provided use C3P0 (which Jira does not use), so I'm not sure if it's applicable - https://github.com/aws/aws-secretsmanager-jdbc    h3. Suggest workaround   AWS Secrets Manager has a API that permits to use scrips to connect to it and collect the new password. A possible workaround would be:   # Create a script to get the new password from the AWS Secrets Manager as described in this page: [How to use AWS Secrets Manager to rotate credentials for all Amazon RDS database types, including Oracle|https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/security/how-to-use-aws-secrets-manager-rotate-credentials-amazon-rds-database-types-oracle/]   # Use the script to update the password retrieve from the AWS to update the database password in JIRA_INSTALL/conf/dbconfig.xml   # Finally, use the script to restart the Jira"

summary: Support for JIT Gsuite.
description: "With SAML, we added a custom multi-valued user attribute in G Suite as described in the article below to represent groups the user belongs to:https://support.google.com/a/answer/6208725?hl=en    This helped work around the mandatory Groups field in the JIT provisioning settings and allowed us to authenticate successfully with a local account being provisioned automatically. However manually duplicating the list of groups a user belongs to in a custom G suite attribute is a lot of overhead when we consider doing this for hundreds of users. It's also error prone (groups have to be typed in, not selected from a list when adding to the custom attribute) and is also a security risk (eg: not removing the group name from the custom attribute when the user's been removed from the Google group could result in a user still having access to resources they're not supposed to have access to). We also reached out to Google to see whether there's a way to fetch the actual Google groups as an attribute, however they said it's not possible at this time and that the application needs to fetch the group information using the Directory API (please see here: https://developers.google.com/admin-sdk/directory/v1/reference/groups/list). Is supporting G Suite as an IdP in the SSO settings using this mechanism something we can request as a feature?"
text: Support for JIT Gsuite. [SEP] """With SAML, we added a custom multi-valued user attribute in G Suite as described in the article below to represent groups the user belongs to:https://support.google.com/a/answer/6208725?hl=en    This helped work around the mandatory Groups field in the JIT provisioning settings and allowed us to authenticate successfully with a local account being provisioned automatically. However manually duplicating the list of groups a user belongs to in a custom G suite attribute is a lot of overhead when we consider doing this for hundreds of users. It's also error prone (groups have to be typed in, not selected from a list when adding to the custom attribute) and is also a security risk (eg: not removing the group name from the custom attribute when the user's been removed from the Google group could result in a user still having access to resources they're not supposed to have access to). We also reached out to Google to see whether there's a way to fetch the actual Google groups as an attribute, however they said it's not possible at this time and that the application needs to fetch the group information using the Directory API (please see here: https://developers.google.com/admin-sdk/directory/v1/reference/groups/list). Is supporting G Suite as an IdP in the SSO settings using this mechanism something we can request as a feature?"""
label: design

summary: Support for Postgresql 13
description: "This issue for adding support to Jira for Postgresql, which was released 9/24/2020.    It would be much appreciated if support could be added to other server/data center products (e.g. Confluence) at around the same time. As a customer, being able to run each Atlassian product on the same database version reduces complexity, maintenance cost and ultimately the cost of ownership for Atlassian tools.    Having support available for more recent versions of postgresql gives us more flexibility in scheduling upgrades and gets us using newer versions more quickly -- instead of waiting an indeterminate amount of time for Atlassian to add support for it, and only then being able to upgrade.     "
text: Support for Postgresql 13 [SEP] """This issue for adding support to Jira for Postgresql, which was released 9/24/2020.    It would be much appreciated if support could be added to other server/data center products (e.g. Confluence) at around the same time. Having support available for more recent versions of postgresql gives us more flexibility in scheduling upgrades and gets us using newer versions more quickly -- instead of waiting an indeterminate amount of time for Atlassian to add support for it, and only then being able to upgrade.     """

summary: Starting a foreground reindex using the REST API could corrupt a snapshot
description: "h3. Issue Summary    When we start the Reindex process using the REST API {{/rest/api/2/reindex?type=FOREGROUND}}, the index snapshot is still able to run on other nodes, which results in corrupt snapshots.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Start both JIRA nodes  # Schedule the index snapshot to run  # Initiate full locked reindex at Node1 a few minutes before the snapshot:  {code:borderStyle=solid}  curl  -X POST http://127.0.0.1:8091/rest/api/2/reindex?type=FOREGROUND  {code}  # Notice that the snapshot still happens on Node2    h3. Expected Results    The index snapshot should not run while the reindex is running.  It should either queue it up and wait for the reindex to finish, or simply handle as a conflict and not run the snapshot.    h3. Actual Results    The index snapshot runs while the reindex is running.    h3. Workaround    * When calling the reindex, make sure that the snapshot is not scheduled to run in the next minutes/hours  * Or make sure to start it from the UI, instead of the API."

Issue Summary    When we start the Reindex process using the REST API {{/rest/api/2/reindex?type=FOREGROUND}}, the index snapshot is still able to run on other nodes, which results in corrupt snapshots.    h3. Workaround    * When calling the reindex, make sure that the snapshot is not scheduled to run in the next minutes/hours  * Or make sure to start it from the UI, instead of the API."""

summary: Clicking on a username on the Versions page redirects to a wrong profile
description: "h3. Issue Summary  When clicking on any users on the Versions page (<JIRA>/projects/<PROJECT_KEY/versions/<VERSION_KEY>) results on being redirected to the profile page of the userkey, instead of the username of the user. This only happens on the Versions page, and only with users that have different userkeys and usernames.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Click on any user on the Versions page, that has a different userkey and username.   # Jira redirects to the profile page of the userkey, showing a ""The specified user could not be found."" error.    h3. Expected Results  Jira redirects to the proper profile page.  h3. Actual Results  Jira is redirecting to the profile page of the userkey.    h3. Workaround   Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available"

summary: Poor Performance due to ProjectRoleActorsZduSafeCache
description: "h3. Issue Summary    Jira nodes experience poor performance due to contention with the ProjectRoleActorsZduSafeCache.  h3. Steps to Reproduce    N/A  h3. Expected Results    Jira will experience no-cache contention for _*ProjectRoleActorsZduSafeCache*_  h3. Actual Results    Thread dumps will show a high number of threads waiting on _*ProjectRoleActorsZduSafeCache*_    _Sample thread:_       h3. Workaround    Upgrade to Jira 8.12.2 onwards as this contains the fix.   h3. Background    We had a call with the author of the changes who was able to give us some background on this.    There was a need to optimize the role actors for given project cache by rewriting it (too much data being deleted on cache eviction). This solved https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRASERVER-69446. The ProjectRoleActorsZduSafeCache class has been introduced to make it work when Jira is in a mixed mode, during the ZDU. Unfortunately it caused some problems with cache replication between the old and new cache and in some cases unnecessary cache invalidations.  h3. Proposed way of testing if the Jira upgrade will solve the problem    The problem should be present only when there are a lot of project role actors modifications. The client could setup Jira 8.11.1 and trigger a lot of such operations (e.g. by some automation), upgrade, e.g. to 8.13, do the test again and see if the problem persists."

summary: Security improvements to the Velocity Uberspector
description: "This ticket documents an improvement to the Velocity Uberspector's security, locking down which classes can be accessed. This change is a defence-in-depth against potential Remote Code Execution (RCE) and Injection attacks.    The versions which do not have this improvement are before version 8.12.3.    *Pre-improvement versions:*   * version < 8.12.3    *Improved versions:*   * 8.12.3   * 8.13.0   * 8.14.0"

summary: Customizable Customfield name for project
description: "I would appreciate function for set customfield name for project scope in Custom Field configuration or Customfield context    For example scenario:    Problem manager has container *Project problem* and he need new customfield name ""Problem start"" so we created new date Customfield ""Problem start""  Week later Test manager need new customfield ""Test iteration start"" in project *Test iterations*, but he wants exactly this name in screen...  week later Service Desk agent new customfield ""*Predicted resolution date""* in project Service desk requests...    After 3 weeks we have 3 customfields in 3 separated containters, all of them are Date fields, not used in more than One exact project, because their name is so Unique, than nobody else can use them, because their orientation is for one specific use Case.    BUT    IF I could create customfield named: ""Date"" and set in Field Config schema or context his personalizated name for some scope....    We would have 1 customfield with 3 separated names for many usecases.   There are BIG Jira implementations, where corporates generate customfields in hundreds (100 text fields in instance is normal...) but all of this is caused because manager need exact name on screen or export.    In architecture of JIRA, there is one conjuctive unique identificator and it is ID of Customfield, We can create many customfields with identical name and different type, so why I can not change Name in scope of project? or issueType?    Advantage: optimalization and performance of database  minimalization generation fields in Jira with one purpose...  reusing existing fields    Disadvantege: JQL name, export name orientation    nowadays I know workaround and it is Script runner Behaviour. But it is not solution, because many times I can not run JS on all screens and set up context. Export is with origin name, JQL as well.     "

summary: It is difficult to identify and correct errors.
description: "If errors occur in the application, error messages are displayed. Some problems arise for partially sighted and blind users:   ▪ Blind users can hardly perceive the messages because they are not in the label of the form fields and are therefore not output by the screen reader in form mode.   ▪ In addition, these are implemented like help texts behind the element concerned and are only highlighted in color. Visually impaired users with color ametropia will have problems recognizing the message. The color adjustment will also not be visible in the contrast adjustment.   ▪ When editing an issue, the problem arises that incorrect data is deleted without the user becoming aware of it. If, for example, a sprint name that does not exist is entered and the user submits the form (""update""), this is saved and the value is removed. This means that users will not recognize errors and will enter incomplete data.     "

summary: Updates are sometimes difficult to use.
description: "All dynamic changes within a dialog mask that do not trigger a complete reload of the page may be recognized by the screen reader. not noticed. The following problems occur:   The window for editing the current filter details of issues does not get focus when opened. Keyboard users cannot edit filters and have to create a new filter.         Drop-down lists for filtering search results cannot be usefully operated with the keyboard. Blind and motor-impaired users have the problem that after activating an option, the focus is set in the background, the search result is updated and the list is not closed. In order to set further filter options from this list, these users have to navigate back to activate the next option.    The change in the status of an issue is only indicated by a fly-in message, which is not output by the screen reader. Blind users therefore have to navigate through the content with a slight additional effort in order to see the current status .         In some pop-ups the problem arises that the focus is not set on the first interactive element after opening, e.g. when creating a new issue (see figure, marker A). Keyboard users therefore have to navigate to the previous content with additional effort. Blind users also have the problem that it cannot be seen that and how many elements are in front of the current position within the pop-up.   If a project is selected when creating an issue, the focus is then set on the ""Summary"" input field. Interactive elements are skipped. Keyboard users have to go back with additional effort to reach these elements.    In all areas of the application the problem arises that after closing pop-ups or reloading the page, e.g. after a status change of a process on the view page, the focus is set to the top of the page. This means that keyboard users have to navigate to previously focused content with additional effort.     "

summary: Issue details cannot be accessed from the agile boards using the keyboard.
description: "Motor-impaired and blind users who cannot use the mouse can navigate using the tab key. Is navigated to an element directly, i. H. Without further input, an action carried out, users cannot specifically control this action. Since each element is reached in a specific order when navigating with the keyboard, users want u. You may just skip an element, but not carry out the associated action every time.   One such problem occurs in the agile boards. When navigating through the activities in the swim lanes, the details area on the right-hand side (if shown) is updated for each focused activity. However, this area cannot be reached with an access key, so that keyboard users in this area can only display and access the last process on the board. This function can therefore not be used by keyboard users."

summary: Some links are not clearly labeled.
description: "Screen readers offer the possibility of outputting all the links on a page one after the other. This enables a blind user to quickly gain an overview of the navigation structure and, if necessary, to switch directly to another page without having to display the entire content every time. In the entire application, however, some links are not clearly labeled so that blind users cannot recognize the function. Affected are i.a. the following elements :   ▪ the pagination links of the data tables, for example on the dashboard in the ""As-signed to me"" area,   ▪ the drop-down links of the sections with the output ""Number Link"",   ▪ the ""Share"" link without labeling, for example the issue view. ""    → Links must be clearly marked. In particular, links that are implemented without text using graphics, icons or SVG require an alternative designation, for example using an <alt> or <arial-label> attribute."

label: non-design

summary: The keyboard focus is difficult to recognize on some elements.
description: "Motor-impaired users navigate through an application with the keyboard. In order to be able to orientate yourself, you depend above all on the fact that the tabulator focus is clearly visible on the interactive elements. The focus is barely visible on many links, as the font color is only slightly darkened. Since a lot of links are affected, e.g. all links in issue lists and issue detail forms, users with restricted mobility cannot orient themselves in the application. The operation of the application is considerably more difficult for them.   For menu items of the closed navigation menu (see illustration, navigation menu on the left), a tooltip is displayed when the focus is retained so that the focused element can be recognized. When the navigation menu is opened, however, the focus on the menu items is not visible.           "

summary: The order of focus does not partly correspond to the visual and logical order.
description: "People with motor disabilities often cannot use the mouse and instead use the tab key to navigate through applications. If the tab order does not correspond to the visual representation and the logical processing order, it is difficult for the user to work efficiently. Even blind people who cannot access visual cues to correctly capture the navigation structure are dependent on a logical tabulator order. In this context, the following problems were identified:    On many pages, content is not reached in the visual and logical order. Orientation and navigation are made more difficult for users with limited mobility. For blind users, it is more difficult to assign interactive elements to the content. For example, the elements are affected:   ▪ Preview area of ​​the backlog on the right-hand side.   ▪ The buttons ""Only my Issues"" and ""Recently Updated"" of the backlog are reached in front of the search field.   ▪ The buttons “Share”, “Export” and Tools in the search are reached before the heading.   ▪ The control elements in the project overview of the dashboard are reached before the project name link.   ▪ The control elements in the header area of ​​the activity areas are always reached last (within the activity area), for example in the user profile (not shown) or in the issue view.   ▪ The buttons for changing the issue within the issue view are reached as the last elements on the page.   ▪ In the user profile, the number of open issues per project is reached before the project name (area ""Assigned open issues per project"", not shown)   ▪ The shortcut message of the ""Assign"" window is only reached after the ""Assign"" and ""Cancel"" buttons.               The operation of the selection list ""More"" in the issue view is not possible without restrictions for users with restricted mobility. If a user navigates to the options in this list with the arrow keys, the focus is removed from the list after the list element ""At-tach files"" and the following options cannot be reached. This means that users with limited motor skills must use the points menu in order to be able to operate these functions. For blind users, this problem is not weighted because the selection lists cannot be used.                  Some elements that can be edited inline receive focus but cannot be activated (see illustration). Keyboard users have to navigate through these elements with additional effort.            The keyboard focus cannot be set in the toolbar of the Richtext editor. This means that keyboard users cannot format text.       A keyboard user is only able to use three elements from the toolbar with a keyboard combination to a very limited extent. This applies to the functions ""bold"", ""oblique"" and ""underlined"", which can be executed using Ctrl b, Ctrl i and Ctrl u. All other functions either do not have a key combination or the key combination is already used for something else by the browser. Getting the keyboard focus into the bar is not possible.   → The toolbar should be accessible with the tab key and operated using the arrow keys, space key and enter key.        "
text: The order of focus does not partly correspond to the visual and logical order. [SEP] """People with motor disabilities often cannot use the mouse and instead use the tab key to navigate through applications. If the tab order does not correspond to the visual representation and the logical processing order, it is difficult for the user to work efficiently. Even blind people who cannot access visual cues to correctly capture the navigation structure are dependent on a logical tabulator order. In this context, the following problems were identified:    On many pages, content is not reached in the visual and logical order. Orientation and navigation are made more difficult for users with limited mobility. For blind users, it is more difficult to assign interactive elements to the content. For example, the elements are affected:   ▪ Preview area of ​​the backlog on the right-hand side.   ▪ The buttons """"Only my Issues"""" and """"Recently Updated"""" of the backlog are reached in front of the search field.   ▪ The buttons “Share”, “Export” and Tools in the search are reached before the heading.   ▪ The control elements in the project overview of the dashboard are reached before the project name link.   ▪ The control elements in the header area of ​​the activity areas are always reached last (within the activity area), for example in the user profile (not shown) or in the issue view.   ▪ The buttons for changing the issue within the issue view are reached as the last elements on the page.   ▪ In the user profile, the number of open issues per project is reached before the project name (area """"Assigned open issues per project"""", not shown)   ▪ The shortcut message of the """"Assign"""" window is only reached after the """"Assign"""" and """"Cancel"""" buttons.               The operation of the selection list """"More"""" in the issue view is not possible without restrictions for users with restricted mobility. If a user navigates to the options in this list with the arrow keys, the focus is removed from the list after the list element """"At-tach files"""" and the following options cannot be reached. This means that users with limited motor skills must use the points menu in order to be able to operate these functions. For blind users, this problem is not weighted because the selection lists cannot be used.                  Some elements that can be edited inline receive focus but cannot be activated (see illustration). Keyboard users have to navigate through these elements with additional effort.            The keyboard focus cannot be set in the toolbar of the Richtext editor. This means that keyboard users cannot format text.       A keyboard user is only able to use three elements from the toolbar with a keyboard combination to a very limited extent. This applies to the functions """"bold"""", """"oblique"""" and """"underlined"""", which can be executed using Ctrl b, Ctrl i and Ctrl u. All other functions either do not have a key combination or the key combination is already used for something else by the browser. Getting the keyboard focus into the bar is not possible.   → The toolbar should be accessible with the tab key and operated using the arrow keys, space key and enter key.        """
label: non-design

summary: Larger element groups cannot be skipped efficiently.
description: "How efficient keyboard users work depends largely on the design of the keyboard navigation. The following problems arise:   ▪ Large groups of interactive elements, such as the menu, must be tabbed through on each page. As a result, the users very often repeat the same action over and over again: tabbing over all links, for example to reach the content. This restricts targeted and fast navigation in the content for users with restricted mobility (see illustration).   ▪ The problem for blind users is that the arrow key navigation between the problem list and the detail view does not work (see figure, marker A).   ▪ If the focus is set back on the list from the detail view, the first element of the list is not focused on, but rather on the last element. Motor-restricted users have to navigate with additional effort to the last focused element "

Motor-restricted users have to navigate with additional effort to the last focused element """

summary: The column labels of some tables are not correctly marked.
description: "The contents of the table can be perceived in a structured manner. Blind users do not have this option. Among other things, you use the column headings to get an idea of the structure of the table, which is the basis for meaningful access to the data it contains. The following problems occur:   ▪ The content in the ""Sub-Tasks"" area in the task view is implemented in a table without column labels (see figure). Blind users will find it more difficult to grasp the content.   ▪ The contents of the Printable View are also implemented in data tables without column labels.        Basically, all tables lack a table name that is marked with the <caption> element. Overall, the table structures are too complex and confusing in many places. Most of the column structures are missing.       → All tables require a name using the <caption> element and meaningful column headings using the <th> element.   → With the sometimes very complex table structures, it should be checked whether the tables should actually be layout tables. These can then be implemented accordingly using <table role = “none“>."

summary: Linked issues created through the "Create Linked Issue" option do not respect the default priority selected in the project's priority scheme.
description: "h3. Issue Summary  Whenever a linked issue is created using the *Create linked issue* functionality, its priority will match the original issue's priority instead of the one selected as the default priority in the issue priority scheme.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Access an issue.   # Click on *More* > *Create linked issues*.   # Fill out the details but do not select a priority.   # Click on *Create*.    h3. Expected Results  * The linked issue's priority should be the one specified in the Issue Priority Scheme.     !Screen Shot 2020-10-07 at 23.32.34.png|thumbnail!     h3. Actual Results  * The linked issue's priority is equal to the priority of the issue where the *Create linked issue* functionality was used.     !Screen Shot 2020-10-07 at 23.31.18.png|thumbnail!     h3. Workaround    Manually select the linked issue's priority if it is displayed on the *Create Issue Screen*."

summary: Deleting users in LDAP don't set them as inactive
description: "h3. Issue Summary    When we delete users from LDAP, they are set as inactive in Jira if they have any references, and deleted otherwise.    But when the references are:   * Watch an issue   * Vote for an issue   * Log work   * Transition a state    Jira will delete the user, leaving the reference broken.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Add a user to LDAP;   # Sync so it shows up in Jira;   # Log in the user;   # Perform one of these actions:   ** Watch an issue   ** Vote for an issue   ** Log work   ** Transition a state   # Delete the user from LDAP;   # Sync the LDAP directory.    h3. Expected Results    Either:   * The users should be set as inactive;   * Or the entries should be cleaned up.    h3. Actual Results    The users are removed, leaving behind broken entries.   * Watcher:  !image-2020-10-07-17-18-04-660.png|width=197,height=145!   * Voter:  !image-2020-10-07-17-19-58-154.png!   * Work log:  !image-2020-10-07-17-21-35-389.png!   * History:  !image-2020-10-07-17-21-23-229.png!    h3. Workaround    If you wish for a user to be made inactive, please ensure that at least one comment was made by that user."

summary: Using function 'membersOf' throws error when using 'assignee was in' clause likely due to inactive users.
description: "h3. Problem definition  'membersOf' function does not properly support ""WAS IN"" and other keywords.    Using function 'membersOf' throws error when using 'assignee was in' clause, likely due to inactive users. Although the JQL function does usually give results with active users and ""WAS IN"" keyword, these keywords are not supported to use with this function and there is no guarantee that it will work properly.    Applying the JQL query returns the following error:     !image-2020-10-07-13-36-41-148.png|thumbnail!     h3. Suggested resolution  Implement proper support for ""WAS IN"" and other keywords listed on [Advanced searching \- functions reference \- Jira \- Atlassian documentation \-  membersOf()|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirasoftwareserver/advanced-searching-functions-reference-939938746.html#Advancedsearching-functionsreference-membersOfmembersOf()] documentation page, that are currently listed as unsupported. "

summary: Please correct the official documentation about sharing filters

They can also be shared globally. You can choose whether you want to share the filter with the permission to edit, or only to view. Any filter that is shared is visible to users who have the 'Jira Administrators' global permission  {quote}    However while it is possible to share a filter with a group, any logged in user, every user in the roles of a project, or to any specific role within a project, it is not possible to share a filter with a single specific user.    Please correct the documentation about it."

summary: Reindex fails because of NPE thrown by CustomFieldValues due to NULL value
description: "h3. Issue Summary  For customers having any records in *customfieldvalue* DB table with *customfield* value set to null, the reindex will stop with the error.    *Note:* We expect a small number of instances suffering from this problem (as values should be assigned to custom fields to be correctly processed). However, the severity is critical as there is no workaround when it occurs (besides removing corrupted data if they are not used).    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Have records in _customfieldvalue_ table with a null value for _customfield_ column   # Got to System->Indexing and trigger reindex (background or full)    h3. Expected Results  Reindex finished without any errors.    h3. Actual Results  Reindex finished with the error: *The re-index did not complete successfully. Please refer to Jira logs for more details.*    The below exception is thrown in the atlassian-jira.log file:    * Note: ""Caused by: java.lang.NullPointerException: element cannot be mapped to a null key""    h3. Workaround  * Validate where corrupted data come from and if you need them (like 'SELECT * from customfieldvalue WHERE customfield is null')  * If you don't need these data, make DB backup and delete rows returned by the previous query.  * If for some reasons you are not able to wipe them out, please upgrade to LTS release where this problem is solved."
text: Reindex fails because of NPE thrown by CustomFieldValues due to NULL value [SEP] """h3. Issue Summary  For customers having any records in *customfieldvalue* DB table with *customfield* value set to null, the reindex will stop with the error.    *Note:* We expect a small number of instances suffering from this problem (as values should be assigned to custom fields to be correctly processed). However, the severity is critical as there is no workaround when it occurs (besides removing corrupted data if they are not used).    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Have records in _customfieldvalue_ table with a null value for _customfield_ column   # Got to System->Indexing and trigger reindex (background or full)    h3. Expected Results  Reindex finished without any errors.    h3. Actual Results  Reindex finished with the error: *The re-index did not complete successfully. Please refer to Jira logs for more details.*    The below exception is thrown in the atlassian-jira.log file:    * Note: """"Caused by: java.lang.NullPointerException: element cannot be mapped to a null key""""    h3. Workaround  * Validate where corrupted data come from and if you need them (like 'SELECT * from customfieldvalue WHERE customfield is null')  * If you don't need these data, make DB backup and delete rows returned by the previous query.  * If for some reasons you are not able to wipe them out, please upgrade to LTS release where this problem is solved."""

This migration task moves data from {{audit_log}}, {{audit_item}} and {{audit_changed_value}} to the new table {{AO_C77861_AUDIT_ENTITY}}  h4. Why this property is useful    The property should restrict the amount of entries that are migrated to the Advanced Audit Log system.    The migration task should not block the usage of Jira, however there are reports that the task is blocking the usage of Jira for non-ZDU deployment in JRASERVER-71075    This feature could be a valuable asset for customer with large audit log row count to minimise time taken to upgrade.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # On Jira version <8.8.0 (eg 8.5.5) create a project called TEST   # Create many audit log entries. Example Script:       # Create or modify JIRA_HOME/jira-config.properties with:       # Shutdown Jira   # Upgrade Jira installation to version >8.8.0, example, 8.12.0   # Start Jira to initiate upgrade    h3. Expected Results   * Around 10 rows are migrated   **        * 10 previous history is shown in Jira Admin -> System -> Audit Log (/plugins/servlet/audit)   * Logging from AuditingMigrator and AuditingMigratorLimitSupplier reports that limit is set to 10 rows    h3. Actual Results   * All rows are migrated       * All previous history is shown in Jira Admin -> System -> Audit Log (/plugins/servlet/audit)   * Logging from AuditingMigrator and AuditingMigratorLimitSupplier reports that limit is 10000000 / 9999000      h3. Workaround    Provide the property directly as a command line argument or via {{setenv.sh }}\ {{setenv.bat}} file. Example:  "
