summary: Provide a user friendly way to identify security issues in a specific version of Jira.
description: "Currently, there are security advisories and security reports in Jira.atlassian.com, but when a Jira admin is looking for all the security issues that affect the version they are using it becomes difficult and time-consuming as it requires:  # Finding all the security advisories, check each one and see if their version is affected.  # Going to Jira.atlassian.com and making a search filter with the security label, and due to the nature of the field ""Affected versions"" a Jira version might be affected but not be indicated in the bug report (let's say that Jira 8.5.3 is affected but the report only has been done in 8.5.4), and since the real versions affected are not included in the bug reports, only the versions that have actually been reported, and due to the fact that there no way to search for versions ""newer than"" or ""older than"", it becomes an unreliable way to check for vulnerabilities.    The suggestion is to provide a web page, or a feature in Jira to easily obtain the list of security issues that are affecting an instance.    This request can also be extended to all the Atlassian server products."
text: Provide a user friendly way to identify security issues in a specific version of Jira. [SEP] """Currently, there are security advisories and security reports in Jira.atlassian.com, but when a Jira admin is looking for all the security issues that affect the version they are using it becomes difficult and time-consuming as it requires:  # Finding all the security advisories, check each one and see if their version is affected.  # Going to Jira.atlassian.com and making a search filter with the security label, and due to the nature of the field """"Affected versions"""" a Jira version might be affected but not be indicated in the bug report (let's say that Jira 8.5.3 is affected but the report only has been done in 8.5.4), and since the real versions affected are not included in the bug reports, only the versions that have actually been reported, and due to the fact that there no way to search for versions """"newer than"""" or """"older than"""", it becomes an unreliable way to check for vulnerabilities.    The suggestion is to provide a web page, or a feature in Jira to easily obtain the list of security issues that are affecting an instance.    This request can also be extended to all the Atlassian server products."""

summary: Sharing a dashboard with a user group after removing "any logged-in user" will not allow the group to be added
description: "h3. Issue Summary    When creating a dashboard, with either a new configuration or a copied one, groups cannot be added when ""any logged-in user"" is present and removed.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Create a new dashboard   # Add ""any logged-in user"" to the share group   # Remove the shared access for ""any logged-in user""   # Attempt to add the new group    h3. Expected Results    The group is added to the share permissions  h3. Actual Results    Jira does not respond   [^GHS-200939 - New bug.mov]  h3. Workaround    Leave ""any logged-in user"" in the share permissions and add the new group. Jira should overwrite the value."

summary: Adding a Condition while simultaneously changing a Transition on a Workflow results in a corrupted Workflow
description: "h3. Issue Summary    If you add a condition to a Workflow Transition while also saving that Transition to a new destination Status while Jira Data Center is under load, it results in a corrupted Workflow XML and the Workflows page will subsequently fail to load.     _Bug for result_: JRASERVER-68050 - Broken XML causes workflow admin page to crash  h3. Steps to Reproduce    _Unable to reproduce in sandbox as this requires Jira Data Center under load conditions._   1. Edit a Workflow Diagram in Chrome browser, say on Tab 1   2. Click on a workflow transition arrow (say TRANSITION-1) - various options available for the transition appears on the right   3. Right click on Conditions and click open in a new tab, say on Tab 2   4. On Tab 1, change either the source or destination of TRANSITION-1. Do not click save yet.   5. On Tab 2, click 'add condition' -> select 'User Is In Any Groups (JSU)' -> scroll & select a desired group under 'Available groups' section -> click 'Add >>' button which shows the group under ""Allowed groups"" section -> Click 'Add' at the bottom   6. Simultaneously, in a quick succession, On Tab 1, click 'Save' button  h3. Expected Results    The Workflow will save with both the new Condition and the new Transition destination Status.   h3. Actual Results    The below exception is thrown in the atlassian-jira.log file:    h3. Workaround    The fix is documented here:    * [Fixing workflows with duplicate occurrance of actions|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirakb/fixing-workflows-with-duplicate-occurance-of-action-781386551.html]    Although the corrupted Worklow result was documented, as well as the fix, the cause has not been documented, therefore we opened this bug. "

summary: Allow the creation of a "Defined" or "Ready for Development" column outside the sprint.
description: "I need a column that is between the backlog and the sprint.    Call it ""Ready for Development"" or ""Defined"" or whatever.    The Backlog is where new requirements land.    The ""Defined/Ready for Development"" column is where they move after they have been force ranked and estimated.    Sprint planning involves pulling from the ""Defined/Ready for Development"".    Having this ability is a huge benefit to managing the project and should be considered a mandatory feature."

summary: On changing assignee gets error " Failed to save issue number" but the issue gets assigned anyways
description: "h3. Issue Summary   * User does not want to give browse project permission to users .    * For example, a user X is created that belong to the group ""jira-software users""   * Under the browse project permission, he has only given permission to the role ""current assignee""   * He has given assign issue permission, edit issue to the current assignee and assignable user permission to Jira -software users   * Now the administrator assigns the issue to User-X and on logging User-X is able to see the issue   * When this user assigns the issue back to the administrator or any other user he gets the following error: ""Failed to save the issue number , You are not allowed to see the issue""   * Failed to save the issue number seems a little contradictory as the issue gets eventually reassigned to the respective administrators or users    * Also another thing to note here is that we don't get an error when the issue is assigned using *assign* option but we get the error when the issue is assigned using *assignee* option    h3. Steps to Reproduce    I have all steps to replicate the issue above and a few more screenshot given below   h3. Expected Results    You are not allowed to see the issue is correct but Failed to save the issue number is incorrect   h3. Actual Results    Incorrect error message displayed creates confusion    h3. Workaround    No workaround available "

summary: Users cannot adapt contrasts to their needs.
description: "✓ The Windows colors from high contrast mode are adopted in Firefox.   Google Chrome does not support the use of Windows colors, which is why settings such as high-contrast mode do not work.         → A separate style switcher with high-contrast mode could be integrated into the application, which users can activate and configure themselves if necessary.   → The users must be informed at a suitable point (e.g. on the start page / dashboard) that the high-contrast add-on from Google Chrome must be installed in order to have a high-contrast mode in Google Chrome.   → The users must be informed at a suitable point (e.g. on the start page / dashboard) that Mozilla Firefox has a high-contrast mode that supports Windows colors. Both browsers are available for Telekom employees."

summary: Please show progress in percentage for upgrade instead of file names
description: "Hi,    Today we upgraded our staging Jira server from 7.13 to 8.5 as a precursor practice for the upcoming upgrading on production server next month.    Everything went on smoothly, except that the upgrade package atlassian-jira-software-8.5.8-x64.bin shows the avatar file names during the process, which is a nuisance.    # We cloned everything from the production system to this staging system a couple months ago so that we could do the stress test. Understandably, this system now has a huge lot of accounts / avatars, just like the production system has.  # A huge portion of our engineers using the system are Chinese and Japanese. So most of them tend to name their avatar filenames using Chinese / Japanese characters.  # Our Jira server is hosted on AWS EC2 node which runs CentOS. We use PuTTY to log on it and do the maintenance, upgrading included.  # Clearly the Linux TTY console knows nothing about Chinese / Japanese characters. So those characters are just misinterpreted as control characters.    So when the upgrade package was doing its thing and showing those supposedly avatar file names, all strange gibberish popped up everywhere in the terminal and the cursor was moving to all directions like it was possessed. Though it might be entertaining (which it really was), what bothered us the most is that we could hardly know it actually stopped and asked us whether we wish to start Jira or exit (the last stage of upgrading) because the message was embedded in the middle of a screen full of ""funny"" characters.    Also, I can understand the need to show the Jira bundled files such as those JAR files, etc, so that if anything goes wrong we could see where it stops. But showing avatar file names really doesn't help much (they are provided by the users anyway, and they are plain data in a sense). I would suggest future Jira upgrade package should instead show a percentage of the portion of total avatar files being processed, not showing each one of their names.    Thank you for reading thus far. Hope you guys can consider this enhancement on future upgrade package. Hope you and your family are doing well!    Best Regards  Tony Chu  TrendMicro TW HQ"

summary: Some graphics do not have alternative text.
description: "{color:#172b4d}Graphics cannot be perceived by blind users and cannot be interpreted by screen readers. Instead, screen readers output alternative texts that were specified for the image. The following problems arise in this context:{color}  {color:#172b4d} On some pages, the screen reader does not output alternative texts for some images because they are not marked with the alt attribute, for example the first icons of the sprint issues in the backlog or the same icons in the active sprint. With these elements, the alternative text is implemented on the surrounding elements and is not output by the screen reader. (see picture1){color}  {color:#172b4d}  {color}  {color:#172b4d} → The icons must have an <alt> attribute that corresponds to the content of the <title> attribute of the surrounding element.{color}  {color:#172b4d}  {color}  {color:#172b4d} The arrows between the status (e.g. ""Open"" to ""In Progress)"" in the ""Activity"" area in an issue view in the ""Transition"" tab are implemented without the alt attribute (see picture 2). ""{color}  {color:#172b4d} → The icons must be provided with an <alt> attribute, which contains a descriptive and content-related alternative text.{color}  {color:#172b4d}  {color}  {color:#172b4d}  {color}  {color:#172b4d}   {color}  {color:#172b4d} In the issue view, all icons for opening and closing the individual sections are missing a descriptive alternative text. For screen reader users, “Number” is displayed.{color}  {color:#172b4d}  {color}  {color:#172b4d} These graphical control elements are links with embedded svg graphics, to which a description cannot be assigned with a classic text alternative as an <alt> at-tribute.{color}  {color:#172b4d} → Instead, an aria label attribute must be assigned.{color}  {color:#172b4d}  {color}  {color:#172b4d}  {color}"

For screen reader users, “Number” is displayed.{color}  {color:#172b4d}  {color}  {color:#172b4d} These graphical control elements are links with embedded svg graphics, to which a description cannot be assigned with a classic text alternative as an <alt> at-tribute.{color}  {color:#172b4d} → Instead, an aria label attribute must be assigned.{color}  {color:#172b4d}  {color}  {color:#172b4d}  {color}"""

summary: JIRA REST API with JWT authentication
description: "We can confirm after a conversation with the DEV team, there is *no support* for server querying of the *JIRA REST API* with *JWT* authentication.      The DEV team is working to implement *oAuth2.0* for the Jira server, but currently, only *Jira cloud* has support for *JWT*.    (?) Can we have a feature that makes Jira consume JWT token for REST APIs usage?    Something similar to the JWT authentication which is being used on Jira Cloud, but, in preference something more ""generic"" that users can integrate with their own apps.    Feature Request created based on [PS-66118|https://getsupport.atlassian.com/browse/PS-66118]    We can see it's implemented for Confluence as well: [https://developer.atlassian.com/server/confluence/How-to-make-REST-API-endpoints-available-for-JWT-authentication/]"

summary: Deleting archived component leaves orphan issue association
description: "h3. Issue Summary    Deleting components that have been archived does not remove the Issue association from the nodeassociation table.    This can lead to gadgets showing a different number of issues without components than what's seen in the Issue navigator.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Add a component to an issue   # Archive the component   # Delete the component    h3. Expected Results    Component is deleted and any association to it is removed  h3. Actual Results    Component is deleted but nodeassociation table still has the link between the issues and component.  h3. Workaround   # Delete the orphaned rows manually with the SQL query below  {code:SQL}delete from nodeassociation where source_node_entity = 'Issue' and sink_node_entity = 'Component' and sink_node_id not in (select id from component);{code}   # Reindex Jira  "

summary: Update document part regarding jira.text.field.character.limit
description: "In the document [https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiraserver/configuring-advanced-settings-938847828.html], it says:  |jira.text.field.character.limit|This property limits the number of characters that can be entered into *Description*, *Environments,* *Comments* and *text custom fields.* The maximum is 2147483647. A value of 0 means unlimited characters.|    The part that regards text custom fields could be misleading.   * Single line text custom fields are *not* impacted by this variable and are always limited to 255 characters.   * Multi-line text custom fields will have the max length defined by this variable.    So, my suggestion is:  |jira.text.field.character.limit|This property limits the number of characters that can be entered into *Description*, *Environments,* *Comments* and *multi-line text custom fields.* The maximum is 2147483647. A value of 0 means unlimited characters.|"

summary: No validation and wrong date when manually inserting an unexpected year format in a date picker field
description: "h3. Issue Summary    When setting a date format with a long year format (e.g *dd.MM.yyyy*) and manually inserting a date with a short year format (for instance 15.12.19) in a date picker field, the date gets inserted as of 2 days earlier (in this example 13.12.19). This is similar to the issue reported in JRASERVER-36354 but we can see here that the date is inconsistent.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Go to _Administration > General Configuration > Advanced_.   # Set the *jira.date.picker.java.format* to *dd.MM.yyyy*.   !DatePickerFormat.png|width=215,height=92!   # Create or edit an issue and manually type a number date in a date picker field (e.g the Due Date field) with format *dd.mm.yy* such as 15.12.19 for example and save the change.   !TestDateFormat.png|width=189,height=183!    h3. Expected Results    The inserted value should be rejected since it doesn't respect the format specified in Jira, or should be accepted as it is *15.12.19*, or converted to a long-year-format *15.12.2019*.  h3. Actual Results    The date inserted shows as of 2 days earlier i.e *13.12.19*.   !WrongTestDateFormat.png|width=307,height=141!  h3. Note    This issue happens with any date format where Jira expects *yyyy* but the year inserted is in the short format *yy.*  h3. Workaround    As a workaround, you may use scripted fields and validate the insertion or date picker fields provided by plugins."

summary: Position of the issue in the sprintboard not recognizable
description: "If content is not specified in a logical order in the source code, screen reader users cannot establish contextual connections and a meaningful reading order of the content is not given. This may mean that makes it difficult to operate and understand the structure of the application.         Blind users cannot see which issue is displayed in which column of the active sprint because visual labels and swimlanes are implemented in structurally independent lists. As a result, all headings and then all swimlanes are output by the screen reader when navigating linearly. Since there is no information stored for the individual issues as to which column of the board they are assigned to, the status of an issue is no longer recognizable for blind users (see picture1).         A blind user is not able to see the column position of the current box within a swim lane.    → The entire structure must be presented in a way that enables the individual columns in the swimlanes to be assigned to the higher-level headings. This could be done by representation in a data table.   → Alternatively, the issues could be related to the swim lanes using hidden, screen-reader-accessible information on the issue boxes.          → The order of the objects must be adapted for the logically correct sequence of the keyboard control."

summary: Force the limit on number of emails sent using password reset option in Jira from "can't access your account?" page.
description: "*_+Scenario:+_*   The link for resetting a password allows users to send unlimited mails. If hackers use this to automate the repeated actions using scripts, this might lead to E-mail bombs hack may create Denial of service (DoS) conditions against e-mail software and even network and Internet connection by taking up a large amount of bandwidth and, sometimes, requiring large amounts of storage space.    +_*Steps to reproduce the scenario:*_+   - Try to login to Jira using <JIRA-Base_URL> and click on can't access your account?   - Select a user account and then send an email to the user and try the same operation multiple times.   - We are able to send number of emails using this scenario.   - We agree that such cases might create mail flooding, E-mail bombs hack may create Denial of service (DoS) conditions.    _+*Suggestions:*+_   - Allow the administrators to set limit on number of emails sent per user using ""can't access your account"" page.   - Allow the administrators to enable a captcha if the number of emails sent for reset password crosses certain threshold (configurable)."

Steps to Reproduce   # One project with comments placed within a wide range of time.   # Create an activity stream gadget with the following filters:   ** {{Project is PROJECT}}    ** {{Activity is Comment Added}}   ** {{Limit to X items}} (!) summary: Restrict the suggestion list when choosing the Board Administrator
description: "When a User creates a board (Scrum or Kanban) and then wants to choose the Board administrator, every user in the Jira instance can be seen and not only the users in the project scope. The suggestion list will provide all the groups and users. As a result of this, the User is able to see all the users and groups in the Jira instance. The suggestion list should be restricted to the users who have browse permission or associated users in that project. "

description: "h3. Issue Summary  Issues cannot be deleted if there is/are other linked issue(s) to it.   h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Create issue A  # Create issue B (or more)  # Link issue B (and others) to issue A  # Delete issue A    h3. Expected Results  The issue is deleted successfully  h3. Actual Results  The issue cannot be deleted. It's timed out on UI. When we review the thread dumps, we see N+1 (N is the number of linked issue to issue A) active threads with the stack trace below:    There will also be locker sessions in the database with the SQL below:    h3. Workaround  Delete the locker sessions in the database so that the threads waiting for these locks can continue to delete the issue."

summary: As a Data Center Administrator, I want to see more detailed and actionable reports in Clustering plugin

The plugin can be extended to provide more value.    *Suggested solutions*    - Show node version (like Jira 8.6, Jira 8.5; helpful during the zero-downtime upgrade)  - In Jira DC: split Custom Fields Indexing report into two separate tables; enrich the report, show the data on the chart.  - Add the cluster management feature: removing the node from the cluster, moving the node to an offline state. summary: Users and Roles screen renders invalid link in React mode resulting in 404 error
description: "h3. Issue Summary    Users and Roles screen in the project settings will render invalid link in React mode (default since 8.60). The link rendered for users is always like this:    <base url>/jira/secure/ViewProfile.jpsa?name=<user>    The *jira* part of URL is invalid, since the base URL is set to something like <base URL>/myjiraserver/ and the link should respect that. Note that when the base URL does not have any URL suffix, the *jira* part is still rendered, though when clicked you can still get where you want, but if you have a context path specified in your jira, you will get HTTP 404 error.    h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Go to any project settings -> Users and Roles  # Add any user to the table and check the link rendered for it.    h3. Expected Results    The link rendered should respect the context path.    h3. Actual Results    Context path rendered always as *jira*, if clicked you might get 404 if your Jira is using a different context path.    h3. Workaround    As a temporary workaround, you can disable rendering of that screen through React and revert to the previous way of rendering:  - Navigate to the following page to access the Dark Features page:    - Enter *jira.users.and.roles.page.in.react.disabled* in the field “Enable dark feature” and clicking on the “Add” button, and verify that the dark feature *jira.users.and.roles.page.in.react.disabled* is showing in the table."

summary: Archived Issues page automatically performs a count from all active projects
description: "h3. Issue Summary    A count of all archived issues in active projects is automatically run when loading the archived issue page even before a search is run. No issues, counts, or other information is displayed on the page when this query is executed.     h3. Steps to Reproduce  # Enable SQL logging   # Load the Archived Issues page (secure/BrowseArchivedIssues!default.jspa)   # Check for count query in logs   # It's important to note that the count is cached(appears to be for 30 minutes). The query can be triggered again if you archive a new issue and then refresh the page  # No results or count are displayed on the page at this point    h3. Expected Results    We'd expect that a query is not run until a search/filter is submitted. Depending on the filter, it may not be necessary to go through all active projects    h3. Actual Results    A count of all archived issues is automatically run for every active project:        In the test case there are only two active projects. In larger instances though, this will be a much larger query.     Because of this the Archived Issues page can take a long time to load in larger environments.    h3. Workaround    Add the following index to Jira's database:      This will increase the query speed time from minutes to seconds and allow the Archived Issues page to load quickly"

Issue Summary    A count of all archived issues in active projects is automatically run when loading the archived issue page even before a search is run. The query can be triggered again if you archive a new issue and then refresh the page  # No results or count are displayed on the page at this point    h3. Expected Results    We'd expect that a query is not run until a search/filter is submitted. Depending on the filter, it may not be necessary to go through all active projects    h3. Actual Results    A count of all archived issues is automatically run for every active project:        In the test case there are only two active projects. In larger instances though, this will be a much larger query.     Because of this the Archived Issues page can take a long time to load in larger environments.    h3. Workaround    Add the following index to Jira's database:      This will increase the query speed time from minutes to seconds and allow the Archived Issues page to load quickly"""

summary: Asynchronous Webhooks in Jira
description: "For Jira Data Center, currently webhooks are built to occur synchronously, which can lead to a backlog of requests, and can negatively impact performance. Some large organizations have banned webhooks for integrations as the requests have caused slower performance and requests that do not occur if they are not performed in a timely manner.         If webhooks could occur asynchronously, then there would be a significant decrease on the impact to organizations using webhooks within Jira for additional integrations with other tools."
text: Asynchronous Webhooks in Jira [SEP] """For Jira Data Center, currently webhooks are built to occur synchronously, which can lead to a backlog of requests, and can negatively impact performance. Some large organizations have banned webhooks for integrations as the requests have caused slower performance and requests that do not occur if they are not performed in a timely manner.         If webhooks could occur asynchronously, then there would be a significant decrease on the impact to organizations using webhooks within Jira for additional integrations with other tools."""
label: design

summary: Search Index for Archived issues
description: "h3. Currently, when issues are archived within Jira, they are not placed into the normal operating search index. While efficient for operations, this does limit the ability to search through the archives. It can be painfully slow when there are tens and hundreds of thousands of issues.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # On a large Jira DC instance, archive 20,000 or more issues   # Try to search for an archived issue   # It will be painfully slow and in some cases impossible to search    h3. Expected Results    Although it may not have been included in the design, it would be nice to have a separate index created when archiving issues. Given the nature of archived issues, this could be a separate index created on a schedule that does not require daily reindex  h3. Actual Results    It appears that archived issues are not indexed, and searches can take an unacceptably long time to return items.  h3. Workaround       Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior.  h3. Suggestion    If there were a separate index and schedulable task to create an index once issues have been archived, it could make locating these issues easier for gathering information when required."

summary: Running  /rest/agile/1.0/epic/none/issue without parameters on a jira instance can cause OOM,  high CPU and full GC
description: "h3. Issue Summary    [/rest/agile/1.0/epic/none/issue|https://docs.atlassian.com/jira-software/REST/7.3.1/#agile/1.0/epic-getIssuesWithoutEpic] pulls back all issues that don't have an epic, even though the results are paginated it appears the call itself is inefficient and can cause performance issues in JIRA. In an enterprise instance, this can be a lot of issues, leading to high memory usage, GC thrashing, high CPU and in some cases outofmemory errors. If you run more than one call at a time it can completely take down Jira forcing a full restart to kill the calls.  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # run /rest/agile/1.0/epic/none/issue on an instance with 500k+ issues (unsubstantiated it may be possible to affect a smaller instance with this call)    h3. Expected Results    The results will be returned without causing performance issues.  h3. Actual Results    *CPU and memory are maxed:*    !image-2020-09-16-16-06-35-840.png|thumbnail!   *Full GCs and OOM is possible in an Enterprise instance:*    !image-2020-09-15-09-34-42-548.png|thumbnail!  h3. Workaround    Adding parameters to the call will narrow down the search and thus the number of issues returned.    So instead of running the call without parameters, you could append a JQL search to narrow the total results:    # Results without parameters:        # Results with parameters:  "

summary: Opening Advanced Audit Log displays "[...] problem loading audit events" flag
description: "h3. Issue Summary    After upgrading to Jira <8.10.0,8.12.1>, opening certain pages in Advanced Audit Log displays a warning message ""There was a a problem loading audit events"" and logs NullPointerException.  h4. Cause:    Jira 8.8.0 introduces Advanced Audit Log feature. During application upgrade, before the feature is enabled, audit log entries are migrated from legacy tables into new one. Several events were migrated incorrectly, populating {{null}} value for {{AuditAttribute}} class fields. Displaying audit log entries fails when event with such empty value is on the paginated list of events.    Affected incorrectly migrated events:  {code:java}  ServiceDeletedEvent  EmailQueueFlashedEvent  JiraUpgradeStartedEvent  JiraUpgradeCancelledEvent  JiraUpgradeApprovedEvent  JiraUpgradeFinishedEvent  JiraUpgradeFailedEvent  ServiceDeletedEvent  FieldScreenFieldMovedEvent  FieldScreenTabMovedEvent  UserAnonymizationStartedEvent  UserAnonymizationFinishedEvent {code}  h3. Steps to Reproduce   # On affected Jira version, upgraded from Jira 8.7.1 or older   # Navigate to ""Audit Log"" functionality   # Browse to pages with past audit entries that could contain one of above events.    h3. Expected Results    Audit events are displayed properly  h3. Actual Results    The below exception is thrown in the atlassian-jira.log file:    h3. Workaround   # If the error is already visible on the first page with audit entries, generate new entries by simply using the instance. Newly registered audit events should ""push out"" incorrectly migrated events to next pages unlocking general Advanced Audit Log view.   # Upgrade to Jira 8.12.2 or newer   # Contact Support in case none of above options is applicable."

summary: Validate Gravatar Server URLs
description: "h3. Problem Definition    Per [https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRASERVER-35902], Jira supports custom Gravatar Server URLs so that administrators may configure their own custom Gravatar services.    At this time, Jira does not validate the URL that is provided. It is possible to provide an invalid URL, which Jira then uses to form the request endpoints for users' Gravatars, which can end up with error responses such as an HTTP 404.    h3. Suggested Solution     When the administrator provides a custom Gravatar URL, Jira should validate that it follows a valid URL structure and points to a valid service.  "
text: Validate Gravatar Server URLs [SEP] """h3. Problem Definition    Per [https://jira.atlassian.com/browse/JRASERVER-35902], Jira supports custom Gravatar Server URLs so that administrators may configure their own custom Gravatar services.    At this time, Jira does not validate the URL that is provided. It is possible to provide an invalid URL, which Jira then uses to form the request endpoints for users' Gravatars, which can end up with error responses such as an HTTP 404.    h3. Suggested Solution     When the administrator provides a custom Gravatar URL, Jira should validate that it follows a valid URL structure and points to a valid service.  """
label: non-design

summary: As a PO or Scrum Master I would like to be able to rank issues independently from a backlog
description: "As a PO or Scrum Master role, it is important to be able to easily search, filter and rank issues to make sure priorities are kept up to date, both on an agile board backlog as well as for in-progress issues. Currently, the only way to modify the rank field is via an agile board, which is tedious and cumbersome at best - and sometimes impossible to do at worst.         I have 2 specific use cases that I have encountered that help illustrate some of the problems I am describing.         Case #1: ranking issues across multiple boards    Suppose you have 2 agile teams working on a single project, each with their own backlogs and dashboards. When working on each team individually you can often use the backlog view for each teams' board to prioritize issues, but it is impossible to rank issues across the boards so the entire list (ie: all work for the entire project) is correctly ranked. Technically you can work around this limitation by rolling a custom agile board solely for the purpose of ranking cross-team work like this, or you can purchase an expensive plugin like Advanced Roadmaps, to provide a workaround but neither of these really solves the underlying problem well. Creating boards solely for ranking purposes can lead to confusion, especially if team members find and start using the wrong board. Further, using Advanced Roadmaps plans are often restricted to executive and management level staff, and thus aren't accessible by the development teams or POs making permission management more difficult.         Case #2: ranking unresolved issues    This issue is mostly applicable to teams making use of Kanban style agile boards. When issues are in flight (ie: not open or closed) they may appear in any number of states or columns on an  agile board. There are tools which will allow you to rank issues on these boards, but they are quite limited. For example, if you are using swim lanes to sort the issues (ie: based on assignee or component, say) there is no way to rank an issue from one swim lane in relation to issues from another swim lane. Similarly, if the issues appear in different columns (ie: to rank ""in progress"" issues in relation to ""in testing"" issues) there again is no way to rank those issues relative to one another.    Why is ranking of in-progress issues important? Aren't you typically supposed to define your rank from the backlog before work begins? In a perfect world, yes, but in practice priorities change and it is important to give users the flexibility to adjust to those changes in priority as easily as possible. In particular, when reporting status information in dashboards and such it is often important for that data to be displayed accurately based on rank. This ensures that users of those reports can ensure that work is being done in the correct priority order, even when it is in progress.         *Request*    In order to make it easier to rank issues independently from an agile board, I propose adding a feature to the ""Filter Results"" dashboard gadget that comes with Jira out of the box to allow rows in those tables to be ranked using the same drag-and-drop mechanics that are used when ranking issues on the backlog for an agile board. In this way users could create filters on their data to represent whatever view they wish, and then adjust the rank of those issues relative to one another, directly in the dashboard.    So as to preserve backwards compatibility, this new feature could be made optional and disabled by default. Simply providing an ""enable ranking"" option in the gadget configuration would allow users to turn on this feature whenever it makes sense for them."

summary: Importing Epics, Epic Links and linked tickets cannot be done using single CSV file
description: "h4. Issue Summary    Importing Epics, Epic Links and linked tickets cannot be done using single CSV file. Two or more CSVs are required to import new issues and Epics and to link them.  h4. Steps to Reproduce   # Prepare a CSV file that contains new Epics, new or existing issues and Epic Links to link those two   # Import the data using Importer functionality    h4. Expected Results    Epics and corresponding issues are created and linked from the same CSV file.  h4. Actual Results    Links are not created after the import.    The below exception is thrown in the atlassian-jira.log file:    h4. Workaround    The workaround is described on the knowledge article [Cannot Import Epic Link from CSV due to Incorrect Format|https://confluence.atlassian.com/jirakb/cannot-import-epic-link-from-csv-due-to-incorrect-format-829030416.html?_ga=2.194238253.127964220.1598210088-1823618877.1573563865].    The idea is to:   * Use one CSV file to import new Epics and issues   * Use the second CSV file to import links between Epics and issues."

The idea is to:   * Use one CSV file to import new Epics and issues   * Use the second CSV file to import links between Epics and issues."""

summary: Improve the "Bundled" category of the Jira plugins' definition
description: "h2. Summary    Jira plugins can be classified as ""Bundled"" or ""User Installed"" in the plugin defintion shown either by the application.xml file (created when a support zip is generated) or the output generated by the UPM rest API end point (https://<HOST>:<PORT>/rest/plugins/1.0/).    Some of the plugins marked as ""User Installed"" are plugins that actually are packaged with the Jira product itself whereas other plugins installed by users are also set as ""User Installed"".   Hence it is not possible to differentiate from the outpus mentioned above which plugins are actually installed by users.  h2. Suggestion    *It would be good to have a new category for those plugins that are install by the users to be able to differentiate them from those that are part of the product and set as ""User Installed"".*  h2. Additional Details    The below excerpt from an application.xml file shows the case where a plugin is categorized as ""User installed"" although it was installed as part of the Jira product and not as a user action.  {code:xml}  <plugin>        <key>com.atlassian.jira.plugin.ext.bamboo</key>        <name>Jira Bamboo Plugin</name>        <version>8.7.1</version>        <vendor>Atlassian Software Systems Pty Ltd</vendor>        <status>ENABLED</status>        <vendor-url>http://www.atlassian.com</vendor-url>        <framework-version>2</framework-version>        <bundled>User installed</bundled>      </plugin>  {code}     The below is an excerpt from the output generated by querying the UPM rest API () and which shows the same {{Jira Bamboo Plugin}} categorized as *""userInstalled"":true*.     "

summary: Provide support for multiple identity providers (IdPs) in native DC SAML connector
description: "h3. Problem Definition  The native SAML connector in Jira Data Center only allows for the configuration of a single IdP. This is limiting for certain use cases, especially where external users may be authenticating to an instance via a different domain.     h3. Suggested Solution  Provide support for configuration multiple IdPs using the native SAML connector.     h3. Workaround   There are several third-party DC SAML/SSO plugins on the Atlassian Marketplace that allow for the configuration of multiple IdPs."

summary: REST API issue create endpoint should have the ability to suppress notifications
description: "h3. Problem Definition    Derived from previous JRASERVER-34423 - There are times when a Jira admin might need to create a large number of issues and choose to use the REST API as a means to do this. However you don't always want to send out notifications to all the users in the notification scheme when this takes place. It would be very helpful if the issue create endpoint api/2/issue had a means to do this, much like the edit issue endpoint (api/2/issue/\{issueIdOrKey} endpoint) has the notifyUsers query parameter.  h3. Suggested Solution    Implement the notifyUsers query parameter on the issue create endpoint as well.  h3. Why this is important    End users are easily annoyed by bulk issue creation. Many users have complained about Jira being overly chatty when it comes to email. This suggested solution would provide Jira admins a quick means to create issues without flooding the inbox of everyone on your team. This implementation method would still log issue histories and trigger other events/listeners just like notifyUsers currently works on api/2/issue/\{issueIdOrKey} endpoint.   The workaround can provide a solution, but it's not ideal because it is not granular enough to only limit notifications for this REST API call, it's overkill to completely change the notification scheme for this single issue creation event, and likely to leave the admin to forget to change it back.  h3. Workaround    It is possible for Jira admin to temporarily modify the notification scheme in use for that project so that no users are sent out notifications for such events as Issue Created, issue assigned, etc. However this work-around is not ideal as it prevents all such notifications for any project using that scheme."

Workaround    It is possible for Jira admin to temporarily modify the notification scheme in use for that project so that no users are sent out notifications for such events as Issue Created, issue assigned, etc. However this work-around is not ideal as it prevents all such notifications for any project using that scheme."""

summary: Extend Jira REST API for Changelog History
description: "As a typical developer, scripter, or user of ETL (extract, transform, load) tools, I need to ability to include parameters specific to Changelog History data in the REST API call so that I can limit the volume of JSON data returned.    We are a large enterprise using the REST API to pull Jira data into Alteryx to perform ETL functions in preparation for loading the transformed data into Tableau for reporting. Currently there is a large volume of Changelog History data returned when using ""...?expand=changelog"". Quite often the vast majority of changelog data is not needed or wanted, so we need the ability to limit what changelog data is returned in the following ways:   * Changes to a specific field or list of fields (both system and custom fields; could be a single field or a comma-separated list of fields)   * Changes made to a specific field match or are similar to <text>, e.g. ~""<text>""   * Changes made to a specific field equal an option or comma-separated list of options for that field   * Changes made to a specific field from one option to another option, e.g. from <option A> to <option B>   * Changes made within certain time frames, e.g.: -1w, EndOfMonth(), before or after <date>, between date range, etc."

Currently there is a large volume of Changelog History data returned when using """"...?expand=changelog"""". Quite often the vast majority of changelog data is not needed or wanted, so we need the ability to limit what changelog data is returned in the following ways:   * Changes to a specific field or list of fields (both system and custom fields; could be a single field or a comma-separated list of fields)   * Changes made to a specific field match or are similar to <text>, e.g. ~""""<text>""""   * Changes made to a specific field equal an option or comma-separated list of options for that field   * Changes made to a specific field from one option to another option, e.g. from <option A> to <option B>   * Changes made within certain time frames, e.g.: -1w, EndOfMonth(), before or after <date>, between date range, etc."""

summary: Issues may not be visible when using the REST API and pagination
description: "In the following scenario, when running the following REST API query to return issues within a certain range of ""Updated"" dates.    It'll return the following data, as example:  ||Key||Updated||  |SSD-1|01/Aug/2019|  |SSD-2|01/Aug/2019|  |SSD-3|01/Aug/2019|  |SSD-4|01/Aug/2019|  |SSD-5|01/Aug/2019|  |SSD-6|01/Aug/2019|  |SSD-7|01/Aug/2019|  |SSD-8|01/Aug/2019|  |SSD-9|01/Aug/2019|    When using {{maxResults=5}}, we have the following:    ||Key||Updated||  |SSD-1|01/Aug/2019|  |SSD-2|01/Aug/2019|  |SSD-3|01/Aug/2019|  |SSD-4|01/Aug/2019|  |SSD-5|01/Aug/2019|    Before browsing to page 2 (startAt=5), issue SSD-5 is modified, resulting in the Updated field to be changed, therefore, making this issue outside of the query scope.    Browsing to page 2, (startAt=5). We have the following output:    ||Key||Updated||  |SSD-7|01/Aug/2019|  |SSD-8|01/Aug/2019|  |SSD-9|01/Aug/2019|    Notice that SSD-6 is not visible in page 2, since SSD-5 was modified, SSD-6 shifted to the previous page.  h3. Expected behaviour    Jira should prevent this behaviour by making the returned issues consistent when using the REST API pagination."

summary: Jira should be able to add or create new status via a REST API endpoint
description: "h3. Problem Definition    When migrating issues from external trackers to Jira Server, it becomes very tedious to first have to create each of the issue statuses that you want to use before importing issues. For large environment that could have had several hundred different statuses in them it then takes a lot longer than it should to import this data correctly.  h3. Suggested Solution    Create REST API endpoint that allows users to create new statuses.  Currently the endpoint [GET /api/2/status|https://docs.atlassian.com/software/jira/docs/api/REST/8.2.1/#api/2/status-getStatuses] will return all statuses in Jira.  But there is no corresponding POST verb that could be used to create these same statuses via REST.    h3. Why this is important    Makes it easier to migrate data into Jira. CSV importer is not able to create new statuses within Jira, and the lack of a REST API endpoint to create a new status means more manual status creation work for an admin. With such a POST or PUT endpoint and admin could create a little REST script to simply call Jira and tell it specifically what statuses should be valid within that site.  h3. Workaround    none"

summary: Provide a way to whitelist IPs for REST API basic authentication
description: "h3. Problem Definition  * Currently Jira doesn't provide a way to restrict or whitelist source IPs (JRASERVER-67452) for remote API interaction at all. One of the challenges of implementing a workaround for this at proxy/network environment level is that the REST API URL patterns for the public API are also often used by requests triggered from the UI. So there is a risk of blocking normal UI based requests while intending to block remote API calls.    h3. Suggested Solution  One way to constrain remote API usage to some extent may be to limit or restrict the use of basic auth for REST API. Provide facility or configuration to restrict basic authentication requests to whitelisted IPs."

summary: Correct REST API Documentation for ReindexIssues
description: "h3. Summary    The REST API document ([https://docs.atlassian.com/software/jira/docs/api/REST/7.12.1/#api/2/reindex-reindexIssues]) mentions that the POST /rest/api/2/reindex/issue endpoint can take JQL as a parameter  {quote}Use either explicitly specified issue IDs or a JQL query to select issues to reindex.  {quote}  However, the endpoint does not explicitly take JQL as a parameter and does not work as described in the document.  h3. Analysis    If we look at the code for the rest endpoint, the logic is simply not there for JQL. It uses [*issueFinder|https://docs.atlassian.com/software/jira/docs/api/7.1.4/com/atlassian/jira/rest/v2/issue/IssueFinder.html] .getIssueObject(issueId))*, which only takes issue keys or id's:    h3. Suggested Implementation    Correct the documentation or add some details on how to make this work with JQL as outlined in the guide. Otherwise, add the logic to make this work with JQL using the traditional Jira syntax of JQL, E.G.   POST rest/api/2/reindex/issue?jql=project=PROJECTKEY"

summary: Controll access to Rest API
description: "h3. Task  I want to control which third party applications can access my Jira Server, both by white- or blacklisting. Thus only applications that are deemed trustworthy can access my Jira Server.      h3. How it could work  * An app must be signed by a valid vendor key and a valid key specific to this application to be able to access the Rest API.   * Jira admins can black or white list apps or a vendor (covering all apps of this vendor, existing and future).  * Access to Rest API is allowed only on basis of the black or white listings; or to all and everybody, if both are empty.    This would definitly be an infrastructure project, but it will appeal to corporations or organisations where more control over their system than usually is needed.    (!) This would be something worth considering across the whole product stack of Atlassian."

summary: Swagger (Open API) JSON or YAML for Jira REST API
description: "h1.Problem  It is very time consuming to hand-craft access to Jira REST API.    The provided API description is UI: https://developer.atlassian.com/cloud/jira/platform/rest    h1.Suggested Solution:  I want to generate a client automatically against a Swagger document.    For example I could use AutoRest to generate a client in C#, NodeJS, Python, Java, Ruby or Go: https://github.com/Azure/autorest.    Please could you publish Swagger document in JSON or YAML that describes Jira REST API?  {quote}  "

summary: What are the network prerequisite for slave and master hosted in different LAN?
description: "Hi everyone,    I am running mesos and marathon in the cloud using 3 masters+slaves dedicated server because it was the minimal requirement recommended by mesos.    Since 2015, I have not added any slaves to the <USER>by trying to keep the cloud spending low.    I recently bought a Synology NAS with storage and I want to host next to it a dedicated server that would run mesos and marathon.    I want to host them in my house, where my ISP provide a dedicated IP and a lot of bandwith.    I have never done such configuration and would like to know things that I should consider::    - Does the masters needs to know how to contact the slaves ?  - Do they need to be on the same network ?  - If they must be in the same network, can they use a tunnel to one OR more masters?  - Do I need to open zk ports on each master `2181` publicly in order to contact the `masters` and is it a security concerns to do so?  - Do I need to do port redirection on my zookeeper slaves?  - Is there any other network or security requirement or prerequisite?    Will this configuration works ?    Thanks for advising."

summary: Reintroduce image fetch deduplication while keeping it possible to destroy UCR containers in PROVISIONING state.
description: "The fix for https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/MESOS-9964 had to drop the image fetch deduplication that was introduced long ago in https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/MESOS-3736. We should reintroduce the deduplication back.    UCR containers pulling the same image at the same time should pull the image only once.  *This deduplication should not prevent killing some of the PROVISIONING containers, and such killing should not interfere with provisioning of other containers.*    I would argue that it is OK for all the containers depending on a single image pull to hang if this single pull hangs.        "

summary: Avoid sending framework updates to agents and subscribers when frameworkInfo/pid didn't change.
description: "Currently, FrameworkInfo is broadcast to agents and V1API events subscribers on every framework resubscription/update, regardless of whether it has actually changed or not.    When schedulers frequently call UPDATE_FRAMEWORK to only update subscription settings (the list of suppressed roles, and, after implementing https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/MESOS-10161, offer constraints), the FrameworkInfo broadcast on every UPDATE_FRAMEWORK call becomes undesirable.    Sending 'pid' update to agents is affected by the same issue."

summary: Implement a new component to launch CSI plugins as standalone containers and make CSI gRPC calls
description: "*Background:*    Originally we want `volume/csi` isolator to leverage the existing [service manager|https://github.com/apache/mesos/blob/1.10.0/src/csi/service_manager.hpp#L50:L51] to launch CSI plugins as standalone containers and currently service manager needs to call the following agent HTTP APIs:   # `GET_CONTAINERS` to get all standalone containers in its `recover` method.   # `KILL_CONTAINER` and `WAIT_CONTAINER` to kill the outdated standalone containers in its `recover` method.   # `LAUNCH_CONTAINER` via the existing [ContainerDaemon|https://github.com/apache/mesos/blob/1.10.0/src/slave/container_daemon.hpp#L41:L46] to launch CSI plugin as standalone container when its `getEndpoint` method is called.    The problem with the above design is, `volume/csi` isolator may need to clean up orphan container during agent recovery which is triggered by containerizer (see [here|https://github.com/apache/mesos/blob/1.10.0/src/slave/containerizer/mesos/containerizer.cpp#L1272:L1275] for details), to clean up an orphan container which is using a CSI volume, `volume/csi` isolator needs to instantiate and recover the service manager and get CSI plugin’s endpoint from it (i.e., service manager’s `getEndpoint` method will be called by `volume/csi` isolator during agent recovery. And as I mentioned above service manager’s `getEndpoint` may need to call `LAUNCH_CONTAINER` to launch CSI plugin as standalone container, since agent is still in recovering state, such agent HTTP call will be just rejected by agent. So we have to instantiate and recover service manager *after agent recovery is done*, but in `volume/csi` isolator we do not have such information (i.e. the signal that agent recovery is done).    *Solution*    We need to implement a new component (like `CSIVolumeManager` or a better name?) in Mesos agent which is responsible for launching CSI plugins as standalone containers (via the existing [service manager|https://github.com/apache/mesos/blob/1.10.0/src/csi/service_manager.hpp#L50:L51]) and making CSI gRPC calls (via the existing [volume manager|https://github.com/apache/mesos/blob/1.10.0/src/csi/volume_manager.hpp#L55:L56]).   * We can instantiate this new component in the `main` method of agent and pass it to both containerizer and agent (i.e. it will be a member of the `Slave` object), and containerizer will in turn pass it to the `volume/csi` isolator.   * Since this new component relies on service manager which will call agent HTTP APIs, we need to pass agent URL to it, like `process::http::URL(scheme, agentIP, agentPort, agentLibprocessId + ""/api/v1"")`, see [here|https://github.com/apache/mesos/blob/1.10.0/src/slave/slave.cpp#L459:L471] for an example.   * When agent registers/reregisters with master (`Slave::registered` and `Slave::reregistered`), we should call this new component’s `start` method (see [here|https://github.com/apache/mesos/blob/1.10.0/src/slave/slave.cpp#L1740:L1742] and [here|https://github.com/apache/mesos/blob/1.10.0/src/slave/slave.cpp#L1825:L1827] as examples) which will scan the directory `--csi_plugin_config_dir` and create the `service manager - volume manager` pair for each CSI plugin loaded from that directory.   * For the `volume/csi` isolator, it needs to call this new component’s `publishVolume` and `unpublishVolume` methods in its `prepare` and `cleanup` method.    In the case of clean up orphan containers during agent recovery, `volume/csi` isolator will just call this new component’s `unpublishVolume` method as usual, and it is this new component’s responsibility to only make the actual CSI gRPC call after agent recovery is done and agent has registered with master (e.g., when this new component’s start method is called)."

And as I mentioned above service manager’s `getEndpoint` may need to call `LAUNCH_CONTAINER` to launch CSI plugin as standalone container, since agent is still in recovering state, such agent HTTP call will be just rejected by agent. So we have to instantiate and recover service manager *after agent recovery is done*, but in `volume/csi` isolator we do not have such information (i.e. the signal that agent recovery is done).    *Solution*    We need to implement a new component (like `CSIVolumeManager` or a better name?) in Mesos agent which is responsible for launching CSI plugins as standalone containers (via the existing [service manager|https://github.com/apache/mesos/blob/1.10.0/src/csi/service_manager.hpp#L50:L51]) and making CSI gRPC calls (via the existing [volume manager|https://github.com/apache/mesos/blob/1.10.0/src/csi/volume_manager.hpp#L55:L56]).   * We can instantiate this new component in the `main` method of agent and pass it to both containerizer and agent (i.e. it will be a member of the `Slave` object), and containerizer will in turn pass it to the `volume/csi` isolator.   * Since this new component relies on service manager which will call agent HTTP APIs, we need to pass agent URL to it, like `process::http::URL(scheme, agentIP, agentPort, agentLibprocessId + """"/api/v1"""")`, see [here|https://github.com/apache/mesos/blob/1.10.0/src/slave/slave.cpp#L459:L471] for an example.   * When agent registers/reregisters with master (`Slave::registered` and `Slave::reregistered`), we should call this new component’s `start` method (see [here|https://github.com/apache/mesos/blob/1.10.0/src/slave/slave.cpp#L1740:L1742] and [here|https://github.com/apache/mesos/blob/1.10.0/src/slave/slave.cpp#L1825:L1827] as examples) which will scan the directory `--csi_plugin_config_dir` and create the `service manager - volume manager` pair for each CSI plugin loaded from that directory.   * For the `volume/csi` isolator, it needs to call this new component’s `publishVolume` and `unpublishVolume` methods in its `prepare` and `cleanup` method. summary: Split source files that require the largest amounts of memory for compilation.
description: "Mesos build is known to be memory-consuming and under-utilizing CPUs when built on hosts with small amount of memory.  What is less known is the fact that the peak RSS of invocations of compiler/linker seems to follow Paretolike distribution.    Top-20 clang++/clang invocations by max RSS in CMake RelWithDebInfo build of mesos-tests on Linux:      The presence of a few files of 3+ GiB is what makes clean build on standard AWS instances (and typical laptops) memory-limited if one tries to avoid compiler OOM.  "

summary: Mesos Agent gets stuck in Draining due to pending unacknowledged status updates
description: "A Mesos agent can get stuck in the Draining mode caused by pending unacknowledged status updates. When the framework becomes disconnected, the agent keeps sending task status updates for terminated tasks of that framework. This leads to a problem when the agent gets stuck in the Draining state because the master transitions the agent from DRAINING to DRAINED state only after all task status updates get acknowledged.    This problem can be resolved by sending [""Teardown"" operation|https://github.com/apache/mesos/blob/8ce5d30808f3744eeded09d530f226079d569a94/include/mesos/v1/master/master.proto#L299-L303] for all lost frameworks. However, it would be much better if this situation could be handled automatically by the Master. At least, we should make it easier for an operator to find out what prevents draining operation to complete."
text: Mesos Agent gets stuck in Draining due to pending unacknowledged status updates [SEP] """A Mesos agent can get stuck in the Draining mode caused by pending unacknowledged status updates. When the framework becomes disconnected, the agent keeps sending task status updates for terminated tasks of that framework. This leads to a problem when the agent gets stuck in the Draining state because the master transitions the agent from DRAINING to DRAINED state only after all task status updates get acknowledged.    This problem can be resolved by sending [""""Teardown"""" operation|https://github.com/apache/mesos/blob/8ce5d30808f3744eeded09d530f226079d569a94/include/mesos/v1/master/master.proto#L299-L303] for all lost frameworks. However, it would be much better if this situation could be handled automatically by the Master. At least, we should make it easier for an operator to find out what prevents draining operation to complete."""
label: non-design

summary: Refactor CSI volume manager to support pre-provisioned CSI volumes
description: "The existing [VolumeManager|https://github.com/apache/mesos/blob/1.10.0/src/csi/volume_manager.hpp#L55:L138] is like a wrapper for various CSI gRPC calls, we could consider leveraging it to call CSI plugins rather than making raw CSI gRPC calls in `volume/csi` isolator. But there is a problem, the lifecycle of the volumes managed by VolumeManager starts from the `[createVolume|https://github.com/apache/mesos/blob/1.10.0/src/csi/v1_volume_manager.cpp#L281:L329]` CSI call, but what we plan to support in MVP is pre-provisioned volumes, so we need to refactor VolumeManager by making it support pre-provisioned volumes."
text: Refactor CSI volume manager to support pre-provisioned CSI volumes [SEP] """The existing [VolumeManager|https://github.com/apache/mesos/blob/1.10.0/src/csi/volume_manager.hpp#L55:L138] is like a wrapper for various CSI gRPC calls, we could consider leveraging it to call CSI plugins rather than making raw CSI gRPC calls in `volume/csi` isolator. But there is a problem, the lifecycle of the volumes managed by VolumeManager starts from the `[createVolume|https://github.com/apache/mesos/blob/1.10.0/src/csi/v1_volume_manager.cpp#L281:L329]` CSI call, but what we plan to support in MVP is pre-provisioned volumes, so we need to refactor VolumeManager by making it support pre-provisioned volumes."""
label: design

summary: Drop autotools build (or leave it as a thin wrapper around CMake).
description: "Now that CMake build is usable for production (at least, in some configurations and with very specific package managers), the next step is to get rig of the need to maintain two separately configured build systems.    To get rid of the automake build (or turn it into a deprecated thin wrapper around the CMake build), we need to reach a feature parity between automake and cmake, at least for actively used features.    Deprecating automake will greatly simplify all the things that require dealing with build system: adding/removing/bundling/unbundling dependencies, changing source files layout, fixing build system quirks and so on.    *CMake build improvements that are not blocking autotools build deprecation are out of scope of this epic.*"

summary: Outstanding Offers accumulating
description: "We manage an Apache Mesos cluster version 1.7.0. We have written a framework in Java that schedules tasks to Mesos master at a rate of 300 TPS. Everything works fine for almost 24 hours but then outstanding offers accumulate & saturate within 15 minutes. Outstanding offers aren't reclaimed by Mesos master. We observe ""RescindOffer"" messages in verbose (GLOG v=3) framework logs but outstanding offers don't reduce. New resources aren't offered to framework when outstanding offers saturate. We have to restart the scheduler to reset outstanding offers to zero.    Any suggestions to debug this issue are welcome."
text: Outstanding Offers accumulating [SEP] """We manage an Apache Mesos cluster version 1.7.0. We have written a framework in Java that schedules tasks to Mesos master at a rate of 300 TPS. Everything works fine for almost 24 hours but then outstanding offers accumulate & saturate within 15 minutes. Outstanding offers aren't reclaimed by Mesos master. We observe """"RescindOffer"""" messages in verbose (GLOG v=3) framework logs but outstanding offers don't reduce. New resources aren't offered to framework when outstanding offers saturate. We have to restart the scheduler to reset outstanding offers to zero.    Any suggestions to debug this issue are welcome."""

summary: Docker Manifest list support
description: "Sonatype Nexus 3.22+, and probably other docker registry solutions, now serves manifest lists.    [https://issues.sonatype.org/browse/NEXUS-18546]    Apache Mesos does not support yet this part of the Image Manifest V2S2 spec.    https://docs.docker.com/registry/spec/manifest-v2-2/#manifest-list    This is not a critical issue as Sonatype Nexus is not a dependency of Apache Mesos, but as we cannot use Nexus > 3.21.2, this leads to side security issues.    [https://support.sonatype.com/hc/en-us/articles/360046233714]    Apache Mesos should support the whole Image Manifest V2S2 specification."

summary: Performance regression in HierarchicalAllocations_BENCHMARK_Test.PersistentVolumes
description: "Compared to 1.9.0, HierarchicalAllocations_BENCHMARK_Test.PersistentVolumes ran on 1.10.x branch/current master shows a regression (up to 50 times in some cases) and exhibits a quadratic dependence of allocation time versus the number of agents in the few-framework cases.    This regression was introduced by   https://reviews.apache.org/r/71460   which employed a single `Resources` object to track offered/allocated resources of all agents on the cluster  and exacerbated by https://reviews.apache.org/r/71488 that followed a similar approach.    In the case when resources of agents are non-addable (for example, each agent has an unique persistent volume), this results in O(N^2) asymptotic time complexity versus the number of agents due to the addition/subtraction of Resources being O(X*Y) where X and Y are number of individual `Resource`s on the left and on the right side.    Moreover, the approach taken in those PRs violates the convention that `Resources` belonging to different agents should not be added/subtracted."
text: Performance regression in HierarchicalAllocations_BENCHMARK_Test.PersistentVolumes  [SEP] """Compared to 1.9.0, HierarchicalAllocations_BENCHMARK_Test.PersistentVolumes ran on 1.10.x branch/current master shows a regression (up to 50 times in some cases) and exhibits a quadratic dependence of allocation time versus the number of agents in the few-framework cases.    This regression was introduced by   https://reviews.apache.org/r/71460   which employed a single `Resources` object to track offered/allocated resources of all agents on the cluster  and exacerbated by https://reviews.apache.org/r/71488 that followed a similar approach.    In the case when resources of agents are non-addable (for example, each agent has an unique persistent volume), this results in O(N^2) asymptotic time complexity versus the number of agents due to the addition/subtraction of Resources being O(X*Y) where X and Y are number of individual `Resource`s on the left and on the right side.    Moreover, the approach taken in those PRs violates the convention that `Resources` belonging to different agents should not be added/subtracted."""
label: non-design

summary: OpenSSLSocketImpl on Windows with 'support_downgrade' is incorrectly polling for read readiness.
description: "OpenSSLSocket is currently using the zero byte read trick on Windows to poll for read readiness when peaking at the data to determine whether the incoming connection is performing an SSL handshake. However, io::read is designed to provide consistent semantics for a zero byte read across posix and windows, which is to return immediately.    To fix this, we can either:    (1) Have different semantics for zero byte io::read on posix / windows, where we just let it fall through to the system calls. This might be confusing for users, but it's unlikely that a caller would perform a zero byte read in typical code so the confusion is probably avoided.    (2) Implement io::poll for reads on windows. This would make the caller code consistent and is probably less confusing to users."
text: OpenSSLSocketImpl on Windows with 'support_downgrade' is incorrectly polling for read readiness. [SEP] """OpenSSLSocket is currently using the zero byte read trick on Windows to poll for read readiness when peaking at the data to determine whether the incoming connection is performing an SSL handshake. However, io::read is designed to provide consistent semantics for a zero byte read across posix and windows, which is to return immediately.    To fix this, we can either:    (1) Have different semantics for zero byte io::read on posix / windows, where we just let it fall through to the system calls. This might be confusing for users, but it's unlikely that a caller would perform a zero byte read in typical code so the confusion is probably avoided.    (2) Implement io::poll for reads on windows. This would make the caller code consistent and is probably less confusing to users."""

summary: Windows overlapped IO discard handling can drop data.
description: "When getting a discard request for an io operation on windows, a cancellation is requested [1] and when the io operation completes we check whether the future had a discard request to decide whether to discard it [2]:        However, it's possible the operation completed successfully, in which case we did not succeed at canceling it. We need to check for {{ERROR_OPERATION_ABORTED}} [3]:        I don't think there are currently any major consequences to this issue, since most callers tend to be discarding only when they're essentially abandoning the entire process of reading or writing.    [1] https://github.com/apache/mesos/blob/1.9.0/3rdparty/libprocess/src/windows/libwinio.cpp#L448  [2] https://github.com/apache/mesos/blob/1.9.0/3rdparty/libprocess/src/windows/libwinio.cpp#L141-L151  [3] https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/win32/fileio/cancelioex-func"

We need to check for {{ERROR_OPERATION_ABORTED}} [3]:        I don't think there are currently any major consequences to this issue, since most callers tend to be discarding only when they're essentially abandoning the entire process of reading or writing. summary: cmake+MSBuild is uncapable of building all Mesos sources in parallel.
description: "When a library (in cmake's sense) contains several sources with different paths but the same filename (for example,  slave/validation.cpp and resource_provider/validation.cpp), the build generated by CMake for MSVC does not allow for building those files in parallel (presumably, because the .obj files will be located in the same directory).    This has been tested observed with both cmake 3.9 and 3.17, with ""Visual Studio 15 2017 Win64"" generator.     It seems to be a known behaviour - see https://stackoverflow.com/questions/7033855/msvc10-mp-builds-not-multicore-across-folders-in-a-project.    Two options for fixing this in a way that will work with these cmake/MSVC configurations are:   - splitting the build into small static libraries (a library per directory)   - introducing an intermediate code-generation-like step optionally flattening the directory structure (slave/validation.cpp -> slave_validation.cpp)    Both options have their drawbacks:   - The first will result in changing the layout the static build artifacts (mesos.lib will be replaced with a ton of smaller libraries), that will pose integration cahllenges,  and potentially will result in worse parallelism.   - The second will result in being unable to use #include without a path  (right now there are three or four such #include's in the whole Mesos/libprocess code buildable on Windows) in changed value of __FILE__ macro (as a consequence, in the example above, `validation.cpp` in logs will be replaced either with `slave_validation.cpp` or with `resource_provider_validation.cpp`)    Note that the second approach will need to deal with potential collisions when the source tree has filenames with underscores. If, for example, we had both slave/validation.cpp and slave_validation.cpp, then either some additional escaping will be needed or, alternatively, such layout could be just forbidden (and made to fail the build).    Preliminary testing shows that on a 8-core AWS instance flattening source trees of libprocess, mesos-protobufs and libmesos results in clean build speedup from ~1 hour to ~30 minutes."

Two options for fixing this in a way that will work with these cmake/MSVC configurations are:   - splitting the build into small static libraries (a library per directory)   - introducing an intermediate code-generation-like step optionally flattening the directory structure (slave/validation.cpp -> slave_validation.cpp)    Both options have their drawbacks:   - The first will result in changing the layout the static build artifacts (mesos.lib will be replaced with a ton of smaller libraries), that will pose integration cahllenges,  and potentially will result in worse parallelism.   - The second will result in being unable to use #include without a path  (right now there are three or four such #include's in the whole Mesos/libprocess code buildable on Windows) in changed value of __FILE__ macro (as a consequence, in the example above, `validation.cpp` in logs will be replaced either with `slave_validation.cpp` or with `resource_provider_validation.cpp`)    Note that the second approach will need to deal with potential collisions when the source tree has filenames with underscores. If, for example, we had both slave/validation.cpp and slave_validation.cpp, then either some additional escaping will be needed or, alternatively, such layout could be just forbidden (and made to fail the build).    Preliminary testing shows that on a 8-core AWS instance flattening source trees of libprocess, mesos-protobufs and libmesos results in clean build speedup from ~1 hour to ~30 minutes."""

summary: failure to destroy container can cause the agent to "leak" a GPU
description: "At work we hit the following problem:   # cgroup for a task using the GPU isolation failed to be destroyed on OOM   # the agent continued advertising the GPU as available   # all subsequent attempts to start tasks using a GPU fails with ""Requested 1 gpus but only 0 available""    Problem 1 looks like https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/MESOS-9950) so can be tackled separately, however the fact that the agent basically leaks the GPU is pretty bad, because it basically turns into /dev/null, failing all subsequent tasks requesting a GPU.         See the logs:                        I'll see if I can reproduce and debug the cgroup destruction problem and report my findings to https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/MESOS-9950), however I think that the bigger issue is the fact that the agent leaks the GPU.         I'm wondering which how the following makes sense:   # Consider the failure of destruction a container a fatal error: this turns this byzantine fault into a clean crash failure, which forces the operator to detect it and deal with it.   # Fix the leak by at least not advertising the GPU anymore - obviously the downside if that the operator might not see it and we lose a GPU.         [~<USER> [~<USER>          "

summary: Agent incorrectly handles draining when empty
description: "When the agent receives a {{DrainSlaveMessage}} and does not have any tasks or operations, it writes the {{DrainConfig}} to disk and is then implicitly stuck in a ""draining"" state indefinitely. For example, if an agent reregistration is triggered at such a time, the master may think the agent is operating normally and send a task to it, at which point the task will fail because the agent thinks it's draining (see this test for an example: https://reviews.apache.org/r/72364/).    If the agent receives a {{DrainSlaveMessage}} when it has no tasks or operations, it should avoid writing any {{DrainConfig}} to disk so that it immediately ""transitions"" into the already-drained state."

If the agent receives a {{DrainSlaveMessage}} when it has no tasks or operations, it should avoid writing any {{DrainConfig}} to disk so that it immediately """"transitions"""" into the already-drained state."""

summary: Update the `usage()` method of containerizer to set resource limits in the `ResourceStatistics` protobuf message
description: "In the `ResourceStatistics` protobuf message, there are a couple of issues:   # There are already `cpu_limit` and `mem_limit_bytes` fields, but they are actually CPU & memory requests when resources limits are specified for a task.   # There is already `mem_soft_limit_bytes` field, but this field seems not set anywhere. summary: OpenSSLSocketImpl with 'support_downgrade' can silently stop accepting sockets.
description: "Related to MESOS-10113, the accept code for OpenSSLSocketImpl will return a failed future from the accept loop body if the initial io::read/io::poll fails when checking for downgrade.    This would cause the accept loop to silently stop, and incoming connections would no longer be accepted."

This would cause the accept loop to silently stop, and incoming connections would no longer be accepted."""
label: non-design

summary: OpenSSLSocketImpl with 'support_downgrade' waits for incoming bytes before accepting new connection.
description: "The accept loop in OpenSSLSocketImpl in the case of {{support_downgrade}} enabled will wait for incoming bytes on the accepted socket before allowing another socket to be accepted. This will lead to significant throughput issues for accepting new connections (e.g. during a master failover), or may block entirely if a client doesn't send any data for whatever reason.    Marking as a bug due to the potential for blocking incoming connections."

summary: Log peer address during TLS handshake failures.
description: "Currently, when accepting a socket from a LibeventSSLSocket or OpenSSLSocket, the caller cannot log the peer address in the case of a failure. We currently do not include the peer address in the accept failure which makes debugging more difficult (e.g. who is trying to connect with a bad SSL handshake? who is disconnecting in the middle of an SSL handshake?).    This should be backported."
